translations.source: Latent syphilis is asymptomatic and characterized by positive syphilis serology. Trichomoniasis: The predominant symptoms include abnormal vaginal discharge with redness of the vulva, itching and painful intercourse. Genital herpes simplex virus (HSV) HSV most commonly presents as painful sores, vesicles or ulcerations on the external genitalia and mouth. Symptomatic genital HSV is a lifelong condition that can be characterized by frequent symptomatic recurrences. Human T-lymphotropic virus type 1 (HTLV-1) Generally asymptomatic, the chronic form of HTLV-1 can cause severe disease, including adult T-cell leukaemia/lymphoma (ATL) and a progressive nervous system condition known as HTLV-1-associated myelopathy or tropical spastic paraparesis (HAM/TSP). Treatment: Effective treatment is currently available for several STIs. Three bacterial STIs (chlamydia, gonorrhoea and syphilis) and one parasitic STI (trichomoniasis) are usually curable with existing, effective single-dose or multiple-dose regimens of antibiotics. For viral STIs (HIV, HSV and HTLV-1), the most effective medications available are antivirals or anti-cancer drugs (in the case of HTLV-1), that can modulate the course of the diseases, though they cannot cure these three diseases. Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) to antibiotics used to treat STIs, in particular gonorrhoea, has increased rapidly in recent years and has reduced successful treatment outcomes. Results from the current Gonococcal AMR Surveillance Programme (GASP) show trends of high rates of quinolone resistance, increasing azithromycin resistance and emerging resistance of extended-spectrum cephalosporins. The emergence of decreased susceptibility of gonorrhoea to extended-spectrum cephalosporins, together with established high-levels of resistance to penicillins, sulphonamides, tetracyclines, quinolones and macrolides make gonorrhoea a multidrug-resistant organism. AMR for other STIs, though less common, also exists, making prevention and prompt treatment critical. To adequately treat STIs it is important to take the appropriate antimicrobials, with the correct dose and duration for the specific STI to ensure adequate treatment or cure, and to prevent the development of antimicrobial resistance.
translations.reference: Sifilis a matakin ƙarshe kuma ba ya nuna ko bayyana wasu alamu amma ya na nuni da cewa sifilis ne ta hanyan gwajin jini. Cutar Trichomoniasis: babban alamomin wannan cuta sun haɗa da zuban ruwan da babu dalili tare da jan kan fatar al'aura, ƙaiƙayi da kuma jin zafi yayin saduwa. Ƙurajen cikin matantaka (Genital Herpes Simplex Virus, HSV) Mafi yawanci wannan cuta na bayyana ne a ƙuraje masu matuƙar zafi da ciwo, masu ruwa, ko kuma mai kamar gyanbo a kan fatar al'aura da kuma cikin baki. Alamomin wannan cuta na tsawon rayuwa ne, wanda ke faruwa akai akai(idan wasu sun mutu wasu su ƙara fitowa). Cutar sankarar jini (HTLV-1) Gabaɗaya ba shi da wasu alamu, matsanancin matakin cutar kan iya jawo matsanancin ciwon wanda ya haɗa da sankarar jini ga manya (ATL) da cigaban matsalar ƙwaƙwalwa. Magani: yanzu haka akwai ingantattun magunguna da su ke nan don magance waɗannan cututtuka dayawa. Ƙwayoyin bacteria guda uku na STIs( Chlamydia, gonoriya da kuma sifilis) da kuma ƙwayar cutar Trichomoniasis, yawanci akan samu waraka ta hanyar amfani da ingantattun ƙwayoyin magunguna na antibiotics wanda ake bayarwa sau ɗaya ko fiye da ɗaya. Game da ƙwayoyin cutar Virus( HIV, HSV, da HTLV-1) maganungunan da su ka fi tasiri a wadace su ne antiviral ko kuma magungunan da su ke daƙusar da cutar daji wato anti-cancer ( idan aka duba abinda ya shafi HTLV-1) da zai iya canza tsarin cututtukan, duk da basa warkar da waɗannan cututtuka uku. Ana amfani da magungunan da su ke jure antibiotics wajen warkar da STIs, musamman gonoriya, da ya ƙaru a ƴan shekarun nan an samu nasarar raguwarsa sosai ta dalilin magance shi. A sakamakon wani bincike da aka yi na sa ido akan cutar gonoriya wato Gonococcal AMR Surveillance Programme a turance (GASP) ya nuna jerin gwanon juriya ga ƙwayar quinone, ƙarin juran azithromycin, da kuma bayyanar juran ƙarin rukunonin cephalosporins. Bayyanar raguwar sauƙin kamuwa gonoriya ga ƙarin rukunonin cephalosporins tare da gina tsantsan juriya ga penicillin, sulphonamides, ja da yalo, quinolones, da kuma macrolides ya sanya ƙwayoyin gonoriya jure magunguna dayawa. Juriyar magunguna ga sauran STIs, duk da bai cika yawa ba, amma kuma yana faruwa, wanda ya ke sanya kariya da waraka da ya kamata cikin halin ƙaƙanikayi. Don samun cikekken magance STIs, yana da matuƙar amfani a samu ƙwayoyin da su ka dace , tare da samun adadin da ya kamata a ɗauka da lokaci , musamman na STIs don tabbatar da samun cikekken magancewa da warkarwa da kuma bada kariya ga samuwar cigaba da jure ƙwayoyin magani.

Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices and potential areas for refinement.

**Hausa Translation:**

Cutarda mai ɓoye bai nuna alamun cuta ba, kuma ana ƙirƙirar shi ne ta hanyar binciken jinin da ke nuna waccin cutar syphilis. Cutar Trichomoniasis: Alamun farko sun haɗa da fitar da ruwan yini mai banƙi da launin rashi a makogwaro, zubar da jini da kuma zubar da jini a lokacin yin jima'i. Cutar Herpes simplex ta mahaɗa (HSV) Kusan dukkanin alamun HSV sun bayyana ne ta hanyar raunuka, ƙuraƙura ko ƙuraƙura a kan mahaɗa da bakin mutum. Cutar HSV da ke nuna alamun cuta tana zama a jiki bisa ƙarancin shekaru, kuma za ta sake bayyana ta hanyar alamun cuta. Kwayar cutar T-lymphotropic ta mutane mai nau'in 1 (HTLV-1) Yawanci ba ta nuna alamun cuta, amma cutar HTLV-1 na iya haifar da cuta mai tsanani, gami da cutar T-cell leukemia/lymphoma (ATL) da kuma cutar tsarin haɓakar jiki mai tsanani da ake kira HTLV-1-associated myelopathy ko tropical spastic paraparesis (HAM/TSP). Magani: Akwai magani mai tasiri don yawan cutan da ke yaduwa ta jima'i. Cutan da ke yaduwa ta jima'i guda uku na kwayar cutar da ke haifar da cuta (chlamydia, gonorrhoea da syphilis) da kuma cuta daya na parasitic (trichomoniasis) galibi za su warƙewa ta amsar kwayar cuta mai ƙarfi ko kwayar cuta mai yawa. Ga cutan da ke yaduwa ta jima'i ta kwayar cuta (HIV, HSV da HTLV-1), mafi kyawun magani shine antiviral ko maganin kwayar cuta (a yanayin HTLV-1), waɗanda za su canza hanyar ci gaban cutar, duk da cewa ba za su warƙewa waɗannan cuta uku ba. Matsalar jinin kwayar cuta (AMR) ga kwayar cuta da ake amfani da su don maganin cutan da ke yaduwa ta jima'i, musamman gonorrhoea, ta ƙaru sosai a cikin shekarun da suka gabata kuma ta rage sakamakon maganin da ya yi nasara. Sakamakon aikin binciken AMR na Gonococcal (GASP) na yanzu yana nuni da ƙarancin jinin quinolone, ƙaruwar jinin azithromycin da kuma bayyanar jinin cephalosporin mai faɗi. Bayyanar rauni ga gonorrhoea ga cephalosporin mai faɗi, da kuma ƙarancin jinin penicillin, sulphonamides, tetracyclines, quinolones da macrolides, ya sa gonorrhoea ta zama cuta mai jinin kwayar cuta da yawa. AMR don sauran cutan da ke yaduwa ta jima'i, duk da cewa ba ta da yawa, har yanzu ta kasance, don haka hana cuta da magani da sauri yana da mahimmanci. Don maganin cutan da ke yaduwa ta jima'i daidai, yana da mahimmanci a ɗauki kwayar cuta ta gaba ɗaya, da adadin da ya dace da tsawon lokaci don cutar da ke yaduwa ta jima'i don tabbatar da magani ko warƙewa, da kuma hana ci gaban matsalar jinin kwayar cuta.**

**Notes on the Translation and Potential Refinements:**

*   **Technical Terminology:** I've tried to use the most common and understandable Hausa terms for medical terminology. However, some terms might require further clarification or adaptation depending on the specific audience.  For example, "antimicrobial resistance" (AMR) is a relatively new concept, and the Hausa term might need further explanation in a broader context.
*   **Sentence Structure:** I've adjusted sentence structures to flow more naturally in Hausa.  Direct, word-for-word translations can often sound awkward.
*   **Cultural Context:**  Consider the cultural context of the target audience.  Some phrases or explanations might need to be adapted to be more culturally sensitive.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  **Crucially, this translation *needs* to be reviewed by a native Hausa speaker with medical knowledge.**  I am an AI and cannot guarantee 100% accuracy, especially with specialized terminology.  A human review is essential for ensuring the translation is both accurate and understandable.
*   **Specific Terms to Check:**
    *   "Vesicles" - The Hausa term used might need to be verified for accuracy.
    *   "Myelopathy" - This is a complex term and the Hausa equivalent should be carefully checked.
    *   "Extended-spectrum cephalosporins" - Ensure the Hausa term is precise and understandable.
*   **Abbreviations:** Consider whether to spell out abbreviations (like HSV, HTLV-1) the first time they are used, or if the Hausa audience is familiar with them.

translations.source: Smallpox Overview: Smallpox is an acute contagious disease caused by the variola virus, a member of the orthopoxvirus family. It was one of the most devastating diseases known to humanity and caused millions of deaths before it was eradicated. It is believed to have existed for at least 3000 years. The smallpox vaccine, created by Edward Jenner in 1796, was the first successful vaccine to be developed. He observed that milkmaids who previously had caught cowpox did not catch smallpox and showed that a similar inoculation could be used to prevent smallpox in other people. The World Health Organization launched an intensified plan to eradicate smallpox in 1967. Widespread immunization and surveillance were conducted around the world for several years. The last known natural case was in Somalia in 1977. In 1980 WHO declared smallpox eradicated – the only infectious disease to achieve this distinction. This remains among the most notable and profound public health successes in history. Symptoms: Early symptoms of smallpox include high fever, fatigue and severe back pain, and less often, abdominal pain and vomiting. Two to 3 days later the virus produces a characteristic rash with bumps full of a clear liquid, which later fill with pus and finally develop a crust that dries and falls off. The rash begins on the face and hands, then spreads to the rest of the body. Lesions develop in the mucous membranes of the nose and mouth and ulcerate soon after formation. Smallpox is transmitted from person to person via infective droplets during close contact with infected people who have symptoms of the disease, or in some cases through contaminated clothing and bedding. It has an incubation period of 7–17 days after exposure and only becomes infectious once a fever develops. People remain infectious until the last scabs fall off. Smallpox was fatal in up to 30% of cases.WHO smallpox recognition card WHO response: The period since eradication has been defined by a lengthy and complex debate focussed on the destruction of the last remaining stocks of live variola virus. In 1996, at the 49th World Health Assembly, Member States decided to have this stock destroyed in June 1999, and only to allow small samples to be kept for research purposes at two designated secure laboratories – one in the United States and one in the Russian Federation. Temporary retention of the existing stocks of variola virus are used for further essential research, which is overseen by the Advisory Committee for Variola Virus Research. This group meets on an annual basis. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat based in WHO Headquarters coordinates all smallpox eradication-related and post-eradication activities. These include overseeing permitted research with live variola virus for the development of countermeasures such as vaccine and antivirals.
translations.reference: Cutar Agana (Smallpox) Bayani: Agana cuta ce mai saurin yaɗuwa ta hanyar ƙwayar cuta ta variola, ɗaya daga cikin dangin cututtukan orthopoxvirus. Tana ɗaya daga cikin cututtuka mafi muni da dan'adam ya taɓa fama da su kuma ta yi sanadiyyar mutuwar miliyoyin mutane kafin a samu nasarar kawar da ita. Ana ganin cutar ta ci zamani a duniyar nan na kusan shekara 3000. Allurar riga-kafin agana, wadda Edward Jenner ya ƙirƙira a shekara ta 1796, ita ce riga-kafin farko da aka samu nasara. Ya nuna cewa masu sana’ar tatsar nonon da a da suka kamu da cutar aganar shanu ba sa kamuwa da cutar agana (smallpox) kuma ya nuna cewa za a iya yin amfani da irin wannan rigakafin cutar a kan wasu mutane. Ƙungiyar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) ta ƙaddamar da wani gagarumin shiri na kawar da cutar agana a shekara ta 1967. An gudanar riga-kafi sosai tare da sa ido a duk duniya tsawon shekaru da dama. Mutum na ƙarshe da aka sani ya kamu da cutar haka kawai a Somalia ne a 1977. A shekarar 1980, WHO ta ayyana ceyan kawar da ƙwayar cutar - watau ita ce kawai cuta mai yaɗuwa da aka samu wannan nasara a kanta. Wannan ya kasance daga cikin fitattun nasarorin da aka samu a fannin kiwon lafiyar jama'a a tarihi. Alamomi: Alamomin farko na cutar agana sun haɗa da zazzabi mai zafi, gajiya da ciwon baya mai tsanani, sai kuna akan samu ciwon ciki da amai. Bayan kwana biyu zuwa 3 ƙwayar cutar tana haifar da wani nau'i na kurji mai cike da ruwa, wanda daga baya yake cika da ɗiwa sannan a ƙarshe ya fitar da ɓawo mai bushewa, sannan sai ya faɗi. Ƙurajen suna farawa ne ta fuska da hannaye, sannan daga baya su bazu zuwa sauran sassan jiki. Za a fara samun raunuka a cikin baki da hanci da sauran wurare, sannan sai su fara raɗaɗi. Ana kamuwa da cutar agana (Smallpox) daga mutum zuwa mutum ta hanyar ɗigo-ɗigon cutar a lokacin kusanci da masu ɗauke da alamomin cutar, ko kuma a wasu lokuta ta hanyar tufafi da wajen kwanciyar mai cutar. Bayan kamuwa da cutar, alamominta suna fara bayyana ne tsakanin kwanaki 7- zuwa 17. Bayan bayyanarsa kuma, da zarar zarar an ga zazzabi, shi ke nan abu ya tabbata. Cutar za ta zauna a jikin mutum har sai ɓawo na ƙarshe ya faɗi. Cutar agana ta kashe mutane kusan kaso 30 na masu ɗauke da ita. Katin gane cutar ta WHO Matakin WHO: Tun bayan kawar da cutar agana, an ta samun muhawarorin da suka mayar da hankali kan yadda za a kawar da ɓirɓishin ƙwayoyin cutar variola. A cikin 1996, a Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta 49, ƙasashe mambobi sun yanke shawarar lalata wannan cuta a watan Yunin 1999, sai dai kawai za a ba da damar adana samfurinta don dalilai na bincike a ɗakunan gwaje-gwaje guda biyu da aka keɓe - ɗaya a ciki a Amurka da wani kuma a Rasha. Ana amfani da waɗannan samfuran ƙwayoyin cuta na variola da aka ajiye na ɗan lokaci ne don ƙarin bincike mai muhimmanci, wanda Kwamitin Ba da Shawarwari don Binciken Cutar Variola ke kulawa. Wannan rukunin kwamitin suna haɗuwa ne duk shekara. Sakatariyar cutar agana ta WHO da ke hedikwatar WHO tana tsara duk ayyukan da suka shafi kawar da cutar da kuma abubuwan da kan zo bayan kawar da cutar. Waɗannan sun haɗa da sa ido kan binciken da aka ba da izinin yi tare da ƙwayar cutar variola mai rai don haɓaka matakan riga-kafin kamar sinadaran hana yaɗuwar ƙwayoyin cuta.

translations.source: Since the eradication era, safer vaccines and specific treatments have been developed for smallpox and related diseases such as monkeypox. The Secretariat is also responsible for emergency preparedness and manages a smallpox vaccine emergency stockpile in the unlikely event that the disease re-emerges. The Secretariat manages the biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the authorized variola virus repositories as mandated by the World Health Assembly, and ensures annual reporting to WHO Governing Bodies. The Smallpox Secretariat supports WHO Archives in preserving the global history of smallpox and documenting the lessons learned from successful eradication of smallpox. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat's current roles and responsibilities include: Variola virus research and development of countermeasures organizing annual meetings of the WHO Advisory Committee on Variola Virus Research (ACVVR) coordinating review of research proposals for work with variola virus through the ACVVR managing relationships with the two variola virus repositories (which are also WHO Collaborating Centres) recording, processing and monitoring requests from researchers for variola virus DNA from the 2 repositories under specific rules conducting biennial biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the 2 global variola virus repositories developing normative guidance regarding research on variola virus DNA and related matters Emergency preparedness and response coordinating and preparing normative guidance on development and use of medical countermeasures for orthopoxviruses developing preparedness and response strategies, and tools to implement preparedness measures maintaining and managing the smallpox global vaccine stockpile coordinating development of a laboratory network for orthopoxvirus detection and diagnostics developing normative guidance and response strategies for human monkeypox and other orthopoxviruses supporting capacity-building and developing training materials contributing to global health security initiatives Preserving global smallpox history overseeing the Smallpox Eradication Programme archives in collaboration with WHO Archives and the WHO Library leading on policy discussions and commemorative events regarding smallpox eradication reporting annually to the WHO Executive Board and the World Health Assembly The Secretariat also mobilizes and manages funding for these activities and for ad hoc events as required such as technical consultations and training.
translations.reference: Tun lokacin da aka kawar da ita wannan cuta, an samar da rigakafi da takamaimun magunguna don cutar agana da cututtukan da ke da alaƙa, kamar cutar ƙyandar biri. Sakatariyar kuma ita ce ke da alhakin shirye-shiryen gaggawa da sarrafa lamurran gaggawa na riga-kafin cutar agana a lokacin da ta ɓulla, wanda ba a yi tsammanina cutar za ta sake ɓulla ba. Sakatariyar tana kula da binciken lafiyar halittu da nazarin halittu na ma'ajiyar ƙwayoyin cuta ta variola da aka ba da izinin yin hakan, kamar yadda Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta umarta; kuma tana tabbatar da an ba da rahoton shekara-shekara ga hukumomin gudanarwa na WHO. Sakatariyar Cutar Agana taimaka wa WHO wajen adana tarihin cutar a duniya da kuma rubuta darussan da aka koya daga nasarar kawar da cutar ta agana. Ayyukan Sakatariyar WHO a yanzu sun haɗa da: Binciken ƙwayar cuta na Variola da inganta matakan kariya shirya tarurrukan shekara-shekara na Kwamitin Ba da Shawarar WHO kan Binciken Cutar Variola (ACVVR a taƙaice) jagorantar bitar shawarwarin bincike don aiki kam cutar variola ƙarƙashin ACVVR tafiyar da dangantaka da ma'ajiyar cutar ta variola guda biyu (waɗanda kuma cibiyoyin haɗin gwiwar WHO ne) ɗaukar bayanai, sarrafawa da kuma sa idanu kan buƙatun da akw turowa daga masu bincike don ƙwayar cutar variola ta DNA daga ɗakunan ajiya guda 2 a ƙarƙashin wasu ƙa'idoji. gudanar da bincike da nazari cikin duk shekara biyu kan tsaro da lafiyar halittu na ma'ajiyar cutar variola guda 2 na duniya kawo cigaba a wajen yin bincike kan ƙwayar cuta ta variola (DNA) da batutuwa masu alaƙa Shirye-shirye don lamuran gaggawa da ɗaukar mataki tattara duk abin da ya dace tare da shirya yadda za a jagoranci haɓakawa da amfani da matakan jinya don ƙwayoyin cuta na orthopox. tafiyar da shirye-shirye da dabarun ɗaukar matakan gaggawa, da kayan aiki don aiwatar da duk wani shirin aikin gaggawa kulawa tare da sarrafa kayayyakin riga-kafin cutar agana na duniya sarrafa hanyoyin tafiyar da cibiyar ɗakunan gwaje-gwaje don binciken gano ƙwayoyin cutar othopoxvirus Samar da yadda za a bi da cutar ƙyandar biri ta ɗan'adam da sauran ƙwayoyin cuta na orthopox da kuma yadda za a tunkare su. taimakawa wajen tabbatar da ƙwarewar ma'aikata da kuma inganta kayan horo ba da gudunmawa wa shirye-shirye kiwon lafiya ma duniya adana tarihin cutar agana a duniya kula da Taskokin Shirin Kawar da Cutar Agana tare da haɗin gwiwar WHO Archives da WHO Library jagoranci kan tattaunawar inda aka sa gaba da abubuwan tunawa da kawar da cutar agana da aka yi bayar da rahoto kowace shekara ga Hukumar Zartarwa ta WHO da Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya Sakatariyar tana kuma tattarawa da sarrafa kuɗaɗe don waɗannan ayyukan da kuma shirye-shirye da aka yi na wucin-gadi kamar yadda ake buƙata, kamar shawarwarin fasaha da ba da horo.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English document into Hausa, aiming for clarity and accuracy. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices and potential areas for refinement.

**Hausa Translation:**

Tun lokacin da aka shaida yaki da cutar Smallpox, an haɓaka allunan da suka fi amanci da kuma magani na musamman don cutar Smallpox da kuma cutan da ke danganta da ita, irin su Monkeypox.  Hukuncin (Secretariat) kuma yana da alhakin shirye-shiryen jin kai da kuma kula da adadin allan Smallpox a matsayin matsala a halin da cutar ta sake bayyana. Hukuncin yana da alhakin bincika tsaro (biosafety) da kuma kiyaye tsaro (biosecurity) na wuraren ajiyar cutar Smallpox (variola virus) da aka ba da izinin a bisa ga umurnin Majalisar Kula da Lafiya ta Duniya (World Health Assembly), kuma yana tabbatar da rahoton shekara-shekara zuwa Jiga-Jigan Kungiyar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO Governing Bodies).  Hukuncin Smallpox Secretariat yana ba da tallafi ga WHO Archives don kiyaye tarihi mai ma'ana na duniya game da cutar Smallpox da kuma rubuta duk abubuwan da aka koya daga yaki da nasarar yaki da cutar Smallpox.  Ayukace-ayukacen Hukuncin Smallpox Secretariat a yanzu sun haɗa da:

*   Bincike da haɓaka magani don cutar Variola Virus da kuma hanyoyin hana cuta.
*   Tashirya taro shekara-shekara na WHO Advisory Committee on Variola Virus Research (ACVVR).
*   Tsara bincika shawarwari na bincike don aikin da ke da alaka da cutar Variola Virus ta hanyar ACVVR.
*   Kula da dangantaka da wuraren ajiyar cutar Variola Virus guda biyu (waɗanda su ma su ne WHO Collaborating Centres).
*   Kira, sarrafa da kuma lura da buƙatun bincikayya don DNA na cutar Variola Virus daga wuraren ajiyar guda biyu bisa ƙa'idoji na musamman.
*   Zanta bincike tsaro (biosafety) da kiyaye tsaro (biosecurity) na wuraren ajiyar cutar Variola Virus guda biyu a duniya kowane shekarar biyu.
*   Haɓaka shawarwari kan bincike akan DNA na cutar Variola Virus da sauran abubuwan da ke da alaka.
*   Shiryewa da amsa ga cutobi.
*   Tsara da shirya shawarwari akan haɓaka da amsar magani don cutan Orthopoxviruses.
*   Haɓaka tsare-tsare da kayan aiki don aiwatar da shirye-shiryen jin kai.
*   Kula da adadin allan Smallpox a duniya.
*   Tashirya haɓaka hanyoyin bincike da gano cutar Orthopoxvirus.
*   Haɓaka shawarwari da tsare-tsare don amsa ga cutar Monkeypox da sauran cutan Orthopoxviruses.
*   Bada goyan baya ga ƙirƙirar ƙarfi da haɓaka kayan koyarwa.
*   Bada goyan baya ga ƙwararrun ƙungiyoyi don lafiya a duniya.
*   Kiyaye tarihi mai ma'ana na duniya game da cutar Smallpox.
*   Karkatar da bayanai na yaki da cutar Smallpox a shirye da WHO Archives da WHO Library.
*   Zama a gaba a tattaunawa kan manufofin da suka shafi yaki da cutar Smallpox da kuma bikin tunawa da nasarar yaki da cutar.
*   Rapota shekara-shekara zuwa WHO Executive Board da World Health Assembly.

Hukuncin kuma yana ƙirƙirar kudin da ake buƙata don waɗannan ayyuka da kuma bikin tunawa da abubuwan da suka faru bisa buƙata, irin su taro na bincike da horo.

---

**Notes on the Translation & Potential Refinements:**

*   **Technical Terminology:** I've tried to use the most appropriate Hausa terms for technical words like "biosafety," "biosecurity," "variola virus," "orthopoxviruses," "countermeasures," etc.  However, some of these might need further refinement by a subject matter expert in Hausa.  I've used transliterations where a direct Hausa equivalent wasn't readily available, but this can be improved.
*   **Clarity and Flow:** I've aimed for clear and understandable Hausa, but the sentence structure might need some adjustments to flow more naturally in Hausa.  Long sentences from the original English were broken down where necessary.
*   **"Secretariat":** I've translated "Secretariat" as "Hukunci," which generally means "authority" or "body." This is a common way to refer to such organizations in Hausa.
*   **"Emergency Stockpile":** Translated as "adadin allan Smallpox a matsayin matsala" (Smallpox vaccine stockpile as a problem). This conveys the idea of a reserve for emergencies.
*   **WHO Acronyms:** I've kept the WHO acronym as "WHO" as it is commonly understood.  ACVVR is also kept as is.
*   **Cultural Context:**  The language used is formal, appropriate for a document of this nature.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  **Crucially, this translation *needs* to be reviewed by a native Hausa speaker with expertise in medical/scientific terminology.**  There may be nuances I've missed, and some terms might be better expressed differently.

**To help me improve the translation further, could you tell me:**

translations.source: Snakebite envenoming Overview: Snakebite envenoming is a potentially life-threatening disease caused by toxins in the bite of a venomous snake. Envenoming can also be caused by having venom sprayed into the eyes by certain species of snakes that have the ability to spit venom as a defence measure. Inadequate past efforts to control snakebite envenoming has produced fragmented, inaccurate epidemiological data. Many victims do not attend health centres or hospitals and instead rely on traditional treatments. However, available data show 4.5–5.4 million people get bitten by snakes annually. Of this, 1.8–2.7 million develop clinical illness and 81 000 to 138 000 die from complications. High-risk groups include rural agricultural workers, herders, fishermen, hunters, working children, people living in poorly constructed houses and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Morbidity and mortality occur most frequently among young people and children suffer higher case fatality. Furthermore, women experience increased barriers to accessing medical care in some cultures and pregnant women are extremely vulnerable. An ongoing crisis restricting access to safe, effective antivenom treatment in many regions, and particularly sub-Saharan Africa, is one factor that contributes to the predisposition for seeking help through traditional medicine. Symptoms: Bites or sprayed venom from venomous snakes can cause a range of acute and serious medical emergencies. Envenoming from different types of snakes can cause different symptoms, some more serious than others. This makes the preparation of correct antivenoms an ongoing problem. Envenoming can cause severe paralysis that may prevent breathing, making immediate medical attention critical. People may also experience bleeding disorders that can lead to fatal haemorrhages or irreversible kidney failure. Severe local tissue destruction may also occur, which can lead to permanent disability and even limb amputation. Children are at higher risk of severe effects due to lower body mass than adults. Most deaths and serious consequences from snake bites are entirely preventable by making safe and effective antivenoms more widely available and accessible, particularly in high-risk areas. High quality snake antivenoms are the only effective treatment to prevent or reverse most of the venomous effects of snake bites. Treatment: Snake antivenoms are effective treatments to prevent or reverse most of the harmful effects of snakebite envenoming and are included in the WHO list of essential medicines. The availability and accessibility of these antivenoms, along with raising awareness on primary prevention methods among communities and health workers, are the best ways to limit serious consequences and deaths from snakebite envenoming. After a bite by a snake suspected of being venomous, follow these steps: Immediately move away from the area where the bite occurred. Remove anything tight from around the bitten part of the body to avoid harm if swelling occurs.
translations.reference: Dafin cizon maciji Bayani: Dafin cizon maciji cuta ce mai yiwuwar cutar da rayuwa wadda ke haifar da guba idan mutum ya gamu da cikin cizon maciji. Haka kuma cutar dafin cizon tana iya samuwa ta hanyar fesa yawu da wani nau’in macizai ke yi a cikin idanuwa a matsayin matakin kare kansu. Rashin ƙoƙarin da aka yi a baya don shawo kan cutar dafin maciji ya haifar da rashin cikakku da ingantattun bayanan yaɗuwar cututtuka. Yawancin waɗanda suka taɓa samun cizon maciji ba sa zuwa cibiyoyin lafiya ko asibitoci; maimakon haka sukan dogara da magungunan gargajiya. Duk da cewa bayanan da aka tattara sun nuna cewa maciji yana sarar mutane miliyan 4.5 zuwa 5.4 a duk shekara. Daga cikin waɗannan, miliyan 1.8 zuwa 2.7 suna samun rashin lafiya kuma 81 000 zuwa 138 000 suna mutuwa daga saboda wannan cizo. Mutanen da suka fi hatsarin fuskantar wannan sun haɗa da masu aikin gona na karkara, makiyaya, masunta, mafarauta, yara masu aiki, mutanen da ke zaune a gidajen da ba a gina su da kyau ba, da kuma waɗanda ke da ƙarancin damar samun ilimi da kiwon lafiya. Kamuwa da cutar da mace-mace na faruwa a-kai-a-kai tsakanin matasa sannan yara kanana sun fi fama da yawan mace-mace. Bugu da ƙari, mata ba sa samun kulawar asibiti a wasu al'adu kuma mata masu juna biyu sun fi zama masu rauni a wannan fannin. Rikicin da ke ci gaba da hana samun kulawar lafiya, ingantaccen maganin riga-kafi a yankuna da yawa, musamman ma yankin kudu da hamadar Sahara, wani abu ne da ke ba da gudummawa wajen yiwuwar neman taimako ta hanyar maganin gargajiya. Alamominta: Cizo ko fesa dafin da macizai masu dafi ke yi na iya haifar da mummunan yanayin gaggawa na rashin lafiya. Saka dafin nau'ikan macizai daban-daban na iya haifar da alamomi daban-daban, sai dai wasu sun fi wasu tsanani. Wannan ya sa shirye-shiryen neman ainihin maganin riga-kafi ya zama cike da matsaloli a halin yanzu. Dafin cizon maciji na iya haifar da shanyewar jiki mai tsanani wanda zai iya hana numfashi, kuma yana haifar da matsananciyar kulawar likita cikin gaggawa. Sannan kuma mutane na iya fuskantar matsalar zubar jini mai kisa ko matsalar ƙoda da ba za a iya maganinta ba. Har ila yau, za a iya samun mummunar lalacewar tsoka, wanda zai haifar da nakasu na dindindin har ma da yanke gaɓoɓin jiki. Yara sum fi samun kansu cikin wannan yanayi mai tsanani saboda rashin nauyin jiki. Yawancin mace-mace da munanan lamurran saran maciji ana iya yin riga-kafinsu gaba ɗaya ta hanyar samar da amintattun magungunan kashe ƙwayoyin cuta da kuma samun dama, musamman a wuraren da ke da haɗarin macizai. Magani masu inganci na macizai su ne kawai ingantaccen magani don hanawa ko karkatar da mafi yawan illolin cizon maciji. Yin magani: Magungunan dafin maciji sun kasance suna da inganci wajen hanawa ko sauƙaƙa mafi yawan illolin cutar saran maciji kuma suna cikin jerin mahimman magunguna na WHO. Samuwar waɗannan magungunan kashe dafin, tare da wayar da kan jama'a kan hanyoyin riga-kafin farko a tsakanin al'ummomi da ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya, su ne mafi kyawun hanyoyin da za a iya maganin mace-mace saboda cizon maciji. Bayan saran maciji da ake zargin yana da dafi, to a bi waɗannan matakan: Nan da nan mutum ya tashi daga wajen da aka yi masa cizon. A cire duk wani abu da zai iya tamke kewayen sashen jikin da aka ciz don gujewa cutarwa idan aka samu kumburi.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the target audience. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices and potential areas for refinement.

**Snakebite Envenoming (Ci gaban Makarkashi):**

Ci gaban makarkashi na iya zama cuta mai hatsari ga rayuwa, wacce ta samo asali ne daga guba da ke cikin sheƙar kwayar makarkashi. Ci gaban guba na iya faruwa maƙi yadda guba ta kunna ido ta amsar wasu makarkashiya da ke da ikon kunna guba don kare kansu. Kokarin kawar da ci gaban guba a baya ya haɗa da bayanin alkalanci mara daidaito. Mutane da yawa ba sa zuwa asibiti ko cibiyoyin lafiya, kuma suna amsar maganin gargajiya. Duk da haka, bayanai da ke nan sun nuna cewa mutane 4.5 – 5.4 miliyan ke samun rauni daga makarkashi kowace shekara. Daga wannan adadi, mutane 1.8 – 2.7 miliyan ke ciwon cuta, kuma mutane 81,000 zuwa 138,000 ke mutuwa sakamakon cuta. Wakilan haddi-haddi sun haɗa da ma'aikatan karkoki, manoma, mazauna tafki, masu bincike, yara, mutanen da ke zaune a gida mara inganci, da kuma waɗanda ke da iyawar ilimi da kula da lafiya. Cutewa da mutuwar suna faruwa mafi yawa a tsakanin matasa, kuma yara suna fuskantar kashewar yawa. Hakanan, mata suna fuskantar wani matsala a kan samun kula da lafiya a wasu al'adu, kuma mata da ke jiran haihuwa suna cikin hadari sosai. Matsalar ci gaba da faruwa a yawan sassan duniya, musamman a Afirka ta Kudu da Kewayin Karkishi, wacce ke hana samun maganin guba mai amanci, na haɗawa da dalilin da ke sa mutane nemi maganin gargajiya.

**Alamuran Ci Gaba:**

Sheƙar makarkashi ko kunna guba daga makarkashiya na iya haifar da wasu cuto masu tsanani da kuma matsala a jiki. Ci gaban guba daga makarkashiya daban-daban na iya haifar da alamu daban-daban, wasu suna da tsanani fiye da wasu. Wannan yana sa shirye-shiryen maganin guba ya zama wani abu da ya kamata a ci gaba da aiwatar da shi. Ci gaban guba na iya haifar da raunin kasala wanda zai hana numfashi, don haka ya sa kula da lafiya ya zama babban abu. Hakanan mutane na iya fuskantar cutar jini, wacce na iya haifar da mutuwar ko kuma lalacewa mara maida hankali a kan koda. Lalacewa mai tsanani a jiki na iya faruwa, wacce na iya haifar da lalacewa mara maida hankali ko kuma yawan jiki. Yaran suna fuskantar hadari sosai sakamakon jikin su na kasa da na manya. Kusan kowane mutuwar da kuma matsala da ke faruwa sakamakon ci gaban makarkashi na iya hana ta ta hanyar samar da maganin guba mai amanci da kuma samun damar amfani da shi, musamman a yankunan da ke da hadari. Maganin guba na iya hana ko maida hankali da kusan dukkanin abubuwan da guba ke haifarwa.

**Magani:**

Maganin guba na iya hana ko maida hankali da kusan dukkanin abubuwan da guba ke haifarwa, kuma an jera shi a cikin jerin maganin da WHO ke amfani da su. Samun maganin guba da kuma samun damar amfani da shi, da kuma yaduwar ilimi kan hanyoyin hana cuta a tsakanin al'umma da ma'aikatan lafiya, su ne mafi kyawun hanyoyin kawar da cutewa da mutuwar sakamakon ci gaban makarkashi. Bayan an sami rauni daga makarkashi mai yiwuwa, bi wannan:

*   Kafa kasara inda raunin ya faru.
*   Cire duk wani abu mai tsanani a kusa da wajan rauni don hana ciwon idon jiki.

**Notes on the Translation:**

*   **Terminology:** I've tried to use common Hausa terms for medical concepts.  "Ci gaban guba" is used for "envenoming," and "maga nin guba" for "antivenom."  These might need to be confirmed with local medical professionals for the most accurate and widely understood terms.
*   **Clarity and Flow:** I've restructured some sentences to improve flow in Hausa.  Direct, word-for-word translations can sometimes sound awkward.
*   **Cultural Sensitivity:** I've tried to be mindful of cultural practices related to healthcare, acknowledging the use of traditional medicine.
*   **Numbers:** I've written numbers in Arabic numerals, which is standard.
*   **"WHO List of Essential Medicines":** I translated this as "jerin maganin da WHO ke amfani da su" (the list of medicines WHO uses).
*   **"High-Risk Areas":** Translated as "yankunan da ke da hadari" (areas with risk).
*   **Further Refinement:** Ideally, this translation would be reviewed by a native Hausa speaker with medical knowledge to ensure complete accuracy and cultural appropriateness.  Specifically, the terminology used for medical conditions and treatments should be verified.

**To help me improve the translation further, could you tell me:**

*   What is the intended audience for this translation? (e.g., general public, healthcare workers, etc.)

translations.source: Reassure the victim, as most venomous snake bites do not cause immediate death. Immobilize the person completely and transport the person to a health facility as soon as possible Applying pressure at the bite site with a pressure pad may be suitable in some cases. Avoid traditional first aid methods or herbal medicines. Paracetamol may be given for local pain (which can be severe). Vomiting may occur, so place the person on their left side in the recovery position. Closely monitor airway and breathing and be ready to resuscitate if necessary
translations.reference: Ka kwantar da hankalin wanda abin aka cizan, saboda yawancin saran maciji ba ya kashe mutum nan-take. A hana mutum motsi gaba ɗaya kuma a kai shi asibiti da wuri-wuri Wasu lokutan idan aka ɗan mammatsa wurin cizon, ana iya dacewa. A guji hanyoyin taimakon gaggawa na gargajiya ko magungunan itace. Ana iya ba da paracetamol don kashe zafin wajen (wanda zai iya zama mai tsanani). Mutun yana iya yin amai, don haka a sanya shi a gefen hagunsu don daina aman. A kula da yadda yake numfashi a hankali kuma ku a kasance a shirye don farfado shi idan akwai buƙatar hakan.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy suitable for a first-aid context. I've included explanations after the translation to justify some choices.

**Hausa Translation:**

"Yi amantawa da mai ciwon, domin yawancin zazzafan cizon kururuwa ba sa haifar da mutuwar nan da nan.  Kuma kawar da mutumin a tsaye kuma kai shi zuwa asibiti da sauriya.  Zai yiwu a yi taron a wurin zazzafan cizon da taɓarɓayar ta, a wasu lokuta.  Ka guje wa hanyoyin da aka kwashe na taimakon gaggawa ko maganin gargajiya.  Ana iya ba da paracetamol don kawar da ciwon a wurin (wanda zai iya zama mai tsanani).  Zai iya faruwa dukansu zubar da abinci, don haka sanya mutumin a gefen hagu a matsayin matsayin farfawa.  Kula da hanyar numfashi da numfashi da kuskura don farfasa idan ya zama a buɗe."

**Pronunciation Guide (approximate - Hausa pronunciation can vary by region):**

*   **Yi amantawa da mai ciwon:**  Yee ah-mahn-tah-wah dah myee chee-wohn
*   **domin yawancin zazzafan cizon kururuwa:**  Doh-meen yah-wahn-cheen zah-zah-fahn chee-zon koo-roo-wah
*   **ba sa haifar da mutuwar nan da nan:**  Bah sah high-far dah moo-too-war nahn dah nahn
*   **Kuma kawar da mutumin a tsaye:**  Koo-mah kah-war dah moo-too-meen ah tsah-yeh
*   **kuma kai shi zuwa asibiti da sauriya:**  Koo-mah kai shee zoo-wah ah-see-bee-tee dah sah-oo-ree-ah
*   **Zai yiwu a yi taron a wurin zazzafan cizon da taɓarɓayar ta:**  Zai yee-woo ah yee tah-ron ah woo-reen zah-zah-fahn chee-zon dah tah-bar-bah-yar tah
*   **a wasu lokuta:**  ah wah-soo loh-koo-tah
*   **Ka guje wa hanyoyin da aka kwashe na taimakon gaggawa:**  Kah goo-jeh wah hahn-yoy-een dah ah-kah kwah-sheh nah tah-ee-mah-kon gahg-gah-wah
*   **ko maganin gargajiya:**  koh mah-gah-neen gar-gah-jee-yah
*   **Ana iya ba da paracetamol don kawar da ciwon a wurin:**  Ah-nah ee-yah bah dah pah-rah-seh-tah-mol don kah-war dah chee-wohn ah woo-reen
*   **wanda zai iya zama mai tsanani:**  wah-ndah zai ee-yah zah-mah myee tsah-nah-nee
*   **Vomiting may occur:**  Zai iya faruwa dukansu zubar da abinci
*   **don haka sanya mutumin a gefen hantu a matsayin matsayin farfawa:**  Dohn hah-kah sah-nyah moo-too-meen ah geh-fehn hahn-too ah mah-tah-sah-een mah-tah-sah-een far-fah-wah
*   **Kula da hanyar numfashi da numfashi:**  Koo-lah dah hahn-yar noom-fah-shee dah noom-fah-shee
*   **da kuskura don farfasa idan ya zama a buɗe:**  dah koo-skoo-rah dohn far-fah-sah ee-dahn yah zah-mah ah boo-deh

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Yi amantawa da mai ciwon" (Reassure the victim):**  This is a direct translation, emphasizing the importance of calming the person.
*   **"Zazzafan cizon kururuwa" (Venomous snake bite):** "Zazzafan cizon" means "hot/painful bite" and "kururuwa" means "snake."
*   **"Kuma kawar da mutumin a tsaye" (Immobilize the person completely):** "Kawar da" means to immobilize or keep still. "A tsaye" means completely or totally.
*   **"Taron a wurin zazzafan cizon" (Applying pressure at the bite site):** "Taron" means pressure.
*   **"Hanyoyin da aka kwashe na taimakon gaggawa" (Traditional first aid methods):**  "Hanyoyin da aka kwashe" means "old/traditional methods." "Taimakon gaggawa" means "first aid."
*   **"Maganin gargajiya" (Herbal medicines):** This is a standard term for traditional/herbal medicine.
*   **"Paracetamol"**:  The word is often borrowed directly into Hausa.
*   **"Mata sayin farfawa" (Recovery position):** This is the standard term for the recovery position.
*   **"Kuskura don farfasa" (Be ready to resuscitate):** "Kuskura" means "carefully" or "closely." "Farfasa" means to resuscitate.

**Important Notes:**

*   **Regional Variations:** Hausa has regional dialects. This translation aims for a generally understandable form.
*   **Context is Key:**  For highly technical medical translations, it's *always* best to have a qualified Hausa medical translator review the work.
*   **Cultural Sensitivity:**  While this translation is accurate, a local expert could further refine it to ensure it resonates best with the target audience.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: Soil-transmitted helminthiases Overview: Soil-transmitted helminth (STH) infections are among the most common infections worldwide and affect the poorest and most deprived communities. They are transmitted by eggs present in human faeces which in turn contaminate soil in areas where sanitation is poor. The main species that infect people are the roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), the whipworm (Trichuris trichiura) and the hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms produce a wide range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea, abdominal pain), general malaise and weakness. Hookworms cause chronic intestinal blood loss that result in anaemia. Soil-transmitted helminths are transmitted by eggs that are passed in the faeces of infected people. Adult worms live in the intestine where they produce thousands of eggs each day. In areas that lack adequate sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways: eggs that are attached to vegetables are ingested when the vegetables are not carefully cooked, washed or peeled; eggs are ingested from contaminated water sources; eggs are ingested by children who play in the contaminated soil and then put their hands in their mouths without washing them. Symptoms: Morbidity is related to the number of worms harboured. People with infections of light intensity (few worms) usually do not suffer from the infection. Heavier infections can cause a range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea and abdominal pain), malnutrition, general malaise and weakness, and impaired growth and physical development. Infections of very high intensity can cause intestinal obstruction that should be treated surgically. S. stercoralis may cause dermatological and gastro-intestinal morbidity and is also known to be associated with chronic malnutrition in children. In case of reduced host immunity, the parasite can cause the hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome that is invariably fatal if not promptly and properly cured and is often fatal despite the treatment. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously endorsed a resolution (WHA54.19) urging endemic countries to start seriously tackling worms, specifically schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminths. The strategy for control of soil-transmitted helminth infections is to control morbidity through the periodic treatment of at-risk people living in endemic areas. People at risk are: preschool children school-age children women of reproductive age (including pregnant women in the second and third trimesters and breastfeeding women) adults in certain high-risk occupations such as tea-pickers or miners.
translations.reference: Cutar tsutsotsi Bayani: Cututtukan tsutsotsi (wato STH a taƙaice) suna daga cikin cututtukan da aka fi sani a duk duniya kuma suna shafar al'ummomin da suka fi talauci da rashin ƙarfi. Suna samuwa ne ta hanyar ƙwai da ke cikin najasar ɗan'adam wanda hakan ke gurɓata ƙasa a wuraren da tsaftar muhalli ta ti ƙaranci. Manyan nau'ukan da ke cutar da mutane su ne roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), whipworm (Trichuris trichiura) da hookworm (Necator Americanus da Ancylostoma duodenale). Tsutsotsi na hanji suna fitar da alamomi iri-iri da suka shafi hanji (zawo, ciwon ciki), rashin lafiya da mutuwar jiki. Hookworms suna haifar da asarar jini na hanji na yau da kullun wanda ke haifar da cutar da ake kira anemia a Turance. Ana samar da waɗannan tsutsotsi ne ta hanyar ƙwai waɗanda ke fitowa a cikin najasar mutanen da suka kamu da cutar. Tsutsotsi na manya suna rayuwa ne a cikin hanji inda suke samar da dubban ƙwai kowace rana. A wuraren da ba su da isasshen tsafta, waɗannan ƙwai suna gurɓata ƙasa. Wannan na iya faruwa ta hanyoyi da yawa: ana cin wannan ƙwan da ke maƙala da kayan lambu ne a lokacin da ba a dafa kayan lambun da kyau ba, ko ba a wanke ko an ɓare bawon da kyai ba; ana cin ƙwan a gurɓataccen ruwa; yaran da ke wasa a gurɓataccen wuri suna cin ƙwan ne idan sun sanya hannayensu a cikin bakinsu ba tare da wankewa ba. Alamunta: kamuwa da cutar yana da alaƙa da adadin tsutsotsi da suka yi wa mutum katutu. Mutanen da ke ɗauke tsutsotsin ƙaɗan yawanci cutar ba ta kama su. Idan kuma suka yi yawa, suna iya haifar da alamomi iri-iri da suka danganci hanji (zawo da ciwon ciki), rashin nuna cewa mutum yana cin abinci mai gina jiki, raunin jiki da rashin girma da haɓakar jiki. Lokacin da cutar ta yi tsanani sosai, tana iya haifar da toshewar hanji wanda ya kamata a ce an yi tiyata. S. stercoralis na iya haifar da cututtuka na fara da hanji kuma an san yana da alaƙa da rashin abinci mai gina jiki na yau da kullum a tsakanin yara. Idan ma mai ɗauke da cutar garkuwar jikinsa ta ragu, ƙwayar cuta na iya karaɗe masa jiki, wadda tana iya kisa a koyaushe idan ba a yi magani da sauri ba kuma sau da yawa mutum yana iya mutuwa duk da an masa jinya. A shekarar 2001, wakilai a Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya sun amince da wani kuduri (WHA54.19) wanda yake kira ga ƙasashe masu fama da cutar da su fara yaƙar tsutsotsi, musamman schistosomiasis da helminths. Dabarar yaƙar cututtukan tsutsotsin ita ce sarrafa cututtuka ta hanyar jinya na lokaci-lokaci na mutanen da ke fama da abin waɗanda kuma ke zaune a yankunan da ke fama da cutar. Mutanen da za su iya daukar cutar: Yaran da ba su shiga makaranta ba tukunna Yara ƴan makaranta matan da suka kai shekarun haihuwa (ciki har da mata masu juna biyu da ke tsakanin wata 6 zuwa 9 da mata masu shayarwa) mutane a wasu sana'o'i masu haɗarin ɗaukar wannan cutar akwai masu noman ganyen shayi ko masu haƙar ma'adinai.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the target audience. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices and potential areas for refinement.

**Soil-transmitted helminthiases (Cututtuka ta Kankiya - Cututtukan Ganye)**

**Bayani:** Cututtukan ganye (STH) suna daga cikin cutan da suka fi yawa a duniya kuma suka shafar al'ummomin mafi talauci da na kasa karkashin matsakaici. Ana yada cutar ta kwayoyin cuta da ke cikin jinin mutane, waɗanda ke ƙara ƙarya ƙaƙƙauyar ƙasa a yammaciin inda aikin tsafta ya kasance mara kyau. Manyan nau'in cutar da ke yiwa mutane cuta su ne: gwarzawar (Ascaris lumbricoides), gwarzin kankiya (Trichuris trichiura) da gwarzoyin alfar (Necator americanus da Ancylostoma duodenale).  Gwarzoyin ciki suna haifar da bambancin alamomin cuta da suka haɗa da alamomin ciki (taƙan ciwo, zubar da ciki), gajiya da rauni. Gwarzoyin alfar suna haifar da jinin ciki na tsawon lokaci, wanda hakan ya haɗa da ƙunci.

Cututtukan ganye suna yaduwa ta kwayoyin cuta da ke fitowa a jinin mutanen da suka kamu da cuta. Gwarzoyin manya suna rayuwa a cikin ƙungiyar ciki inda suke haifar da izarin kwayoyin cuta kowace rana. A yammaciin da ba su da isasshen aikin tsafta, waɗannan kwayoyin cuta ke ƙarya ƙaƙƙauyar ƙasa. Hakan na iya faruwa ne ta hanyoyi da dama: kwayoyin cuta da ke haɗiye ga kayan abinci na amfanani ana ci gaba da su lokacin da ba a dafa, wanka ko tsire-tsire da kyau; kwayoyin cuta ana ci gaba da su daga gaɓobin ruwa marasa tsafta; kwayoyin cuta ana ci gaba da su ne ta yara da suke wasan a ƙasa mai cuta, sannan su shiga baki ba tare da wanka ba.

**Alamomin cuta:** Matsalar cuta ta dangana da adadin gwarzoyin da ke jiki. Mutanen da ke da cuta mai haske (kwanƙwaro kaɗan) yawanci ba sa ciwo daga cutar. Cututtukan da suka fi yawa na iya haifar da bambancin alamomin cuta da suka haɗa da alamomin ciki (taƙan ciwo da zubar da ciki), rashin abinci, gajiya da rauni, da kuma raƙaƙƙar girma da ci gaba na jiki. Cututtukan da suka fi yawa na iya haifar da toshewa a cikin ƙungiyar ciki wanda ya kamata a yi masa aikin tiyaka. *S. stercoralis* na iya haifar da cutar da ke shafar jini da ciki kuma an tabbatar da cewa yana da alaƙa da rashin abinci a yaran. Idan rigakafi ya ƙi ƙarfi, kwayar cuta na iya haifar da cutar ta ƙara yawan gaske/yaduwa wanda a karkice zai kasance mai kasala idan ba a warshiya ba da sauri da kuma yadda ya kamata, kuma galau yawan mutane suna mutuwa duk da warshiya.

A shekarar 2001, wakilan Majalisar Kula da Lafiya ta Duniya sun amince da ƙa'ida (WHA54.19) da ta ƙara wa kasashe masu cuta da za su fara magance cutar yaki da cutar, musamman schistomiasis da cututtukan ganye. Dabarar kawacewa cututtukan ganye ta hanyar kawance cuta ta hanyar warshiya na yawan mutane da ke cikin kasashe masu cuta. Mutanen da ke cikin hadari sun haɗa da: yara masu shekaru ƙanana, yara a makarantu, mata mata da ke da shekarun neman yara (gami da mata da ke jiran haihuwa a kwata biyu da uku da mata da ke shayarwa da nono), manya a wasu ayyukan da suka fi kasala, irin su masanan shayi ko minan.

---

**Notes on the Translation & Potential Refinements:**

*   **Terminology:** I've tried to use common Hausa terms for medical concepts. "Cututtuka ta Kankiya - Cututtukan Ganye" is a descriptive translation of "Soil-transmitted helminthiases."  It might be beneficial to consult with a Hausa-speaking medical professional to ensure the most accurate and widely understood terminology.
*   **Clarity and Flow:** I've aimed for clear and straightforward language.  Some sentences might need further refinement to ensure they flow naturally in Hausa.
*   **Cultural Context:**  Consider the literacy level and cultural background of the intended audience.  Simplifying language further might be necessary for some populations.
*   **Technical Terms:** Some technical terms (like "hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome") are challenging to translate directly. I've provided a descriptive explanation.  A more detailed explanation might be needed depending on the audience.
*   **"WHA54.19":** I've left this as is, as it's a reference code.
*   **Emphasis:**  I've used bolding to highlight key terms and phrases.

**To help me improve the translation further, could you tell me:**

*   Who is the intended audience for this translation? (e.g., healthcare workers, general public, children)
*   What is the purpose of the translation? (e.g., educational material, public health campaign)
*   Are there any specific terms or phrases that are particularly important to get right?

translations.source: TreatmentThe WHO recommended medicines –albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – are effective, inexpensive and easy to administer by non-medical personnel (e.g. teachers). They have been through extensive safety testing and have been used in millions of people with few and minor side-effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are donated to national ministries of health through WHO in all endemic countries for the treatment of all children of school age. Ivermectin for the control of S. stercoralis is expected to be available at affordable price from 2021.
translations.reference: Magani: Magungunan da WHO ta ba da shawarar aiki da su -albendazole (400 MG) da mebendazole (500 MG) - suna da tasiri, ba su da tsada kuma ga sauƙin gane yadda za a yi amfani da shi ko ga ma'aikatan da ba likita ba (misali malamai). An duba magungunan sosai ta hanyar gwaji mai yawa kuma an yi amfani da su a kan miliyoyin mutane, kuma ba a ga wata illa tare da su ba. Duka albendazole da mebendazole ana ba da su gudummawa ga ma'aikatun lafiya na ƙasa ta ƙarƙashin WHO a duk ƙasashen da ke fama da cutar don kula da duk yaran da suka isa shiga makaranta. Ivermectin wanda ake sa ran zai rage yaɗuwar S. stercoralis zai kasance a farashi mai araha daga 2021.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy suitable for a public health context. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices I made.

**Hausa Translation:**

Magani: Magungunan da WHO ta ƙayyade – albendazole (400 mg) da mebendazole (500 mg) – sun fi kiyayyar aiki, kasanya mai rahusa, kuma na iya zama da sauƙi ga ma'aikata ba likitoki ba (alalokacin, malamai). An yi gwajin lafiyarsu sosai kuma an yi amfani da su a miliyoyin mutane tare da ɓarkewar lafiya kaɗan da kuma ƙananan abubuwan da ke damun jiki. Albendazole da mebendazole an bayar da su ga ma'aikatar lafiya ta kasa ta hanyar WHO a duk kasasance da cutar domin maganin yaran makarantar. An sa ran Ivermectin don kawar da *S. stercoralis* zai kasance da farashi mai araha tun shekarar 2021.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **WHO:** I've kept "WHO" as is, as it's a widely recognized acronym even in Hausa.
*   **mg:**  I've kept "mg" as it's standard for dosage.
*   **Non-medical personnel:** I translated this as "ma'aikata ba likitoki ba" (workers who are not doctors). This is a clear and understandable way to convey the meaning.
*   **Endemic countries:** I translated this as "kasasance da cutar" (countries with the disease).
*   **School age:** I translated this as "yaran makarantar" (children of school age).
*   ***S. stercoralis***: I've kept the scientific name as is, as it's likely to be used in technical contexts.
*   **Affordable price:** I translated this as "farashi mai rahusa" (affordable price).
*   **"ɓarkewar lafiya kaɗan da kuma ƙananan abubuwan da ke damun jiki"**: This translates to "few instances of good health and minor side effects." I tried to capture the nuance of "few and minor side-effects" in a way that sounds natural in Hausa.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:** This translation is based on the provided text.  If this document is part of a larger campaign or program, it's crucial to ensure consistency with existing Hausa translations.

translations.source: Stillbirth Overview: A baby who dies after 28 weeks of pregnancy, but before or during birth, is classified as a stillbirth.There are nearly 2 million stillbirths every year – one every 16 seconds. Over 40% of all stillbirths occur during labour – a loss that could be avoided with improved quality and respectful care during childbirth including routine monitoring and timely access to emergency obstetric care when required. Experiencing a stillbirth during pregnancy or childbirth is a tragedy insufficiently addressed in global agendas, policies and funded programmes. There are psychological costs, especially to women and their families, such as maternal depression, financial consequences and economic repercussions, as well as stigma and taboo.In 2014, the World Health Assembly endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes a global target of 12 or fewer third trimester (late) stillbirths per 1,000 total births in every country by 2030. In 2021, 139 mainly high-income and upper middle-income countries had met this target, but 56 countries will not each the ENAP target by 2030 if further efforts are not made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million babies will be stillborn; nearly half of these (7.7 million, 48%) will occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: With quality health care throughout pregnancy and childbirth, most stillbirths are preventable. The major causes of stillbirth include pregnancy and childbirth-related complications, prolonged pregnancy, maternal infections such as malaria, syphilis and HIV, maternal conditions especially hypertension, and diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (when an unborn baby is unable to achieve its growth potential and therefore smaller than it should be). Congenital abnormalities are responsible for less than 10% of stillbirths reported nationally in high-income countries and the exact proportion in low-income settings is unknown due to limitations in diagnostics. Factors related to age of the mother and practicing smoking can also increase the risk of maternal disease and stillbirth. Stillbirths can be prevented through family planning to avoid unwanted pregnancies, good health and nutrition prior to and during pregnancy, quality and respectful antenatal and childbirth care including adequate skilled health personnel including midwives. Syphilis treatment in pregnancy could also prevent an estimated 200 000 stillbirths, while fetal heart rate monitoring and labour surveillance and prompt interventions when needed are crucial for preventing 832 000 intrapartum stillbirths and reducing neonatal deaths. Stillbirths are strongly linked to adverse social and economic determinants of health. Prevention and responsive care need to be integrated across the continuum of maternal health care, and beyond. This includes respectful and supportive care in the event of a death. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and their communities is essential to help address issues of stigma associated with stillbirth. WHO response: More needs to be done to integrate stillbirth prevention within global and national agendas for high quality health care for women, adolescents, and babies. Through the Global Strategy for Women's, Children's and Adolescents Health 2016–2030 and implementing the recommendations of the Every Newborn Action Plan (2014), WHO is working to end preventable stillbirths, as part of efforts to improve maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health. Most stillbirths do not receive a birth certificate and are not registered. Improving systems for reporting births and neonatal deaths is a matter of human rights and a prerequisite for reducing stillbirths. In addition, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately capture and report stillbirths for monitoring and improving the quality of care. WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on both stillbirths and neonatal deaths. WHO also has guidance to help countries review and investigate individual deaths so they can recommend and implement solutions to prevent similar ones in the future and a standard classification system for perinatal deaths (ICD-PM). WHO and UNICEF are supporting a call for collective action to end preventable stillbirths by:
translations.reference: Haihuwar jariri a mace Bayani: Jaririn da ya mutu bayan makonni 28 a ciki, kafin ko lokacin haihuwa, ana ƙirga shi a matsayin wanda aka haifa a mace. Ana haihuwar jarirai kusan miliyan 2 a mace a kowace shekara - jariri ɗaya ke nan a cikin duk daƙika 16. Fiye da kashi 40 cikin 100 na jariran da ake haifa a mace suna mutuwa ne a lokacin naƙuda – asarar da za a iya guje wa ta hanyar ingantaccen tsari da nuna kulawa yayin haihuwa, ciki har da sa ido na yau da kullum da ba da kulawar haihuwa ta gaggawa lokacin da ya kamata. Mutuwar jarirai a lokacin juna biyu ko lokacin haihuwa wani bala'i ne wanda ba a magance shi ba a cikin ajandar duniya, manufofi da shirye-shiryen da aka ba wa kuɗi. Ana samun kaɗuwar zuciya, musamman ga mata da danginsu, kamar shiga damuwa lokacin jego, matsalolin kuɗi, da kuma nuna ƙyama da ƙiyayya. A shekarar 2014, Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta amince da Tsarin Hidima ga Jarirai (ENAP), wanda ya haɗa da burin rage adadin mace-macen jarirai a cikin mahaifa da guda 12 ko ƙasa da haka a wata na huɗu zuwa shida lokacin rainon ciki a jimillar haihuwa 1,000 a kowace ƙasa nan da 2030. A shekarar 2021, ƙasashe 139 galibi masu ƙarfin tattalin arziki da masu tattalin arziki dama-dama sun cimma wannan manufa, amma ƙasashe 56 ba za su cimma wannan manufa ta ENAP ba nan da shekarar 2030 idan ba a ƙara yin kokari ba. Idan aka ci gaba a haka, za a haifi jarirai miliyan 15.9 a mace; kuma kusan rabin waɗannan (watau miliyan 7.7, 48%) duk a yankin ƙasashen kudu da Sahara. Abubuwan da ke jawo shi: Idan akwai ingantacciyar kulawar lafiya tun daga lokacin rainon ciki da haihuwa, ana iya kauce wa mafi yawan haihuwar jarirai a mace. Manyan abubuwan da ke haifar da haihuwar jarirai a mace sun haɗa da matsalolin rainon ciki da haihuwa, doguwar naƙuda, cututtuka kamar zazzabin cizon sauro, cutar sifilis da cutar sida, hawan jini, da ciwon sukari, da hana girman jaririn da ke ciki. Matsalolin da ake haifar mutum da su suna haddasa ƙasa da kaso 10 na mutuwar jarirai kafin a haife su kamar yadda aka ruwaito a ƙasashe masu ƙarfin tattalin arziki kuma ba a san ainihin adadin ba a ƙasashe masu ƙarancin tattalin arziki saboda rashin iya binciko waɗannan lamurran. Abubuwan da suka danganci shekarun uwa da kuma shan taba suna iya ƙara yiwuwar kamuwa da cututtukan da ke da alaƙa da jego da kuma haihuwar jarirai a mace. Ana iya kauce wa haihuwar jarirai a mace ta hanyar tsarin iyali don hana samin haihuwar da ba a buƙata, samun cikakken kiwon lafiya da abinci mai gina jiki kafin da lokacin rainon ciki, samun ingantacciyar kulawa yayin haihuwa, ciki har da samar da ƙwararrun ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya, kamar da ungozoma. Maganin cutar sifilis a lokacin rainon ciki shi ma zai iya hana haihuwar kimanin jarirai 200 000 a mace, yayin da saka idanu kan bugun zuciya da sa ido a kan aiki da kuma kawo ɗauki lokacin da ake buƙata suna da mahimmanci don kauce wa mutuwar jarirai da ke cikin mahaifansu guda 832,000 tare da rage mutuwar jarirai. Haihuwar jariri a mace har yanzu tana da alaƙa mai ƙarfi da munanan al'amurran lafiya na zamantakewa da tattalin arziki. Riga-kafi da nuna kulawa ta hanyar kai agaji suna buƙatar a haɗa su a yi amfani da su a fannin kula da lafiyar mata da sauran fannoni. Wannan ya haɗa da kulawa da tallafi a yayin mutuwa. Sauraron abubuwan da suka faru mata da al'ummominsu da suka fito yana da mahimmanci don taimakawa wajen magance matsalolin ƙyama da ke da alaƙa da haihuwar jarirai a mace. Amsar WHO: Ana buƙatar ƙara zage damtse don ganin an sanya yaƙi da haihuwar jarirai a mace cikin ajandoji na duniya da na ƙasa don ingantaccen kiwon lafiya ga mata, matasa, da jarirai. Ta ƙarƙashin shirin Dabarun Lafiyar Mata, Yara da Matasa a Faɗin Diniya 2016-2030 da amfani da shawarwarin kowane Tsarin Hidima wa Jarirai a (2014), WHO na aiki don kawo ƙarshen haifuwar jarirai mace, a matsayin wani ɓangare na ƙoƙarin inganta lafiyar mata masu juna biyu, Jariri, yaro da lafiyar matasa. Yawancin jariran da ake haihuwa a mace ba sa samun takardar shaidar haihuwa kuma ba su da rajista. Inganta tsarin bayar da rahoton haihuwa da mutuwar jarirai al'amari ne na 'yancin ɗan'adam kuma abu ne da ake buƙata don rage haifar jarirai a mace. Bugu da ƙari, akwai buƙatar ƙarfafa tsarin bayanan kiwon lafiya na yau da kullum don samar da sahihin rahoton haihuwa don sa ido da haɓaka ingancin ba da kulawar lafiya. WHO ta ba da kayan aiki don taimaka wa ƙasashe su inganta ɗaukar ƙididdigarsu game da haihuwa da kuma mutuwar jarirai. WHO ta kuma ba da hanyoyi don taimaka wa ƙasashe yin nazari da bincika mutuwar mutane don su iya ba da shawararda aiwatar da mafita don hana irin waɗannan a nan gaba da kuma samar da tsarin tantance mutuwa kafin haihuwa (ICD-PM). WHO da UNICEF suna goyan bayan kira ga matakin gama kai don kawo ƙarshen haifuwar jarirai a mace ta hanyar:

Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices and potential areas for refinement.

**Translation (Hausa):**

**Babban Bayanai kan Mutum Mai Haɗuri (Stillbirth):** Mutum mai haɗuri shi ne jariri da ya mutu ne bayan makonni 28 na juna biyu, ko kuma kafin haihuwa ko a lokacin haihuwa. Kusan miliyan 2 na mutum mai haɗuri ke faruwa kowace shekara – daya kowane dakika 16. Sama da kashi 40% na mutum mai haɗuri ke faruwa a lokacin haihuwa – wanda za’a iya hana ashe ta hanyar inganta ingancin kula da kuma kulawa mai mutunci a lokacin haihuwa, gami da binciken yawa da samun isassun kayan aikin kula da cutar haihuwa a lokacin da kake bukata.

Zubar da jariri a lokacin juna biyu ko haihuwa babban abin damuwa ne, kuma ba a bayyana shi sosai a cikin ayyukan duniya, manufofin da kuma shirye-shiryen da aka kafa. Akwai kashe-kashenin ilimin hankali, musamman ga mata da iyalansu, irin su cutar wahala a matsayin mahaifi, sakamakon kudi da tasiri mai illa a kan tattalin arziki, da kuma illa da tabar maka.

A shekarar 2014, Majalisar Kula da Lafiya ta Duniya ta amince da Dukiyar Aikin Jariri (Every Newborn Action Plan - ENAP), wacce ta haɗa burin duniya na kusan 12 ko ƙaramar mutum mai haɗuri a kowace ƙasa kowane 1,000 jarirai a shekarar 2030. A shekarar 2021, kasashe 139, galibi kasashe masu kudin da suka yi yawa da kuma matsakaicin kudi, sun cimma wannan burin, amma kasashe 56 ba za su cimma burin ENAP ba a shekarar 2030 idan ba a ƙara ƙoƙari ba. Idan yanayin yanzu na ci gaba, miliyan 15.9 na jarirai za su zubar da rayuwarsu; kusan rabi na wannan adadi (miliyan 7.7, 48%) za a gani a Afirka ta Kudu da Sahal.

**Abubuwan Da Ke Haɗuwa:** Tare da kula da lafiya mai inganci a duk lokacin juna biyu da haihuwa, za’a iya hana yawancin mutum mai haɗuri. Manyan abubuwan da ke haɗuwa sun haɗa da cutar juna biyu da haihuwa, tsawaita juna biyu, cutar da ke shafar mata irin su malaria, syphilis da HIV, yanayin mata irin su high blood pressure da sukari, da kuma kawar da girman jariri (inda jariri bai isa girman da ya kamata ba). Matsalolin da suka haɗuwa da haihuwa sun haɗa da kasan kashi 10% na mutum mai haɗuri da aka bayar a kasashe masu kudin da suka yi yawa, kuma adadin da ya dace a cikin kasashe marasa kudin da suka yi yawa bai sani ba saboda iyakancewar bincike. Abubuwan da suka shafi shekarar mahaifi da kuma kasancewa a kan kasuwanci na taba na iya ƙara haɗarin cutar mahaifi da mutum mai haɗuri.

Za’a iya hana mutum mai haɗuri ta hanyar tsara iyalwa don guje wa juna biyu mara izila, kula da lafiya da abinci mai gina jiki kafin da kuma a lokacin juna biyu, kula da lafiya mai inganci da mutunci a lokacin juna biyu da haihuwa, gami da adadin ma'aikata na lafiya da kuma m midwives. Maganin syphilis a lokacin juna biyu na iya hana kusan mutum mai haɗuri 200,000, yayin binciken rate na zuciyar jariri da binciken haihuwa da kuma tsanaki lokacin da kake bukata yana da mahimmanci don hana mutum mai haɗuri 832,000 a lokacin haihuwa da rage mutuwar jarirai. Mutum mai haɗuri yana da alaka mai ƙarfi da abubuwan illa na zamantauwa da tattalin arziki.

Kula da hana mutum mai haɗuri da kuma amsawa ta buƙata ya kamata a haɗa shi a duk fannin kula da lafiya ta mata, kuma a wajen wannan. Wannan ya haɗa da kulawa mai mutunci da kuma tallafi a lokacin mutuwar. Jin dadin raayoyin mata da al'umomin su yana da mahimmanci don taimakawa wajen warware matsalolin da suka shafi mutum mai haɗuri.

**Amsar WHO:** Ya kamata a yi ƙarin aiki don haɗa hana mutum mai haɗuri a cikin ayyukan duniya da kuma ƙasa don kula da lafiya mai inganci ga mata, matasa, da jarirai. Ta hanyar Taron Kula da Lafiya na Mata, Yara da Matasa 2016–2030 da aiwatar da shawara na Dukiyar Aikin Jariri (2014), WHO na aiki don kawar da mutum mai haɗuri, a matsayin wani bangare na ƙoƙarin inganta lafiyar mahaifi, jarirai, yara da matasa. Yawancin mutum mai haɗuri ba su samu takardar haihuwa ba kuma ba a shigar da su a cikin gwanon kwamitin. Inganta tsarin bayar da rahoton haihuwa da mutuwar jarirai al'amurce ne na hakkokin dan-adam da kuma buƙatar rage mutum mai haɗuri. Bugu da kari, akwai buƙatar ƙarfafa tsarin bayar da bayanai na yau da kullun don daidaita da kuma bayar da rahoton mutum mai haɗuri don saka idanu da inganta ingancin kula. WHO yana ba da kayan aiki don taimaka wa kasashe don inganta bayansu akan mutum mai haɗuri da mutuwar jarirai. WHO kuma yana da jagora don taimaka wa kasashe don duba da kuma bincika mutuwar mutane don su iya bayar da shawara da aiwatar da hanyoyin hana irin wannan a gaba da kuma tsarin rarraba mai ma'auni don mutuwar perinatal (ICD-PM). WHO da UNICEF suna tallafawa kiran aiki na hadin gwiwa don kawar da mutum mai haɗuri.

**Hausa Translation:**

Ƙara wayar da hankali da ƙara ƙarƙuzuwar dangantaka, tallafawa mata da iyalai da suka yi jiƙan mutuwar yaro, ƙarfafa tsarin lafiya don samar da babban matakin kula da lafiya, kafa burin rage mutuwar yaro a matakin kasa da kananan hukumomi, da inganta aiyukan adadi akan mutuwar yaro don ƙara ƙarfi da ilimi.

**Pronunciation Guide (approximate - Hausa pronunciation can be nuanced):**

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **"Increasing awareness"**: Translated as "Ƙara wayar da hankali" (literally, "increasing the spreading of awareness"). This is a common and natural way to express this concept in Hausa.
*   **"Reducing stigma"**: Translated as "ƙara ƙarƙuzuwar dangantaka" (literally, "increasing the reduction of relationship/connection").  Stigma is a complex concept, and this phrasing aims to capture the idea of lessening the negative social connections and judgments associated with stillbirth.
*   **"Bereaved women and families"**: Translated as "mata da iyalai da suka yi jiƙan mutuwar yaro" (women and families who have experienced the grief of the death of a child).  This is a more descriptive and culturally appropriate way to convey the meaning.

*   **"Nationalizing and localizing stillbirth targets"**: Translated as "kafa burin rage mutuwar yaro a matakin kasa da kananan hukumomi" (setting the target of reducing stillbirth at the national and local government levels).  This aims to be clear about the scope of the targets.
*   **"Improving measurement of stillbirths"**: Translated as "da inganta aiyukan adadi akan mutuwar yaro" (and improving the measurement activities on stillbirths).
*   **"To enhance evidence and knowledge"**: Translated as "don ƙara ƙarfi da ilimi" (to increase strength and knowledge).

translations.source: Substandard and falsified medical products Overview: Up to two billion people around the world lack access to necessary medicines, vaccines, medical devices including in vitro diagnostics, and other health products, which creates a vacuum that is too often filled by substandard and falsified products. This problem is growing as global supply chains become more complex, meaning products manufactured in one country may be packaged in a second country and distributed across borders to be marketed or sold to consumers in a third. The growth of e-commerce also contributes to this trend by making it easier to purchase medicines online, often from unauthorized sources. WHO has identified this issue one of the urgent health challenges for the next decade given that more than one in ten medicines in low- and middle-income countries are estimated to be substandard or falsified. No country remains untouched from this issue, and WHO has received reports of substandard or falsified medical medicines, vaccines and in vitro diagnostics from all regions of the world. Both generic and innovator medicines can be falsified, ranging from very expensive products for cancer to very inexpensive products for treatment of pain. WHO Member States have agreed on a comprehensive global strategy focused on prevention, detection and response to move towards achieving increased access to quality, safe, effective and quality medical products. Impact: Substandard and falsified medical products create many problems for both the individual taking the medicine and for families, the larger society, health care system and supply chain. When medicines do not work the way they should, they can fail to treat or prevent disease, contribute to antimicrobial resistance, waste precious resources and lead to a loss of confidence in medicines and distrust of healthcare providers. This can have a negative socioeconomic impact, contributing to related loss in productivity and adding expenses to the individual and national health system. They are most likely to reach patients in situations where there is constrained access to quality and safe medical products, poor governance and weak technical capacity. Although this issue impacts all countries, countries where there are weak or non-existent regulatory systems bear the greatest burden of substandard and falsified medical products. In an increasingly globalized world, no one country has sufficient resources and capacity to deal with this issue in isolation. Through global systems and processes such as the WHO Global Surveillance and Monitoring System and WHO Member State Mechanism, Member States are working together to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.reference: Kayan aikin jinya da magunguna marasa inganci da na bogi Bayyani: Kimanin mutane biliyan biyu a duniya ba su da damar samun ainihin magunguna, alluran riga-kafi, na'urorin likitanci da suka hada da na bincika rashin lafiya, da sauran kayayyakin kiwon lafiya, waɗanda saboda rashinsu sai dai kawai a yi amfani da marasa inganci kuma na bogi. Wannan matsala tana ta ƙaruwa ne yayin da salsalar samar da kayayyaki na duniya ke ƙara cika da sarƙaƙƙiya, ma'ana kayayyakin da aka ƙera a wata ƙasa ana iya tattara ninke su cikin fakiti a wata ƙasa kuma a tura su don a tallata ko a sayar da su ga masu siye a wata ƙasa daban. Bunƙasar kasuwancin zamani ya taimaka wajen wannan al'amari ta hanyar sauƙaƙawa wajen siyan magungunan ta intanet, yawanci ta hannun waɗanda ba su cancanta ba. . Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta bayyana wannan matsalar da ɗaya daga cikin matsalolin lafiya da za a fuskanta nan da shekara goma, inda aka yi hasashen samun ɗaya daga cikin magunguna goma yakasance ba shi da inganci ko na jabu a ƙasashen da ke da ƙaranci da matsakaicin kuɗin shiga. Babu wata ƙasa da wannan al'amari bai shafa ba, in Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta samu rahotanni game da magunguna da allurori marasa inganci ko na jabu da kuma gwaje-gwajen cututtuka daga kome lungu da saƙo na duniya. Wannan lamari na yin jabu ka iya shafar kowane nau'i na magani, kama daga magungunan kansa masu tsada zuwa magungunan kashe zafi masu sauƙi. ƙasashen da cikin Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya sun amince da a ɗauki babban mataki da zai mayar da hankali wajen kawarwa da ganowa da kuma ɗaukar mataki wajen samun ƙaruwar kaiwa ga ingantacce, amintacce kuma gangariyar magani. Tasiri: Magunguna marasa inganci ko na jabu na haifar da matsaloli da dama ga ɗaiɗaikun mutane da iyalai da suke amfani da su da ma al'umma da tsarin kula da lafiya da kuma hanyoyin rarraba su. Yayin da maganunguna ba su yi aiki ba, za su gaza ne wajen yin maganin cuta, lamarin da ka iya haifar da tirjiyar ƙwayoyin cuta da ɓarnar dukiya da kuma daina amincewa da da magunguna da kuma masu samar da kulawa ta lafiya. Wannan ka iya haifar da mummunan tasiri a kan tsarin tattalin arziki da zamantakewa, wanda shi kuma zai taimaka wajen daina samar da magungunan da ƙarin kashe kuɗin ga ɗaiɗaikun mutane da kuma tsarin lafiya na ƙasa. Wannan tasiri ka iya kaiwa ga marasa lafiya, a yayin da aka samu tarnaƙi wajen kaiwa wa ga ingantacce kuma amintaccen magani da rashin shugabanci mai nagarta da kuma rauni wajen amfani da fasaha. Kodayake wannan matsala ta shafi kafatanin ƙasashen duniya, to amma a ƙasashen da da suke da rauni ko rashin tsarin sa-ido, sun fi samun magunguna marasa inganci ko na jabu. Sakamakon yadda duniya ke ci gaba da kasancewa tamkar ɗaya, babu wata ƙasa da ke da ƙarfin tattalin arzikin da za ta tunkari wannan matsalar ita kaɗai. Ta hanyar tsare-tsare na duniya da kuma hukumomi irin su Hukumar Sa-ido ta Hukumar Llafiya ta Duniya da kuma Kwamitin Mambobin ƙasashe na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya, Mabobin ƙasashe na aiki kafaɗa da kafaɗa domin hana da gano da kuma ɗaukar matakai a kan magaunguna marasa inganci ko jabu.
