translations.source: Wildfires Overview: Wildfires are increasing around the globe in frequency, severity and duration, heightening the need to understand the health effects of wildfire exposure. The risk of wildfires grows in extremely dry conditions, such as drought, heat waves and during high winds.Wildfire smoke is a mixture of hazardous air pollutants, such PM2.5, NO2, ozone, aromatic hydrocarbons, or lead. In addition to contaminating the air with toxic pollutants, wildfires also simultaneously impact the climate by releasing large quantities of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases into the atmosphere. With climate change leading to warmer temperatures and drier conditions and the increasing urbanization of rural areas, the fire season is starting earlier and ending later. Wildfire events are getting more extreme in terms of acres burned, duration and intensity, and they can disrupt transportation, communications, water supply, and power and gas services. Impact: Wildfires that burn near populated areas can have significant impact on the environment, property, livestock and human mortality and morbidity depending on the size, speed and proximity to the fire, and whether the population has advanced warning to evacuate. Wildfire smoke is a mixture of air pollutants of which particulate matter (PM) is the principal public health threat. PM2.5 from wildfire smoke is associated with premature deaths in the general population, and can cause and exacerbate diseases of the lungs, heart, brain/nervous system, skin, gut, kidney, eyes, nose and liver. It has also been shown to lead to cognitive impairment and memory loss. Firefighters and emergency response workers are also greatly impacted by injuries, burns and smoke inhalation, particularly at high concentrations. More interdisciplinary research is warranted to understand the latent and long-term health effects of wildfire exposure on vulnerable populations (children, older people, pregnant people, chronically ill people), particularly for geographic areas enduring repeated and cyclical exposure to these wildﬁre events. WHO Response: The magnitude of the physical and human costs from wildfires can be reduced if adequate emergency prevention, preparedness, response and recovery measures are implemented in a sustainable and timely manner. Working with Member States, WHO helps to build resilient and proactive health systems that can anticipate the needs and challenges during emergencies, so that we can be more likely to reduce risks and respond effectively when needed. WHO works with partners in preparing, preventing, detecting, responding and recovering from emergencies and disasters, including environmental exposures, such asimplementing early warning systems and issuing health and air quality advisories; containing the release of hazardous materials; assessing health needs of the community and infrastructure damage; restoring primary care services, like immunization, child and maternal health, and mental health; strengthening human resources for disaster management; collecting, analysing and disseminating information related to emergencies and disasters that are likely to occur in a region; establishing and managing stocks of relief supplies and equipment; calling for emergency funding to support health action; and developing national policies, recommendations and national emergency response plans.
translations.reference: Gobarar daji Taƙaitaccen bayani: yawan ƙaruwar afkuwar gobarar daji abu ne da ke nuni da buƙatar fahimtar ƙalubale na lafiya da wutar daji ke da shi ga waɗanda ya ritsa da su. Illar wutar daji na ƙaruwa matuƙa a wuri da ke ƙamfar ruwa kamar inda ake da ƙarancin ruwan sama da tsananin zafi da iska mai ƙarfi. Hayaƙin wutar daji na ɗauke da sinadai masu guba da ke gurɓata iska kamar PM2.5 da ozone da hydrocarbon ko lead. Sannan, game da batun gurɓata iska da sinadarai masu guba, gobarar daji a lokaci guda na shafar sauyin yanayi ta hanyar fitar da iska ga sararin samaniya a matsayin hayaƙin da ke ɗauke da kabon-dai-oksida da sauran nau'ikan iska da ke cuɗanya da zafi. La'akari da sauyin yanayi da ke kan gaba wajen ɗumama yanayi da yanayi bushewar wuri da ƙaruwar ɗabi'ar mai da ƙauyuka birane, akwai yuwuwar gobarar na iya fara ruruwa da wuri da kuma ɗaukar lokaci mai ɗan tsawo. afkuwar gobarar daji na cikgaba da tsananta ta hanyar ƙone kadada mai yawa da irin lokaci da ƙarfin da gobarar ke da shi wanda tana iya kawo cikas ga harkokin sufuri da sadarwa da samar da ruwan sha da samar da makamashi da iskar gas. Tasiri: gobarar daji da ke ƙone wurare masu cukowar al'umma zai iya shafar muhalli da dukiya da dabbobi da ɗabi'u da kamuwa da cutattuka duk da ya danganta da girman wurin da sauri da kusanci da gobarar da kuma yuwuwar samun gargaɗi na barin wurin da abin ka iya faruwa. hayaƙin gobarar daji haɗe yake da batun gurɓata iska wanda ya sanya ta ɗaya daga cikin lura a fannin lafiyar al'umma. ana alaƙanta mutuwar fuju'a ta mutane da sinadarai masu girman PM2.5 da ake samu a cikin iskar gobarar daji wanda kuma ke taɓarɓara cutattukan da suka shafi hunhu da na zuciya da tsarin zubin ƙwaƙwalwa da jijiyoyi da fata da hanji da ƙoda da idanu da hanci da hanta. sannan bincike ya tabbatar da cewa takan iya shafar sashen tunanin abin da aka saba yi na ƙwaƙwalwa da mantuwa. Masu kashe gobarar dajin da jami'an kai ɗauki na gamuwa da raunuka da shaƙar iska da yawa. bincike a fannoni daban-daban ya nuna irin tasirin na dogon lokaci da gobarar daji ke da shi ga lafiyar masu fama da ita (yara da tsofaffi da masu juna biyu da masu fama da cuta mai tsanani) musamman a wurare da ake yawan samun afkuwar gobarar daji akai-akai. Matakin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya: za a iya rage yadda gobarar daji ke shafar al'umma idan aka samar da wadatattun hanyoyin magance ta da gaggawa da zaman ko-ta-kwana da tabbatar da matakan kai ɗauki a kan lokaci ba tare da wata gazawa ba. Yin aiki tare da ƙasashen mambobin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya wajen taimaka musu su samar da tabbataccen tsarin kiwon lafiya da zai uta fuskantar ƙalubale na gaggawa da ka iya tasowa ta yadda za a iya rage haɗari da kai dauki a lokacin da ya dace. Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya na yin aiki da masu haɗin guiwa wajen shiryawa da magancewa da ganowa da farfaɗowa daga yanayi na gaggawa ko annoba, har ma da jan hankalin al'umman da ke fuskantar barazanar gobarar ta hanyar gargaɗinsu a kan lokaci da ba shawarwari na kiwon lafiya da na ingancin matsayin iskar da ake shaƙa.; Ba da bayanai na abubuwa masu haɗari da duba matakin kiwon lafiya da al'umma ke buƙata da na kayan more rayuwar da aka yi asara.; Maido da kiwon lafiya a matakin farko kamar rigakafi da inganta lafiyar mata da ƙananan yara da lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa da kula da al'umma wajen magance afkuwar annobar.; Ƙarfafar shirin mutane don bada kulawa kan afkuwar ibtila’i.; ƙarɓa da yaɗa bayanai masu alaƙa da yanayi na buƙatar gaggawa da annoba da ake hasashen suna iya faruwa a wani yanki; assasa da kula da kayan tallafi da sauran kayayyaki.; Yin kira wajen samar da kuɗin tallafawa wajen ɗaukar mataki na kiwon lafiya; da samar da tsare-tsare na ƙasa da shawarwari da tsara yadda za a ba da agajin gaggawa a mataki na ƙasa.

**Translation (Hausa):**

**Bincike Akan Wuta da ke Karkata Mace-Mace (Wildfire):** Wuta da ke karkata mace-mace suna karɓuwa a duniya baki daya, wajen yawan gaske, tsanaki, da lokaci, wanda ke ƙara buƙatar fahimtar tasirin wuta akan lafiya. Haɗarin wuta da ke karkata mace-mace ya ƙaru a cikin yanayi mai ƙaƙƙarfan bushi, kamar ƙunci, zafin jiki, da iska mai ƙarfi.  Kamus na wuta da ke karkata mace-mace shine wani ruwan gizo mai cutarwa, gami da PM2.5, NO2, ozone, hydrocarbons mai daɗi, ko kuma ƙwarƙwarar.  Baya ga cutar da iska da cutarwa, wuta da ke karkata mace-mace kuma tana tasiri akan yanayi ta fitar da kusan kowane adadi na carbon dioxide da sauran isassanci na isassanci zuwa isassanci.  Tare da canjin yanayi da ke haifar da zafin jiki da yanayi mai bushi, da kuma karuwar birane a yankunan karkara, kakar wuta ta fara da wuri kuma ta ƙare da dadi.  Abubuwan wuta da ke karkata mace-mace suna zubar da ƙarfi a cikin adadi na acres da aka ƙone, lokaci, da ƙarfi, kuma suna iya kasancewa da abubuwan haɗin gwiwa, sadarwa, samar da ruwa, da ayyukan wutar lantebbi da gas.

translations.source: Women's health Overview: Being a man or a woman has a significant impact on health, as a result of both biological and gender-related differences. The health of women and girls is of particular concern because, in many societies, they are disadvantaged by discrimination rooted in sociocultural factors. For example, women and girls face increased vulnerability to HIV/AIDS. Some of the sociocultural factors that prevent women and girls to benefit from quality health services and attaining the best possible level of health include: unequal power relationships between men and women; social norms that decrease education and paid employment opportunities; an exclusive focus on women’s reproductive roles; and potential or actual experience of physical, sexual and emotional violence. While poverty is an important barrier to positive health outcomes for both men and women, poverty tends to yield a higher burden on women and girls’ health due to, for example, feeding practices (malnutrition) and use of unsafe cooking fuels (COPD).
translations.reference: Kiwon lafiyar mata Taƙaitaccen bayani: kasancewa namiji ko mace abu ne mai muhimmanci ga batun lafiya saboda irin bambance-bambance da ke samu na halitta. Lafiyar mata da ƙananan yara mata na da muhimmanci domin a wasu al'ummatan sukan rasa gata saboda bambanci da ke da alaƙa da al'ada ta rayuwar yau da kullum. Ga misali mata da ƙananan yara mata na fuskantar barazanar kamuwa da HIV/AIDS. wasu daga ciki dalilai masu alaƙa da al'ada ta yau da kullum da ke daƙile mata daga samun ingantaccen kulawa ta lafiya sun hada da: bambancin iko da ke akwai tsakanin maza da mata; Tsarin zamantakewa da ke daƙile ilimi da batun damarmaki na aikin da za a biya mutum; Ba wa ɓangaren batun rawar mata wajen haihuwa muhimmanci; da da kuma fuskantar cin zarafi na sarari ko na fannin saduwa ko na imoshan. Duk da talauci na taka rawa wajen kawo cikas ga samun ingantaccen kiwon lafiya ga maza da mata; mata da ƙananan yara mata sun fi fuskantar ƙalubalen talauci, ga misali ɓangaren cin abinci (rashin wadataccen abinci) da amfani da abin girki masu illa (COPD).
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Hausa, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the context of women's health and potential cultural nuances. I'll also include some notes after the translation explaining some of the choices I made.

**Hausa Translation:**

**Bincike game da Lafiyar Mata:** Kasancewa namiji ko mata yana da tasiri mai yawa akan lafiya, sakamakon bambance-bambancen jima'i da kuma bambance-bambancen da ke danganta da launin kai. Lafiyar mata da mata yarinya ta zama babban damari saboda a al'ummomin da dama, suna fuskantar wani nauyi bisa ga tsanga-tsangin da ke addace su bisa ga al'ada da kuma abubuwan da suka shafi al'umma. Alalokacin, mata da mata yarinya suna fuskantar hadarin samun cutar HIV/AIDS. Wasu daga cikin abubuwan da suka shafi al'umma waɗanda ke hana mata da mata yarinya amfana da ayyukan lafiya na ingila da kuma samun mafi kyawun lafiya sun haɗa da: rashin daidaito a tsakanin maza da mata; ayyukan al'ada waɗanda ke rage dama ga ilimi da kuma aikin yi; mai da hankali ne kawai ga ayyukan haihuwa na mata; da kuma yiwuwa ko kuma kwarewa da cutar da jiki, jima'i da kuma zubar da rai. Alamanin talauci babban cikas ce ga kowane mutum akan samun lafiya, amma talauci kan yi yawa akan lafiyar mata da mata yarinya, bisa ga abubuwan da suka hada da hanyoyin abinci (ciwon abinci) da amfani da makamin gas keɓaɓɓen (COPD).

**Pronunciation Guide (approximate - Hausa pronunciation can be tricky!):**

*   **Bincike:** Been-chee-keh
*   **Lafiyar:** Lah-fee-yar
*   **Mata:** Mah-tah
*   **Namiji:** Nah-mee-jee
*   **HIV/AIDS:**  Hee-vee/Ay-dees (pronounced similarly to English)
*   **COPD:**  See-oh-pee-dee (pronounced similarly to English)
*   **Tsanga-tsangin:** Tsahn-gah-tsahn-geen
*   **Al'umma:** Al-uh-mah

**Notes on Translation Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Women's health Overview"**: I translated this as "Bincike game da Lafiyar Mata" which means "An Investigation/Review about Women's Health."  "Bincike" is a good general term for an overview or examination.
*   **"Biological and gender-related differences"**:  I used "bambance-bambancen jima'i da kuma bambance-bambancen da ke danganta da launin kai."  "Jima'i" refers to biological differences (sex), and "launin kai" refers to gender-related differences (social roles, expectations).
*   **"Discrimination rooted in sociocultural factors"**:  I translated this as "tsanga-tsangin da ke addace su bisa ga al'ada da kuma abubuwan da suka shafi al'umma."  "Tsanga-tsangin" means discrimination, "addace" means to oppress or burden, and the rest clarifies the source of the discrimination.
*   **"Unequal power relationships"**:  I used "rashin daidaito a tsakanin maza da mata" which means "lack of equality between men and women."
*   **"Social norms that decrease education and paid employment opportunities"**: I translated this as "ayyukan al'ada waɗanda ke rage dama ga ilimi da kuma aikin yi" which means "customary practices that reduce access to education and employment."
*   **"Exclusive focus on women’s reproductive roles"**: I used "mai da hankali ne kawai ga ayyukan haihuwa na mata" which means "only focusing on the reproductive roles of women."
*   **"Physical, sexual and emotional violence"**: I translated this as "cutar da jiki, jima'i da kuma zubar da rai" which means "harm to the body, sexual harm, and emotional harm."
*   **"Poverty tends to yield a higher burden on women and girls’ health"**: I used "talauci kan yi yawa akan lafiyar mata da mata yarinya" which means "poverty tends to weigh heavily on the health of women and girls."
*   **"Feeding practices (malnutrition)"**: I used "hanyoyin abinci (ciwon abinci)" which means "feeding practices (malnutrition)."
*   **"Unsafe cooking fuels (COPD)"**: I used "amfani da makamin gas keɓaɓɓen (COPD)" which means "use of unsafe fuel (COPD)."  I kept COPD as is, as it's a widely recognized acronym.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:** Hausa has a rich oral tradition.  The best translation will always depend on the specific audience and purpose.
*   **Cultural Sensitivity:**  Be mindful of cultural norms and sensitivities when discussing topics like gender roles, violence, and health.
*   **Terminology:**  Medical terminology can be challenging.  It's often necessary to use descriptive phrases rather than direct translations.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Hausa speaker with expertise in health and gender issues to ensure accuracy and cultural appropriateness.



I hope this translation is helpful!  Please let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate or if you have any questions about this translation.

translations.source: Yaws (Endemic treponematoses) Overview: Yaws is a chronic skin infection characterized by papillomas (noncancerous lumps) and ulcers. It is caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pertenue, which belongs to the same group of bacteria that causes venereal syphilis. Yaws primarily affects children aged under 15 years who live in poor communities in warm, humid and tropical forested areas of Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Pacific islands. The majority of affected populations live rural areas, far from health services. Poverty, low socio-economic conditions and poor personal hygiene facilitate the spread of yaws. Although there are over 80 000 cases of yaws each year, experts believe the disease can be controlled and ultimately eradicated for several reasons. First, it only occurs in humans, not animals, making control much easier. It is also easy to treat with readily available drugs and has already been eliminated in some countries, including India. The remaining pockets of yaws infection, although usually in remote places, means further spread is less likely with proper surveillance and control measures. Symptoms: There are two stages of yaws infection. The early stage is when the patient is infectious and can spread the disease to others. In this stage, a papule (a noncancerous, outward-growing lump) develops at the site of infection. This papule is full of the organisms and may persist for 3–6 months followed by natural healing. Without treatment, this is followed by disseminated skin lesions over the body. Bone pain and bone lesions may also occur. The second stage is noninfectious and typically appears five years after infection. It is characterized by disabling consequences of the nose, bones and palmar/plantar hyperkeratosis (thickening). After a field diagnosis based on these symptoms, the disease can be confirmed by examining a sample from a skin legion under a special type of microscope (darkfield examination). Blood tests are not typically used because yaws is closely related to the bacterium that causes syphilis and the two diseases will show the same result. Treatment: There is currently no vaccine for yaws. Health education and improved personal hygiene are the most important tactics for prevention. Two antibiotics can be used to treat yaws. A single oral dose of azithromycin (30 mg/kg, maximum 2 g) is the recommended treatment. Benzathine penicillin (single intramuscular dose) at 0.6 million units (children aged under 10 years) and 1.2 million units (people aged over 10 years) can be used for patients with suspected clinical treatment failure after azithromycin, or patients who cannot be treated with azithromycin.
translations.reference: cutar fata (Endemic treponematoses) Taƙaitaccen bayani: cutar fata ta Yaws cutar fata ce mai tsananin gaske da ke da yanayi na kumburi da gyambo (ciwo). Bakteriyar da ake kira Treponema Pallidum ce ke haifar da wannan cutar fata wacce aka sanya ta cikin kashin baktariyar da ke haifar da sifilis da ake samu ta hanyar saduwa. cutar fata ta Yaws ta fi shafar yara 'yan ƙasa da shekaru 15 da ke rayuwa cikin talauci a wuri mai yanayin zafi a yankin Afirka da Asiya da Latin America da tsibirin Fasifik. Mafi yawan waɗanda ke kamuwa da cutar na rayuwa ne a ƙauyuka da ke da nisa da wuraren kiwon lafiya. Talauci da yanayi na rashin abin hannu da rashin tsabta ne ke taimakawa wajen yaɗuwar cutar fata ta Yaws. duk da akwai rahotanni 80000 na kamuwa da cutar fata ta Yaws a duk shekara, masana sun yi amanna cewa za a iya magance cutar da ma kawar da ita saboda da dalilai masu yawa. Da farko dai ana samun cutar ne a cikin bil'Adama ba dabba ba wanda ke nuna sauƙin magance cutar. Za a iya magance cutar cikin sauƙi ta hanyar amfani da magunguna da tuni akwai su kamar yadda aka yi nasarar kawar da cutar a wasu ƙasashe kamar Indiya. Wasu 'yan ƙalilan da ke da cutar na zaune a keɓantattun wurare wanda hakan ke nuna sauƙin daƙile yaɗuwarta idana aka yi amfani da matakai na bibiya da kulawa. Alamomin kamuwa: Akwai matakai biyu na kwamuwa da cutar fata ta Yaws. mataki na farko shi ne matakin da mai larurar ke ɗauke da cutar kuma zai iya yaɗawa ga sauran mutane. a wannan mataki a kan samu ɓullowar ƙurji (wanda bai shafi kansa ba da ke ɓullowa kamar ƙurji) wanda ke fara girma da zarar mutum ya kamu da shi. ƙurjin yana dauke da ƙwayar cutar da kuma kan ɗauki watanni 3-6 ana maganinsa. Idan kuma ba a ɗauki mataki na magani ba, yana cigaba da yaɗuwa ne a kan fatar zuwa sauran sassan jiki. ana iya fara jin raɗaɗi daga cikin ƙashi da musayar lafiyayyen ƙashi da wani. mataki na biyu shi ne matakin da cutar ba za ta iya yaɗuwa ba wanda hakan na faruwa ne kusan shekaru biyar da kamuwa da cutar. ana iya fayyace cutar da ƙandarewar hanci da ƙashi da tafin hannu/dunduniya (kaurara). bayan nazarin alamomin cutar, za a iya tabbatar da cutar ne kawai idan aka haska fatar da wata nau'in nau'irar ganin ƙwaƙwaf ta musamman (darkfield examination). ba cika amfani da gwajin jini ba saboda cutar fata ta yaws na da alaƙa da bakteriyar da ke haifar da cutar sifilis, don haka sakamako ɗaya za a samu na cututtukan biyu. Magani: A yanzu ba babu wata rigakafi ta cutar fata ta yaws. Ilimantarwa da inganta tsabtar jiki su ne muhimman dabarun da ake amfani da su na kariya. ana iya amfani da nau'o'in antibayotik guda biyu wajen maganin cutar fata ta yaws. an yi ba da shawarar amfani da maganin sha na azithromycin (30 mg/kg, maximum 2 g). za a iya amfani da maganin Benzathine penicillin (ta hanyar allura sau ɗaya) a kan waon 0.6 (ga yara 'yan ƙasa da shekaru 10) da awon 1.2 (ga yara 'yan sama da shekaru 10) ga marasa lafiya da ake zargin gazawar maganin da aka ɗora su a kai bayan azithromycin ko marasa lafiyar da laurarsu ta fi ƙarfin azithromycin.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the target audience (likely those with limited access to healthcare information). I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices and potential areas for further refinement.

**Yaws (Cutarsa) Bayani:** Cutarsa cuta ce mai tsanani wacce ke hasashen da ƙwayoyin papilloma (ƙwayoyin da ba su da cuta) da ƙura. Ta'arancin da ke haifar da cutar shi ne bakteriya mai suna *Treponema pallidum subspecies pertenue*, wacce ta kasance a cikin ƙungiyar bakteriyoyi da ke haifar da cutar sukiri. Cutarsa ta Yaws ta fi kasawa yara 'yan kasa da shekaru 15 da ke zaune a cikin al'ummomi maras yalwa a yankin Afirka, Asiya, Amurka ta Kudu da tsibirin Pacific, inda akwai iskar babu da zafi da kuma dazuzzaka. Yawancin mutanen da ake cutar da su suna zaune a kananan kauyuka, kuma suna nesa da ayyukan lafiya. Talauci, matsayin zamantakewa mai kasala da kuma wahalci a tsabtace jiki suna taimaka ma yaɗuwa da cutar. Albeit akwai sama da 80,000 cutawa kowace shekara, kwararru sun ce za'a iya kawace cutar kuma a kawar da ta gaba daya bisa dalilai da dama. Na farko, ta faru ne a mutane kawai, ba a dabbobi ba, wanda hakan ya sa kawace ta ta fi sauƙi. Hakanan, ta'arancin ne mai sauƙi don magance ta da magunguna da ke nan, kuma ta riga ta ajiye a wasu ƙasashe, gami da Indiya. Ragowar wurare da ke da cutar Yaws, ko da sun kasance a nisa, yana nuni akan yiwuwar yaɗuwa ta rage daidai da aiwatar da tsarin bincike da kawace cutar.

**Alamomin cuta:** Akwai matsayin biyu na cutar Yaws. Mataki na farko shi ne lokacin da mai cuta ke iya yaduwa da cutar ga sauran mutane. A wannan matakin, wata ƙwaya (ƙwaya mai ban tsoro, wacce ke fitowa daga jikin mutum) ke bayyana a wurin da aka kamu da cutar. Wannan ƙwayar tana cike da bakteriyoyi kuma na iya zama na watanni 3-6, sannan ta warƙewa ta kanta. Idan ba a yi magani ba, wannan ya biyo bayan ƙura a jikin mutum. Tsanowar ƙa'ida da ƙura a jiki ma sukan bayyana. Mataki na biyu bai iya yaduwa ba kuma yawanci ya bayyana shekaru biyar bayan kamuwa da cuta. Ya hasashen da cutarwar da ke hana a kunne, ƙa'ida da kuma ƙura mai ƙura a hannu da ƙafafu (ƙura mai ƙura). Bayan binciken lafiya bisa ga waɗannan alamomin, za'a iya tabbatar da cutar ta hanyar bincikar samfurin daga ƙura a karkashin kusan naɗin gani na musamman (binciken filin). Ba a tafi da gwajin jini sosai saboda cutar Yaws ta kusan shaidawa bakteriya da ke haifar da sukiri, kuma dukansu cutokin za su nuna sakamako iri ɗaya.

**Magani:** A halin da ake ciki, babu allurar rigakafin cutar Yaws. Taliqin lafiya da inganta tsabtace jiki shine mafi mahimmancin hanyoyin hana cuta. Za'a iya amfani da abubuwan da ke da alaka da kwayar cuta biyu don magance cutar Yaws. Ana bada shawarar maganin azithromycin (30 mg/kg, mafi yawan 2 g) a matsayin daya a baki. Benzathine penicillin (daga daya a zubar da shi a jiki) a adadin 0.6 million units (yara 'yan kasa da shekaru 10) da 1.2 million units (mutanen da ke da shekaru sama da 10) za'a iya amfani da su ga mutanen da ake shakka ne akan gaza magance cutar bayan azithromycin, ko mutanen da ba za'a iya magance su da azithromycin ba.

---

**Notes on the Translation & Potential Refinements:**

*   **Hausa Vocabulary:** I've tried to use common and easily understandable Hausa terms.  Some medical terms are inherently difficult to translate directly, so I've opted for explanations or approximations.
*   **"Yaws" Translation:** I used "Cutarsa" as a translation for "Yaws." This is a reasonable approximation, but it might be beneficial to consult with a Hausa medical professional to see if a more specific or established term exists.
*   **Clarity and Sentence Structure:** I've broken down some longer English sentences into shorter, more manageable ones for Hausa readers.
*   **Cultural Sensitivity:**  The language is respectful and avoids potentially stigmatizing terms.
*   **Technical Terms:** Terms like "papule," "hyperkeratosis," and "darkfield examination" are challenging. I've provided explanations where possible.  A glossary of terms might be helpful for a more technical audience.
*   **Verification:** **Crucially, this translation *needs* to be reviewed by a native Hausa speaker with medical knowledge.**  Nuance and accuracy are vital in medical translations.
*   **Dialect:** Hausa has different dialects. This translation aims for a generally understandable form, but regional variations might require adjustments.

**To help me improve the translation further, could you tell me:**

*   Who is the intended audience for this translation? (e.g., general public, healthcare workers, etc.)
*   Are there any specific terms or phrases that are particularly important to get right?
*   Is there a particular Hausa dialect that is preferred?

translations.source: This treatment will result in complete clinical healing in 95% of cases but patients should be re-examined 4 weeks after receiving the antibiotics. A review of the historic documents from 1950s shows that over 90 countries and territories were endemic for yaws. Only 14 out of the 90 countries and territories have recent data on yaws based on the routine surveillance system; however, these figures may just be an indication of the presence of the disease and not its full extent. Ghana, Papua New Guinea and the Solomon Islands report over 10 000 cases per year. Ecuador and India appear to have interrupted transmission.
translations.reference: wannan mataki na magani sai warkar kashi 95% na masu cutar, sai dai akwai buƙatar sake duba su bayan mako 4 da karɓar antibiyotik ɗin. wani bincike ya nuna bayanan da suka gabata daga 1950 cewa ƙasashe 90 da yankuna suna fama da cutar fata ta yaws. kuma 14 cikin 90 na ƙasashen ne ke da bayanan na kusa-kusa a kan cutar fata ta yaws idan aka yi la'akari da tsarin bibiyar cutar, duk da cewa waɗannan al'ƙaluma ka iya zama manuniya ce ta wanzuwar wannan cutar, sai dai an rage mata ƙarfi. Ƙasashen Ghana da Papua New Guineada Tsibirin Solomon sun tabbatar da cewa suna samun sama masu cutar 10000 a kowacce shekara. Ƙasashen Ecuado da India sun yi nasarar daƙile yaɗuwar cutar.

Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided English document, aiming for clarity and accuracy suitable for a medical context. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices I made.

**Hausa Translation:**

Wannan maganin zai haɗa cikakken warkewa a dan adadin kashi 95% na al’amura, amma ya zama dole a duba janyuci a karkashin kwanaki 4 bayan samun kwayar cuta. Binciken takardun tarihi daga shekarun 1950 ya nuna cewa sama da kasashe da yankuna 90 sun sami cutar yaws. Kasashe da yankuna 14 ne kawai daga cikin 90 suka da idan yawa a kan cutar yaws bisa ga tsarin binciken yawa; duk da haka, wannan adadi na iya zama alamar kasancewa kawai na cutar, ba kuma cikakken yawan gaske. Ghana, Papua New Guinea, da Tsibirin Solomon sun bayar da rahoton sama da kwayar cuta 10,000 a kowace shekara. Ecuador da Indiya sun bayyana an katse yaduwar cutar.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **"Treatment"**: Translated as "Maganin" (the medicine/treatment).
*   **"Clinical healing"**: Translated as "Warkewa" (healing/recovery).  I used "cikakken warkewa" to emphasize "complete" healing.
*   **"Re-examined"**: Translated as "duba janyuci" (examine again/re-examine).
*   **"Historic documents"**: Translated as "Takardun tarihi" (historical documents).
*   **"Endemic"**: Translated as "sami cutar" (to have the disease/be endemic to). This is a common way to express endemicity in Hausa.
*   **"Routine surveillance system"**: Translated as "tsarin binciken yawa" (routine surveillance system).
*   **"Indication of the presence"**: Translated as "alamar kasancewa" (sign of presence).
*   **"Full extent"**: Translated as "cikakken yawan gaske" (complete true amount/full extent).
*   **"Report"**: Translated as "bayar da rahoton" (to give a report/report).
*   **"Interrupted transmission"**: Translated as "an katse yaduwar cutar" (the transmission of the disease has been cut/interrupted).

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Medical terminology can be very specific. If this translation is for a specific medical guideline or patient education material, it might be necessary to consult with a Hausa-speaking medical professional to ensure the most accurate and culturally appropriate wording.
*   **Terminology Consistency:**  For ongoing translation projects, it's crucial to develop a glossary of Hausa terms for common medical concepts to ensure consistency.

translations.source: Yellow fever Overview: Yellow fever is a viral disease that is transmitted to humans by the bites of infected mosquitoes. It is prone to epidemics and is preventable with a vaccine. These day-biting mosquitoes breed around houses (domestic), in forests or jungles (wild), or in both habitats (semi-domestic). Yellow fever is a high-impact, high-threat disease, with a risk of international spread, representing a potential threat to global health security. There are 3 types of transmission cycles: Sylvatic (or jungle) yellow fever spreads when monkeys are bitten by wild mosquitoes that pass the virus on to other monkeys and humans. Intermediate yellow fever spreads when semi-domestic mosquitoes infect both monkeys and people. This is the most common type of outbreak in Africa. Urban yellow fever can cause large epidemics when infected people introduce the virus into heavily populated areas with high mosquito density and where people have little immunity. Occasionally, infected travellers have exported cases to countries that are free of yellow fever. However, the disease can only spread easily to a new country if there are mosquito species able to transmit it, specific climatic conditions, and the animal reservoir needed to maintain it. Symptoms: Yellow fever can present with a wide range of symptoms and severity. Once contracted, the yellow fever virus incubates in the body for 3–6 days. Many people do not experience symptoms, but when these do occur, the most common are fever, muscle pain with prominent backache, headache, loss of appetite, and nausea or vomiting. In most cases, symptoms disappear after 3–4 days. A small percentage of patients enter a second, more toxic phase within 24 hours of recovering from initial symptoms. High fever returns and several body systems are affected, usually the liver and kidneys. In this phase, people are likely to develop jaundice (yellowing of the skin and eyes, hence the name yellow fever), dark urine, and abdominal pain with vomiting. Bleeding can occur from the mouth, nose, eyes, or stomach. Half of these patients die within 7–10 days.Yellow fever is difficult to diagnose, especially during the early stages. More severe cases can be confused with severe malaria, leptospirosis, viral hepatitis (especially fulminant forms), other haemorrhagic fevers, infection with other flaviviruses (such as dengue haemorrhagic fever) and poisoning. Polymerase chain reaction (PCR) testing in blood and urine can sometimes detect the virus in early stages of the disease. In later stages, testing to identify antibodies is needed (IgM). Treatment: Yellow fever infections can cause serious illness and can be fatal in severe cases. Early supportive treatment in hospitals improves survival rates.
translations.reference: Zazzaɓin masassara ko shawara Taƙaitaccen bayani: zazzaɓin masassara ko shawara cuta ce da 'yan Adam ke kamuwa da ita ta hanayr ciwon sauri. akwai yuwuwar yaɗuwarsa kuma ana iya magance ta ta hanyar rigakafi. waɗannan sauraye da ke cizo na iya hayayyafa a kusa da muhallan mutane (domestic) da dazuka (wild) da kuma duka muhallan biyu (semi-domestic). masassara ko shawara zazzaɓi ne mai tasiri kuma cuta mai illar gaske da ke da yuwuwar yaɗuwa a duniya wanda ake kallo a matsayin barazana ga kiwon lafiya a duniya. akwai hanyoyi 3 na hanyoyin yaɗuwar cutar: Sylvatic; cutar na yaɗuwa ne ta hanyar ciwon da sauraye ke yi wa birrai wanda daga nan ne suke yaɗa wa sauran birrai da mutane. zazzaɓin masassara ko shawara tsakanin halittu biyu na yaɗuwa ne a lokacin da sauraye suka harbi birrai da mutane. wannan nau'i ne na cuta ake yawan samun ɓarkewarsa a yankin Afirka. Zazzaɓin masassara na iya yaɗuwa a birane idan mutanen da suka harbu tare da yaɗa ta a wuraren da ke da yawan sauraye a kuma muhallin da mutane ke da ƙarancin kariya daga cutar. a wasu lokutan matafiya ne yaɗa wannan zazzaɓi na masassara a ƙasashen da ba su da ita. kodayake, ƙwayar cutar na yaɗuwa cikin sauƙi ne a ƙasashen da ke da nau'in wannan sauro wanda ke iya yaɗa cutar, ko wani yanayi da kuma yawan dabbobin da ke iya ajiye ƙwayar cutar. Alamomin kamuwa da cutar: zazzaɓin masassara na da alamomin mabambanta. da zarar mutum ya kamu, ƙwayar cutar na ƙyanƙyashewa a jikinsa ne tsakanin kwanaki 3-6. Wasu mutane ba za sa jin alamun kamuwa, amma idan hakan ta faru, mafi yawan alamun da mutum ke ji shi ne zazzaɓi, zugi tare da ciwon baya da ciwon kai da rashin son cin abinci da jin hararwa ko yin amai. A mafi yawancin lokuta akan nemi alamun zazzaɓin a rasa bayan kwanaki 3-4. wasu daga cikin marasa lafiya suna shiga mataki na biyu kuma mafi haɗari bayan awanni 24 daga daina jin alamomin farko. Zazzaɓi mai zafi na dawowa da kuma ke shafar sassan jiki da dama kamar hanta da ƙodoji. A wannan mataki, akwai yuwuwar mutane su fuskanci sauyin launi a wani sashe na jiki (sauyawar launin fata da ƙwayar idanu zuwa launin rawaya wanda hakan ya sa ake kiran cutar da 'yellow fever') da bawali mai launin baƙi da ciwon ciki da hararwa. Ana iya samun zubar jini daga baki da hanci da idanu ko ciki. Rabin waɗannan marasa lafiya sun mutu a cikin kwananki 7-10.cutar zazzaɓin yana da wuyar ganewa,musamman a lokacin farkon matakan. Za'a iya rikitar da lamura masu tsanani tare da matsanancin zazzabin cizon sauro, leptospirosis, hepatitis na kwayar cuta (musamman nau'ikan fulminant), sauran zazzabi na haemorrhagic, kamuwa da wasu flaviviruses (kamar zazzabin haemorrhagic na dengue) da guba. Gwajin polymerase{PCR} a cikin jini da fitsari wani lokaci na gano ƙwayar cutar a farkon matakan cutar. A cikin matakai na gaba, ana buƙatar gwaji don gano ƙwayoyin cutar (IgM). Matakin magance cutar:kamuwa da zazzaɓin masassara na iya haifar da mummunar cuta kuma na iya zama mai kisa a lokuta masu tsanani. tallafin magani na farko a asibitoci yana inganta ƙimar rayuwa.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices and potential areas for further refinement.

**Hausa Translation:**

**Gaɓoɓin cutar nan Rabin Ganye (Yellow Fever):** Cutar nan Rabin Ganye wata cuta ce da ke faruwa ta hanyar yaƙin ƙirjin ruwan sama mai cuta. Tana iya zama cuta mai yaduwa sosai (epidemic) kuma za'a iya hana ta ta amfani da allura. Waɗannan ƙirjin ruwan sama da ke yiƙin a rana suna haɓaka kusa da gida (a cikin gida), a daji ko kuma a makogwaro (a waje), ko kuma a duka wurare biyu (a tsakance). Cutar nan Rabin Ganye babban cuta ce mai tasiri kuma mai barazuba, tana da haɗarin yaduwa a duniya, wacce ke haɓaka barazanar lafiyar duniya. Akwai ragama biyu na yaduwar cutar:

*   **Ragama Daji (Sylvatic):** Cutar nan Rabin Ganye tana yaduwa lokacin da ƙungiyoyi ke yiƙin ta ƙirjin ruwan sama a daji, waɗanda ke kaiwa cutar zuwa ga ƙungiyoyin da sauran mutane.
*   **Ragama Tsaka (Intermediate):** Cutar nan Rabin Ganye tana yaduwa lokacin da ƙirjin ruwan sama ke yiƙin da ke haɗawa da ƙungiyoyi da mutane. Wannan ita ce mafi yawan cuta da ke faruwa a Afirka.
*   **Ragama Birni (Urban):** Cutar nan Rabin Ganye na iya haifar da cuta mai yaduwa sosai a birane lokacin da mutanen da ke da cutar ke kawo cutar zuwa wurare da yawa da ƙirjin ruwan sama da yawa, kuma mutane ba sa da matsakaicin juriya. A wasu lokuta, mutanen da ke da cuta sun kai cutar zuwa ƙasashe da ba su da cutar nan Rabin Ganye. Koyawa, cutar nan Rabin Ganye na iya yaduwa cikin sauƙi zuwa sabuwar ƙasa idan akwai ƙirjin ruwan sama da na iya yaduwa da ita, yanayi na musamman, da kuma wuraren da ke da ƙungiyoyi don ci gaba da ita.

**Alamomin cuta:** Cutar nan Rabin Ganye na iya bayyana da alamomin da dama da tsanawakin bamban-bamban. Da zarar an kamu da cutar, ƙirjin ruwan sama na jinyashi a jiki na tsawon kwanaki 3-6. Mutane da yawa ba sa jin alamomin, amma lokacin da alamomin ke faruwa, mafi yawan su su ne zubar da ruwan jiki, tsanani a jiki tare da ciwon baya, ciwon kai, rasa sha'awar abinci, da ciwon tsiwo ko rashin tsiwo. A mafi yawan lokuta, alamomin sun ɓace bayan kwanaki 3-4. Kananan kashi na mutane shiga mataki na biyu, mai tsanowar gaske a cikin sa'o'i 24 na farfadowa daga alamomin farko. Zubar da ruwan jiki yana dawo da shi kuma jikin mutum yana shafar jikin jiki da yawa, galibi jini da koda. A wannan matakin, mutane suna da ganganin samun launin kulli (launin launin launi da ido, don haka an fi sani cutar nan Rabin Ganye), pee mai duzuwa, da ciwon jiki tare da rashin tsiwo. Jini na iya fitowa daga bakin, baki, ido, ko makogwaro. Kashi biyu na mutane suna mutuwar a cikin kwanaki 7-10. Cutar nan Rabin Ganye yana da wahala a gano, musamman a matsayin farko. Lamarin cuta na iya zama ruwan tabarau da cutar malaria mai tsanani, leptospirosis, hepatitis mai cutewa (musamman matsayin tsanowar gaske), sauran cutar jini, cutar da sauran flaviviruses (kamar cutar jini ta dengue) da guba. Ayyakar PCR a jini da pee na iya gano cutar a matsayin farko. A matsayin gaba, gwajin gano kwayoyin jijiya yana buƙatar (IgM).

**Magani:** Cutar nan Rabin Ganye na iya haifar da cuta mai tsanani kuma na iya zama mai mutuwar a matsayin cuta mai tsanani. Magani na gaba da gaba a asibiti yana bunkasa yawan mutane.

**Notes on the Translation:**

*   **Terminology:** I've tried to use standard Hausa medical terminology where possible.  "Cutar nan Rabin Ganye" is the established term for Yellow Fever.
*   **Clarity and Flow:** I've restructured some sentences to ensure they flow naturally in Hausa.  Direct, word-for-word translation can often sound awkward.
*   **Cultural Context:**  I've considered how concepts like "domestic" and "wild" habitats might be understood in a Hausa context.
*   **Technical Terms:**  I've attempted to translate technical terms accurately, but some might require further review by a medical professional familiar with Hausa. For example, "polymerase chain reaction (PCR)" is a tricky one, and the translation might need refinement.
*   **Dialect:** This translation aims for a generally understandable Hausa, but regional dialects might have slightly different preferred terms.
*   **Accuracy:** While I've strived for accuracy, it's *crucial* that this translation be reviewed by a native Hausa speaker with medical expertise to ensure it's completely accurate and appropriate for its intended audience.

**To help me improve the translation further, could you tell me:**

*   What is the intended audience for this translation? (e.g., general public, healthcare workers)
*   Are there any specific terms or phrases that are particularly important to get right?
*   Is there a specific Hausa dialect that is preferred?

translations.source: There is currently no specific anti-viral drug for yellow fever, but specific care to treat dehydration, fever, and liver and kidney failure improves outcomes. Associated bacterial infections can be treated with antibiotics. Patients need to stay under mosquito nets during the day to limit the risk of spreading to others through mosquito bites. Vaccination is the single most important measure for preventing yellow fever. The prevention of outbreaks can only be achieved if the majority of the population is immunized. The yellow fever vaccine is safe and affordable, and a single dose provides life-long immunity against the disease. People who recover from yellow fever infection are also protected for life. Read more about the yellow fever vaccine. Mosquito control can also help to prevent yellow fever and is vital in situations where vaccination coverage is low or the vaccine is not immediately available. Mosquito control includes eliminating sites where mosquitoes can breed and killing adult mosquitoes and larvae by using insecticides in areas with high mosquito density. Community involvement through activities, such as cleaning household drains and covering water containers where mosquitoes can breed is an important and effective way to control mosquitoes.
translations.reference: A halin yanzu babu wani takamaiman maganin rigakafin ƙwayar cuta don zazzaɓi, amma takamaiman kulawa don magance rashin isashshen ruwa a jiki, zazzabi, da gazawar hanta da koda yana inganta sakamako. Ana iya magance ƙwayoyin cuta masu alaƙa da maganin rigakafi. Marasa lafiya suna buƙatar zama a gidan sauro da rana don irage haɗarin yadawa ga wasu ta hanyar cizon sauro. Alurar riga kafi ita ce hanya mafi mahimmanci don hana zazzabi. Za a iya hana barkewar cutar ne kawai idan an yiwa yawancin jama'a allurar rigakafi. Maganin cutar zazzabin shawara yana da aminci kuma mai araha, kuma kashi ɗaya na alluran rigakafi yana ba da rigakafi na tsawon rayuwa daga cutar. Mutanen da suka warke daga kamuwa da cutar zazzaɓi kuma suna da kariya har tsawon rayuwars. ƙara karantawa game da allurar rigakafin zazzaɓin cizon sauro. Kula da sauro na iya taimakawa wajen hana zazzabin rawaya kuma yana da mahimmanci a cikin yanayin da allurar rigakafi ba ta da yawa ko kuma ba a samun rigakafin nan da nan. Kula da sauro ya haɗa da kawar da wuraren da sauro ke hayayyafa da kashe manya sauro da tsutsa ta hanyar amfani da maganin kashe kwari a wuraren da ke da yawan sauro. Shigar da al'umma ta hanyar ayyuka, kamar tsaftace magudanar ruwa da kuma rufe kwantena na ruwa inda sauro ke iya hayayyafa hanya ce mai mahimmanci kuma mai inganci don magance sauro.

Cututtukan kwayar cuta da ke haɗuwa da cutar za a yi magani da antibiotics.  Mata da maza sun bukaci su zauna ƙarƙashin gwarzuwa a rana domin kawar da haɗarin yaduwar cutar ta hanyar zafin ƙungiyar.  Tafiya da allura shine mafi mahimmancin mataki don hana yaduwar cutar da jikin mutum ta kankara.  Hanya daya tilo don hana yaduwar cutar ta jikin mutum ta kankara shi ne idan yawancin mutane sun yi allura. Allurar kankara ta jikin mutum ta kankara ta amincika kuma ta kasashe, kuma daya allura na ba da kariya a rayuwar mutum daga cutar.  Mutanen da suka warke daga cutar da jikin mutum ta kankara suma sun kasance kariya a rayuwar mutum. Karanta karin bayani game da allurar kankara ta jikin mutum ta kankara.  Kwamantar da zafin ƙungiyar kuma na iya taimakawa wajen hana yaduwar cutar da jikin mutum ta kankara kuma yana da mahimmanci a yanayi inda kwarewar allura ta kasance ƙasa da ƙarfi ko allurar ba ta nan kai tsaye.  Kwamantar da zafin ƙungiyar ya haɗa da kawar da wurare da zafin ƙungiyar ke haifuwa da kashe zafin ƙungiyar mazaunan da larvae ta amfani da insecticides a wurare da ke da yawan zafin ƙungiyar.  Kungiyoyin jama'a ta hanyar ayyukan, kamar a tsaftace magudan rago a gida da rufe kwano da ruwa inda zafin ƙungiyar ke haifuwa, ya zama hanyar da ta fi amsa da kuma ta fi tasiri don kwantar da zafin ƙungiyar.

**Pronunciation Guide (approximate - Hausa has tones that are difficult to represent in text):**

translations.source: People with medical needs are “left behind in pain” reveals new report The World Health Organization (WHO) has published a new report on access to morphine for medical use, which describes how the global distribution of morphine, as a vital pain medicine, is unequal and does not fulfil the medical need. The report, titled “Left behind in pain”, highlights the problems in access to this essential medicine and offers actions to improve safe access through balanced policy. Despite morphine being an effective and relatively low-cost medicine for relieving strong pain, listed since 1977 in the first edition of the WHO Model list of Essential Medicines, the disparity in access across countries is stark. There is a 5- to 63-fold difference in the estimated median consumption of morphine between high-income countries and lower income countries. Consumption pattern varies significantly across countries of similar wealth; it does not correspond to medical need, as indicated by the estimated number of days people are in pain or suffer with severe shortness of breath for people with a terminal illness. This data echoes that of the 2018 Lancet Commission which described the lack of access to pain relief medication as ‘one of the most heinous, hidden inequities in global health’ with the richest 10% of countries possessing 90% of distributed morphine-equivalent opioids. “Leaving people in pain when effective medicines are available for pain management, especially in the context of end-of-life care, should be a cause of serious concern for policy-makers,” says Dr Yukiko Nakatani, WHO Assistant Director-General for Medicines and Health Products. “We must urgently advocate for safe and timely access to morphine for those in medical need through balanced policy, everywhere.” Reasons for the disparity Access to morphine for medical use is influenced by many interacting factors including enablers related to good governance, reliable/efficient procurement and supply processes, resource availability and capacity-building activities, along with barriers related to overly restrictive legislation and policies, inadequate service provision, and misinformed attitudes and perception. Given different country contexts, enablers, barriers and priorities for action vary; a stakeholder survey presented in this report, with respondents from 105 WHO Member States, confirms this variation. For example, irregular supply of morphine and other strong opioids at health facilities due to limited financing were commonly noted as a barrier in low- and middle-income countries compared to high-income countries. More than a third of respondents across all regions also noted barriers arising from legislative and regulatory factors while recognizing the importance of a legislative and regulatory framework in achieving safe access. Unduly restrictive requirements could hamper patient access because they impede the flow of supply or make prescribing and dispensing difficult for health-care professionals. It is important to note that concerns about the potential harmful effects of opioids are valid, such as their potential to lead to opioid use disorder. For this reason, a certain amount of caution about the potential harms of opioid use (e.g. use in chronic non-cancer pain) is important for public health insofar as its use is well-informed and proportionate to risks. Such concerns should not undermine the benefits of opioid use when clinically indicated and when used safely by trained professionals. Opportunities for actionThe report presents a complementary set of areas for action that aim to improve safe access to morphine. These include implementing small-scale regional or state-wide programmes on improving access to morphine for medical use with a package of essential services and products for palliative care that is formulated according to the WHO Model List of Essential Medicines and the WHO Essential Package of Palliative Care. They also include improving governance, streamlining procurement and supply processes to address any inefficiency, improving resourcing such as stable funding, enhancing competent skill sets of the health workforce, and raising awareness about both the benefits and potential harms of opioid use. The success of these actions will hinge on collaboration and cooperation among all stakeholders at national, regional, and global levels.
translations.reference: An bar mutanen da ke da buƙatar kulawar likita a baya cikin raɗaɗi, sabon rahoto ya bayyana. Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) ta fitar da wani sabon rahoto kan samun damar yin amfani da sinadarin morphine a matsayin magani, wanda ya bayyana yadda rarraba morphine a duniya a matsayin maganin raɗaɗi mai mahimmanci, bai daidaita ba kuma baya biyan bukatun likita.B50. Rahoton, mai taken "cikin raɗaɗi", yana nuna matsalolin samun damar yin amfani da wannan magani mai mahimmanci tare da bayyana hanyoyin samar da shi cikin ta hanyar daidaitattun manufofi. Duk da cewa morphine magani ne mai inganci kuma mai sauƙin farashi don kawar da raɗaɗi mai ƙarfi, wanda aka jera tun 1977 a cikin wallafar WHO ta farko na jerin samfuran Mahimman Magunguna, rarrabuwar kai a cikin ƙasashe yana da ƙarfi. Akwai bambanci ninki 5 zuwa 63 a kiyasin matsakaicin yawan amfani da morphine tsakanin ƙasashe masu samun kuɗin shiga da ƙasashe masu ƙaramin karfi. Yanayin amfani ya bambanta sosai a cikin ƙasashe masu irin wannan arziki; Bai dace da buƙatun kiwon lafiya ba, kamar yadda ƙiyasin adadin kwanakin da mutane ke fama da raɗaɗi ko fama da matsanancin ƙarancin numfashi ga masu fama da rashin lafiyan da ba na tashi ba. Wadannan bayanan sun yi daidai da na Hukumar Lancet ta 2018 wacce ta bayyana rashin samun damar yin amfani da magungunan rage jin zafi a matsayin daya daga cikin rashin adalci mafi muni, a harkar kiwon lafiya ta duniya tare da 10% mafiya arzikin ƙasashe da ke daukar kashi 90% na morphine da aka rarraba. daidai opioids. Barin mutane cikin raɗaɗi a lokacin da ake samun ingantattun magunguna don magance raɗaɗin ciwo, musamman a yanayin kula da lafiya na karshen rayuwa, ya kamata ya zama abin damuwa ga masu tsara manufofi, in ji Dokta Yukiko Nakatani, Mataimakin Daraktan WHO. -Gabaɗaya don Magunguna da Kayayyakin Lafiya. Dole ne mu yi kira da gaggawa a samar da morphine kan lokaci ga waɗanda ke cikin buƙatun likita ta hanyar madaidaitan manufofi, a ko'ina. Dalilan banbance-banbance Damar samun morphine a matsayin magani yana da alaƙa da abubuwa da dama da suka haɗa da shugabanci nagari, ingantaccen sayayya da hanyoyin samar da kayayyaki, wadatar albarkatu da ayyukan haɓaka iya aiki, tare da shamakain da ke da alaƙa da ƙaƙƙarfan dokoki da manufofi, ƙarancin bayar da kulawa, da rashin fahimtar halaye da fahimta. Duba da yanayin ƙasashe daban-daban, abubuwan da ke samar da dama da tsaiko da fifiko wajen mahimmancin ɗaukar mataki sun bambanta; wani binciken masu ruwa da tsaki da aka gabatar a wannan rahoto, mai masu amsa tambayoyi daga mambobin ƙasashen WHO guda 105, ya tabbatar da wannan bambancin. Misali, ana ɗaukar rashin samar da sinadarin morphine da sauran magungunan opioid masu karfi a wuraren kiwon lafiya saboda ƙarancin kuɗaɗe a matsayin wani shamakai a ƙasashe masu ƙaramin karfi da matsakaicin kuɗin shiga idan aka kwatanta da ƙasashe masu tasowa. Fiye da kashi ɗaya bisa uku na waɗanda suka amsa tambayoyi a duk faɗin yankuna sun bayyana cewa dalilan dake kawo cikas sun shafi dokoki da ƙa'idoji, a yayin da suka fahimci muhimmancin tsarin doka da ƙa'ida wajen samar da dama cikin aminci. ƙayyadaddun buƙatu na babu gaira babu dalili na iya kawo cikas ga majiyyaci wajen samun dama saboda suna hana samarwa ko rubutawa da rarrabawa ga ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya. Yana da kyau a lura cewa damuwa game da yuwuwar illolin magungunan opioid abu ne mai tushe, kamar yuwuwar su na haifar da matsalolin dakr da alaƙa da amfani da opioids. Saboda wannan dalili, ɗaukan wani mataki na taka tsantsan game da yuwuwar cutarwar amfani da opioid (misali amfani a cikin ciwon daji na yau da kullun ba tare da ciwon daji ba) yana da mahimmanci ga lafiyar jama'a matuƙar an san amfaninsa daidai yake da haɗarinsa. Kada ire-iren waɗannan damuwa su lalata fa'idojin amfani da magungunan opioid lokacin da lafiya ta buƙaci hakan da lokacin amfani da su ta hanyar ƙwararrun ma'aikata. Damar daukar mataki: Rahoton ya gabatar da wasu jerin ɓangarorin ɗaukar matakai da nufin inganta damar samun morphine. Waɗannan sun haɗa da aiwatar da ƙananan shirye-shirye na yankuna da na jihohi kan haɓaka damar yin amfani da morphine wajen kiwon lafiya tare da mahimman ayyuka da kayayyaki don kulawa da jin daɗi, wanda aka tsara akan Jerin Kayayyakin WHO na Mahimman Magunguna da Muhimman Ƙunshin Lafiya na Kulawar WHO. Har ila yau, sun haɗa da inganta tsarin mulki, daidaita sayayya da hanyoyin samar da kayayyaki don magance duk wani tasgaro, inganta kayan aiki kamar samar da kuɗaɗe, samar da ƙwarewa a ma'aikatar kiwon lafiya, da wayar da kan jama'a game da fa'idodi da illar amfani da opioid. Nasarar waɗannan ayyukan za ta ta'allaƙa ne kan haɗin gwiwa da haɗin kai tsakanin dukkan masu ruwa da tsaki a matakan ƙasa, yanki da duniya baki ɗaya.

Rahoton, mai taken “Kame a cikin ciwon”, ya nuna matsala a kan hanyoyin samun wannan maganin da ke buɗewa kuma ya ba da ayyukan don bunkasar hanyoyin samun magani cikin koshin lafiya ta hanyar manufofin da suka dace.

Kodayke morphine magani ne mai tasiri da kuma kadan farfajarsa don kawar da ciwon da ke da ƙarfi, an jera shi tun shekarar 1977 a editan farko na Jerin Magunguna na WHO, kewayance a kan hanyoyin samunsa a tsakanin kasashe ya bayyana. Akwai bambance-bambance na 5 zuwa 63 a cikin adadin morphine da ake ƙididdigar amfani da shi a tsakanin kasashe masu kudin da ya kai kima da kasashe da ke da kudin da bai kai kima ba. Ayyukan amfani da magani sun bambanta sosai a tsakanin kasashe da ke da kudin da kusan iri ɗaya; bai biyo bayan buƙatar lafiya ba, kamar yadda adadin kwanakin mutane da ke cikin ciwo ko kuma suna fama da wahala sosai a numfashi ga mutanen da ke da cuta mai tsanowa ya nuna. Wannan bayanin ya jaddada abin da kwamitin Lancet na 2018 ya bayyana, wanda ya bayyana rashin samun magani don kawar da ciwo a matsayin 'dayan manyan abubuwan da ba a tabbarar da su ba a lafiyar duniya,' tare da kasashe 10% mafi kudin da suka mallaka kashi 90% na morphine-equivalent opioids da aka rarraba.

"Barin mutane a cikin ciwo lokacin da akwai magunguna da suka fi kiyayyar kawar da ciwo, musamman a cikin yanayin kula da mutanen da ke ƙarshen rayuwarsu, ya kamata ya zama abin damuwa ga mazaɓɓun gwamnati," in ji Dr Yukiko Nakatani, mataimakiyar babban sakataren WHO kan Magunguna da Kayayyakin Lafiya. "Muna bukatar da gaggiyar neman hanyoyin samun morphine ga mutanen da ke buƙatar lafiya ta hanyar manufofin da suka dace, a ko'ina."

**Dalilan bambance-bambance**

Hanyoyin samun morphine don amfanin lafiya yana shafar abubuwan da suka haɗu da yawa, gami da abubuwan da ke da alaka da daidaito na mulki, tsarin siye da samar da abubuwa mai amsa/efisiyenta, samun kariya da kuma ƙarawar ƙarfi, da kuma ƙayyadaddun dokoki da manufofin, ƙarancin hidima, da kuma ra'ayoyi da fahimmai mara kyau. Dangane da yanayin kasashe daban-daban, abubuwan da ke ba da taimako, ƙayyadaddun dokoki da manufofin, da kuma jefa da al'amuran aiki ya bambanta; binciken masu ruwa da suka haɗa kai a wannan rahoton, tare da amintattun mutane daga kasashe 155 na WHO, ya tabbatar da wannan bambance-bambancen. Alalokacin, samar da morphine da sauran opioids na ƙarfi a asibitai saboda kawar da kudin da za'a siyo da shi, yawanci an lura da shi a matsayin ƙayyadaddun dokoki a kasashe da ke da kudin da bai kai kima ba, idan aka kwatanta da kasashe da ke da kudin da ya kai kima ba. Fiye da mutane uku a kowace yankun kasa, sun lura da ƙayyadaddun dokoki da dokokin da ke hana samun magani, yayin da suke girmama mahimmancin dokokin da ke ba da izinin samun magani cikin koshin lafiya. Takamaqar da ƙa'idoji na iya hamadantar da samun magani ga marasa lafiya saboda yana hana samar da magani ko kuma ya sa babban likita ya yiwa marasa lafiya magani da kuma bayar da magani. Ya zama dole a lura cewa, damuwa game da tasirin cutarwa na opioids na da kyau, kamar yadda hakan na iya haifar da cutar da ake yiwa opioids. Don wannan dalili, ya zama dole a yi takaici game da yiwuwar cutarwa da ake samu ta amfani da opioids (misali, amfani a cikin cutar da ba ta da kansa), har zuwa lokacin da amfani da magani ya zama mai kyau da kuma yayi daidaito da haɗin gaske. Irin wannan damuwarsa bai kamata ya ɓata fa'idar amfani da opioids ba lokacin da ya cancanta a kan ilimi da kuma amfani da shi cikin koshin lafiya ta hanyar ƙwararrun likitoci.

**Kwarewar aiki**

Rahoton ya gabatar da jerin abubuwan aiki waɗanda ke daidaita juyi don bunkasar hanyoyin samun morphine cikin koshin lafiya. Waɗannan sun haɗa da aiwatar da shirye-shiryen ƙananan yanki ko jihar don bunkasar hanyoyin samun morphine don amfanin lafiya tare da kunshin ayyukan da ke buɗewa da abubuwan da suka dace don kula da mutane da ke ƙarshen rayuwarsu, waɗanda aka shirya su bisa ga Jerin Magunguna na WHO da Kayan Aikin Kula da Lafiya na WHO. Suna haɗawa da bunkasar daidaito na mulki, da tsarin siye da samar da abubuwa don warware duk wani rashin efisiyenta, da bunkasar kariya, da ƙarawar ƙarfi na ƙwararrun likitoci, da kuma ƙara wayar da kan jama'a game da fa'idar da za'a samu ta amfani da opioids da kuma haɗin gaske da za'a samu. Nasarar waɗannan ayyukan zai dogara ne akan hadin gwiwa da taimakon dukkanin masu ruwa da suka haɗa kai a matakin kasa, yanki, da duniya.

---

**Notes on the Translation:**

*   **Terminology:** I've tried to use common Hausa medical terms. However, some terms might need further refinement based on specific regional dialects or the target audience's level of medical knowledge.
*   **Sentence Structure:** I've adjusted sentence structures to flow naturally in Hausa, which sometimes requires rephrasing to maintain accuracy.
*   **Cultural Sensitivity:** I've been mindful of cultural nuances and avoided phrasing that could be misinterpreted.
*   **"Opioid Use Disorder":** This is a complex term. I've translated it as "cutar da ake yiwa opioids" which is a reasonable approximation, but a more precise term might be needed depending on the context.
*   **"Balanced Policy":** I used "manufofin da suka dace" which means "appropriate policies."
*   **Review:** It's *highly recommended* that a native Hausa speaker with medical knowledge review this translation for accuracy and clarity.

translations.source: Small islands agree bold plan of action on noncommunicable diseases and mental health Today, the 2023 Bridgetown Declaration on Noncommunicable Diseases (NCDs) and Mental Health was launched, to address some of the world’s deadliest diseases in Small Island Developing States (SIDS), which are especially at risk. The Declaration is a key outcome of the SIDS Ministerial Conference on Noncommunicable Diseases (NCDs) and Mental Health, running from 14 to 16 June, co-hosted by the World Health Organization (WHO), the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO) and the Government of Barbados. SIDS are disproportionately impacted by NCDs – which cause 74% of all deaths globally – due to their reliance on imported food, commercial influences and the climate crisis. A new WHO report shows that 8 of the 15 countries with more than a 30% risk of premature death from cardiovascular disease, cancer, diabetes, or chronic respiratory disease are SIDS. The 10 countries with the highest obesity rates globally are all SIDS in the Pacific, where over 45% of adults live with obesity. Mental health conditions are common in SIDS countries, affecting an estimated 15.2% of the population in the Caribbean and 11.2% of the population in the Pacific. People with mental health conditions face a higher risk of premature mortality, including from unaddressed physical health conditions and from suicide. Stigma, specialized staff shortages and the impact of climate change contribute to a challenging situation in SIDS that requires immediate attention. The 2023 Bridgetown Declaration outlines bold steps to address the range of social, environmental, economic and commercial issues that lead to NCDs and mental health conditions. Developed through an inclusive process led ‘for’ and ‘by’ SIDS, the declaration highlights that NCDs and mental health conditions cannot be properly addressed without responding to the climate crisis. “Bold action for our climate, good health, and well-being relies on redressing and reorganizing global financing to unlock billions in investment, while making it less punishing for developing countries to pay their debts,” said Her Excellency Mia Amor Mottley, Prime Minister of Barbados. “Funding for climate change adaptation and mitigation in the most vulnerable countries is also key, with noncommunicable diseases and mental health accounted for.” "The challenges faced by Small Island Developing States are interconnected and multifaceted. Climate change, environmental degradation, social and economic inequalities, and the commercial determinants of health exacerbate the burden of NCDs and mental health conditions," said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. "Despite their limited resources and geographical constraints, these nations have shown remarkable resilience in the face of adversity. Together, we must forge a path that is based on equity, resilience, and sustainability. We must strengthen health systems, enhance prevention and early detection measures, and prioritize the integration of NCD and mental health services into primary health care. "The Director-General also pledged that WHO would work to mobilize financial resources to develop climate resilient, environmentally sustainable health-care facilities in the SIDS. WHO will also continue to advocate for ‘loss and damage’ funding for climate change adaptation and mitigation investments in lower income countries. As part of agreeing the Declaration, SIDS signed up to undertake a suite of actions, covering areas such as engaging all of society, accelerating implementation, securing investments, aligning across governments and accounting for progress. The conference also showcased specific commitments by SIDS on NCDs and mental health, including to improve access to mental health care in primary care facilities, update tobacco control legislation to cover e-cigarettes and vaping, and reduce populations’ salt and sugar intake. These commitments align with WHO’s ‘best buys’ – a set of cost-effective, evidence-based interventions to tackle NCDs. The list of best buys was recently updated at the World Health Assembly in May to include secondary prevention for rheumatic fever, acute and long-term management of asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease as well as several interventions for cervical, breast, colorectal, liver and childhood cancer, and the comprehensive treatment of cancer for those living with HIV. “SIDS in the Americas remain overwhelmingly vulnerable to NCDs.
translations.reference: Ƙananan tsibirai sun amince kan babban shirin ayyuka kan cututtukan da ba sa yaɗuwa da cututtukan ƙwaƙwalwa. A yau, an ƙaddamar da sanarwar Bridgetown na 2023 akan Cututtukan da ba sa yaɗuwa (NCDs) da Lafiyar Ƙwaƙwalwa, don magance wasu cututtukan masu tsanani a duniya a cikin Ƙananan Tsibirai masu tasowa (SIDS), musamman waɗanda ke cikin haɗari. Sanarwar ita ce mahimmiyar sakamako na taron ministocin SIDS kan cututtukan da ba sa yaɗuwa (NCDs) da lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa, wanda ke gudana daga ranar 14 zuwa 16 ga Yuni, wanda Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO), da kuma Pan American Health Organisation (PAHO) suka shirya. Gwamnatin Barbados. NCDs na da matuƙar tasiri ga SIDS, wanda hakan ke haifar da kashi 74% na duk mace-mace a duniya saboda dogaro da abincin da ake shigowa da su daga waje, tasirin kasuwanci da kuma rikicewar yanayi. Wani sabon rahoton na WHO ya bayyana cewa 8 daga cikin ƙasashe 15 da ke da fiye da kashi 30 cikin dari na haɗarin mutuwa da wuri daga cututtukan zuciya, ciwon daji, ciwon sukari, ko cututtukan numfashi na yau da kullun sune SID. Ƙasashe 10 da ke da mafiya yawan masu fama da cutar ƙiba a duniya duk SID ne a cikin Pasifik, inda sama da kashi 45% na manya ke rayuwa da cutar ƙiba. Matsalolin lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa ya zama ruwan dare a cikin ƙasashen SIDS, wanda ya shafi kimanin kashi 15.2 na yawan jama'ar Caribbean da kashi 11.2 na yawan jama'ar Pasifik. Mutanen da ke fama da matsalar lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa suna fuskantar haɗarin mutuwa da wuri, rashin samun isasshen kulawa ta lafiyar jiki gami da kuma yunkurin kashe kawunansu. Abin kunya, ƙarancin ƙwararrun ma'aikata da tasirin sauyin yanayi suna ba da gudummawa wajen fuskantar ƙalubale a cikin SIDS wanda ke buƙatar kulawa da gaggawa. Sanarwar Bridgetown ta 2023 ta zayyana ƙwararan matakai don magance matsalolin zamantakewa, muhalli, tattalin arziki da kasuwanci waɗanda ke haifar da NCDs da yanayin lafiyar hankali. Ta hanyar gamammiyar tsari da SID ta jagoranta, sanarwar ta nuna cewa ba za a iya magance NCDs da matsalolin lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa yadda ya kamata ba, ba tare da magance rikicin yanayi ba. Ɗaukan ƙwararan matakai da suka dace a game da yanayi, lafiya da kuma walwalarmu, ya dogara ne kan gyaran tsarin tafiyar da kuɗaɗe a duniya, don samar da biliyoyin hanyoyin zuba hannun jari, da rage wa ƙasashe masu tasowa nauyin biyan basussukan da ake bin su. Firayim Ministan Barbados. Bayar da kuɗi ga ƙasashe masu rauni don daidaitawa da sauyin yanayi abu ne mai muhimmanci, tare da ƙididdige cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa da lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa. Ƙalubalen da Jihohin masu tasowa a Kananan Tsibirai ke fuskanta, suna da alaƙa da juna, da fuskoki daban-daban. Sauyin yanayi, lalacewar muhalli, rashin daidaiton zamantakewa da tattalin arziki, da kuma hanyoyin kasuwanci na kiwon lafiya suna ƙara tsananta nauyin NCDs da yanayin lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa," in ji Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Darakta-Janar na WHO. Duk da ƙarancin albarkatun da suke da shi da kuma matsalar yanki, wadannan al’ummomi sun nuna matukar jajircewa wajen fuskantar matsaloli. Tare, dole ne mu samar da hanyar da ta ginu akan daidaito, juriya, da ɗorewa. Dole ne mu ƙarfafa tsarin kiwon lafiya, haɓaka matakan kariya da ganowa da wuri, da kuma ba da fifiko ga haɗin gwiwar NCD da ayyukan kula da lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa a cikin kiwon lafiya na farko. Darakta-Janar ɗin ya kuma yi alƙawarin cewa WHO za ta yi aiki don tattara kuɗaɗe don bunƙasa yanayin juriya, wuraren kula da lafiya masu ɗorewa a cikin SIDS. WHO za ta kuma ci gaba da bayar da shawarwari don samar da kuɗaɗe na asara da lalacewa don daidaita canjin yanayi da rage saka hannun jari a ƙasashe masu ƙaramin karfi. A matsayin wani ɓangare na amincewa da sanarwar, SIDS ta sanya hannu don gudanar da ayyuka daban-daban, waɗanda suka shafi fannoni kamar haɗar da al'umma gabaɗaya, haɓaka aiwatarwa, tabbatar da saka hannun jari, daidaitawa tsakanin gwamnatoci da lissafin ci gaba. Taron ya kuma nuna takamaiman alƙawurra na SIDS akan NCDs da lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa, gami da inganta samun damar kula da lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa a wuraren kulawa na farko, sabunta dokokin sarrafa taba don rufe sigari da vaping, da rage yawan cin gishiri da sukari. Waɗannan alƙawuran sun yi daidai da mafi kyawun tsarin WHO masu sauƙin kuɗi, abubuwan da suka dogara da shaida don magance NCDs. A baya-bayan nan, an sabunta jerin mafiya kyawun sayayya a Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya a watan Mayu don haɗe ƙananan rigakafi ta cutar rheumatism, cikakkiyar kulawa da cutar numfashi mai tsanani da tsawo, cututtukan huhu, da kuma cututtukan kansar mahaifa, nono, colorectal, hanta da ciwon daji na yara, da kuma cikakken maganin cutar kansa ga masu ɗauke da cutar HIV. "SIDS a ƙasashen nahiyar Amurka na cigaba da zama masu rauni sosai ga NCDs.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided document. I've aimed for clarity and accuracy, keeping in mind the target audience and the importance of conveying the technical information correctly.  I've also included some notes after the translation regarding potential nuances and areas where further refinement might be needed depending on the specific context.

**Translation (Hausa):**

**Isarar Tsaka Mai Karfi Kan Cututtuka Na Ilma da Lafiya ta Ranci a Tsaka-tsakanin Gunduma**

A yau, 2023 Sanarwar Bridgetown kan Cututtuka Na Ilma (NCDs) da Lafiya ta Ranci an gabatar, don warware wasu matsalar cututtuka mafi kashe mutane a duniya a cikin Kasashen Tsaka-tsakanin Gunduma (SIDS), waɗanda ke cikin hadari sosai. Sanarwar wacce ta fi kwanaki ke nan ta fito ne daga Taron Ministocin SIDS kan Cututtuka Na Ilma (NCDs) da Lafiya ta Ranci, da ya gudana daga 14 zuwa 16 Yuni, wanda kungiyar WHO (World Health Organization), PAHO (Pan American Health Organization) da gwamnatin Barbados suka shirya tare.

SIDS suna fuskantar tasiri mafi girma daga NCDs – waɗanda ke haifar da kashi 74% na mutuwar mutane a duniya – sakamakon dogaro da abinci mai shigar da kasashen waje, tasiri kasuwancin da al'adar kasuwanci da rikici na yanayi. Sabon rahoton WHO ya nuna cewa, kasashe 8 akan kasashe 15 da ke da kimaɗa fiye da 30% na mutuwar da za ta faru da wuri sakamakon cutar zuciya, kansa, sukari ko cutar numfashi mai tsanani, su ne SIDS. Kasashe 10 da ke da mafi yawan matsalar jujuwa a duniya, duk su SIDS ne a Pacific, inda sama da kashi 45% na manya suna da jujuwa.

Matsalolin lafiyar hankali suna da yawa a kasashen SIDS, waɗanda ke shafar kimaɗa 15.2% na yawan jama'a a Caribbean da kimaɗa 11.2% na yawan jama'a a Pacific. Mutanen da ke da matsala lafiyar hankali suna fuskantar hadari sosai na mutuwar da za ta faru da wuri, gami da matsalolin lafiya na jiki da ba a warware ba da kuma kasala. Tsaurara ra'ayi, kawar da ma'aikata na musamman da tasirin canjin yanayi ke haɗaɗɗa yanayi mai wuya a SIDS wanda ya buƙaci kulawa nan da nan.

Sanarwar Bridgetown ta shekarar 2023 ta bayyana matakan da suka fi karfi don warware jigon matsalar zamantauwa, muhittaci, tattalin arziki da kasuwanci waɗanda ke haifar da NCDs da matsala lafiyar hankali. An shirya sanarwar ta hanyar aikin da aka shirya tare da SIDS, ta nuna cewa NCDs da matsala lafiyar hankali ba za su iya warware ba ba tare da amsa ga rikici na yanayi ba.

“Aikin da ya fi karfi don yanayina, lafiya mai kyau da kuma kyakkyawan zamantauwa ya dogara ne akan gyara da tsara kariya ta duniya don fitar da biliyoyin kudi zuwa saka hannun jari, yayin da hakan ya rage zafin tsoro ga kasashen da ke tasowa don biyan bashi,” inji Her Excellency Mia Amor Mottley, Shugabar gwamnatin Barbados. “Kudin da za a kashe don gyara canjin yanayi da hana ya fi kimaɗa, tare da lura da cututtuka na ilma da lafiyar hankali.”

“Matsalolin da SIDS ke fuskanta sun haɗa juna kuma sun kasance da yawa. Canjin yanayi, lalacewar muhittaci, bambance-bambancen zamantauwa da tattalin arziki, da kuma ƙa'idojin kasuwanci na lafiya suna ƙara yawan adadin NCDs da matsala lafiyar hankali,” inji Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Babban Daraktan WHO. “A duk da kasoƙinsu na ƙarancin albarkatunci da ƙuntawar ƙasashe, waɗannan ƙasashe sun nuna ƙarfin jinin da ba ta daɗewa ba a fuskantar mawuyacin hali. Tare, dole ne mu ƙirƙiri hanya da ta dogara ne akan daidaito, juriya da kuma dorewa. Dole ne mu ƙarfafa tsarin lafiya, ƙara ƙarfi da kuma gano cuta da wuri, da kuma ba da fifiko ga haɗaɗɗa ayyukan NCD da lafiyar hankali a cikin kula da lafiya na gaba ɗaya.”

Babban Daraktan ya kuma alkawari cewa WHO zai yi aiki don taro kudaden da za a kashe don gina wurare na kula da lafiya waɗanda ke jurewa canjin yanayi da kuma kiyaye muhittaci a cikin SIDS. WHO zai ci gaba da nemawa kudin ‘ɓacewa da lalata’ don saka hannun jari kan gyara canjin yanayi da hana a kasashe masu ƙarancin kudi. A matsayin wani bangare na amincewa da Sanarwar, SIDS sun amince da aiwatar da jerin ayyuka, da suka haɗa da shigaɓe duk al'umma, saurin aiwatar da ayyuka, tabbatar da saka hannun jari, daidaita a duk gwamnati da kuma ƙididdige ci gaba. Taron ya nuna maƙasoshi na musamman da SIDS ke yiwa NCDs da lafiyar hankali, gami da inganta samun lafiyar hankali a wuraren kula da lafiya na gaba ɗaya, sabunta dokokin kula da taba tabarau don rufe sigareta lantarki da kuma rage yawan shayarwa da sukari a cikin abincin jama'a. Waɗannan maƙasoshi sun haɗa da ‘mafi kyawun siyarwa’ na WHO – jerin hanyoyin da ke da tasiri da kuma dogaro da bincike don warware NCDs. An sabunta jerin ‘mafi kyawun siyarwa’ a taron Majalisar Kula da Lafiya ta Duniya a watan Mayu don haɗa da hana cuta da warware cutar rheumatic, kula da cutar iska da kuma cutar numfashi mai tsanani da kuma yawan adadin hanyoyin warware cutar mahaifa, cutar tsuntsaye, cutar ƙungiya da cutar kansu, da kuma warware cutar kansu ga mutanen da ke rayuwa da HIV. “SIDS a Amurka har yanzu suna cikin hadari sosai ga NCDs.”

**Notes on the Translation & Potential Refinements:**

*   **Technical Terminology:** I've used common Hausa terms for "noncommunicable diseases" (NCDs) and "mental health." However, depending on the specific audience (healthcare professionals vs. general public), these might need further clarification or adaptation.
*   **Cultural Nuances:**  The concept of "stigma" related to mental health can be particularly sensitive. The phrasing used ("Tsaurara ra'ayi") is a general translation, but a more culturally appropriate term might be needed.
*   **Specificity:** Some phrases are quite complex.  For example, "commercial determinants of health" is a relatively new concept.  I've tried to convey the meaning accurately, but a more detailed explanation might be necessary in some contexts.
*   **Flow and Readability:** I've aimed for a natural flow in Hausa, but it's always beneficial to have a native Hausa speaker review the translation for clarity and readability.
*   **Dialect:** Hausa has different dialects. This translation uses a fairly standard form, but consider the target audience's dialect for optimal understanding.

translations.source: This Ministerial Conference comes at a unique time and will be key to amplifying the voice and needs of SIDS, bringing NCDs and mental health to the forefront of our priority list,” said Dr Jarbas Barbosa, PAHO Director, and WHO Regional Director for the Americas. “To build on this momentum, we must act now. PAHO is ready to support accelerated implementation of interventions in our region, including mainstreaming care for these conditions into primary health services and taking advantage of innovations such as digital health tools.” SIDS countries have a track record of driving global decision-making; their Port of Spain and SAMOA pathway agreements paved the way for the first-ever UN political declaration on NCDs in 2018. The conference and the Declaration will inform and shape forthcoming UN General Assembly High-Level Meetings on universal health coverage in September 2023 and on NCDs in 2025.
translations.reference: Wannan taron na ministoci ya zo a lokaci na musamman, kuma zai zama mabuɗi don ci gaba da tattaunawa, dangane matsalar (mutuwa ta fuju'a) SIDS, da kuma bayyana matsalar (cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa) NCDs, da matsalar (lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa ) a sahun gaba cikin jerin muhimman abubuwan tattaunwa, in ji Dr Jarbas Barbosa, Daraktan ( ƙungiyar lafiya ta Amurka)PAHO, kuma Daraktan (ƙungiyar likitoci ta duniya yankin Amurka) WHO. Domin tabbatar wannan yunƙurin, dole ne mu yi ƙoƙari yanzu. PAHO (ƙungiyar lafiya ta Amurka) a shirye take don tallafawa aiwatar da ayyukan gaggawa a yankinmu, gami da kula da waɗannan yanayi cikin ayyukan kiwon lafiya a ƙaramin mataki, da kuma cin gajiyar abubuwan bunƙasawa, kamar kayan aikin kiwon lafiya na na'ura. Ƙasashe masu fama da (mutuwar fuju'a) SIDS sun yi nasarar yanke shawara ta duniya; a nan ne birnin Spain (Port of Spain) da yankin ( SAMOA )suka shimfida yarjejeniya, wacce ta ba da damar yin sanarwar duniya a Karo na farko, dangane da cututtuka marasa yaduwa, (NCDs) a shekara ta 2018. Taron na ƙarawa juna sani, da sanarwar da ta gabata; za su haska babban taron majalisar Ɗinkin duniya da ke tafe, kan batun kiwon lafiyar duniya a watan Satumba na 2023 da kuma kan cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa (NCDs) a shekara ta 2025.
