Here's the Hausa translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I've included explanations of some choices after the translation to justify certain phrasing.

**Hausa Translation:**

Wannan Taron Ministocin ya zo a lokaci mai wahaka kuma zai zama babban abin damfari don karfafa muryar da buƙatun ƙasashe masu ƙarancin tasiri (SIDS), da kuma fitar da cutan da ke haɓaka a hankali (NCDs) da lafiyar zuciya a gabaɗaya a matsayin babban burinmu," in ji Dr Jarbas Barbosa, Darakta Janar na PAHO, da kuma Mai daɗawa na WHO a Amurka. "Don ci gaba da wannan motsin, dole ne mu yi aiki yanzu. PAHO a shirye ke ta tallafawa aiwatar da sauri na hanyoyin magance matsala a yankinmu, gami da haɗa kula da waɗannan cutokin a cikin ayyukan lafiya na farko da amfani da sababbin kayayyakin fasaha, irin su kayan aikin lafiya na dijital." Kasashe na SIDS suna da tarihin fitar da ƙudirin duniya; yarjejeninsu na Port of Spain da SAMOA Pathway sun shirya hanyar don sabon bayanin siyasa na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya kan NCDs a shekarar 2018. Taron da Bayanin hakan zai ba da bayanin shawara kuma ya shaperin zaune-zaune na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya a kan kwarewar lafiya ta kowa a shekarar 2023 da kan NCDs a shekarar 2025.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **SIDS:** I've kept "SIDS" as is, as it's a common abbreviation even in Hausa discussions about international development.  Adding a full translation would be cumbersome and less recognizable.  I've added the full translation in parentheses the first time it appears: "ƙasashe masu ƙarancin tasiri (SIDS)"
*   **NCDs:** Similar to SIDS, I've kept "NCDs" and provided the full translation in parentheses the first time it appears: "cutan da ke haɓaka a hankali (NCDs)"
*   **PAHO & WHO:**  These are international organizations, and their acronyms are generally used in Hausa as well.
*   **"Amplifying the voice":** I translated this as "karfafa muryar" which means "strengthening the voice." This is a common and appropriate way to express this concept in Hausa.
*   **"Bringing...to the forefront":** Translated as "fitar da...a gabaɗaya" which means "bringing...to the front/fore."
*   **"To build on this momentum":** Translated as "Don ci gaba da wannan motsin" which means "To continue this movement/momentum."
*   **"Accelerated implementation":** Translated as "aiwatar da sauri" which means "fast implementation."
*   **"Mainstreaming care":** Translated as "haɗa kula" which means "integrating care/including care."
*   **"Digital health tools":** Translated as "kayan aikin lafiya na dijital" which means "digital health tools."
*   **"Track record":** Translated as "tarihin" which means "history/record."
*   **"Paved the way":** Translated as "sun shirya hanyar" which means "they paved the way/they prepared the path."
*   **"Inform and shape":** Translated as "ba da bayanin shawara kuma ya shaperin" which means "to give advice and to shape."
*   **"Universal health coverage":** Translated as "kwarewar lafiya ta kowa" which means "universal health experience/coverage."

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context and intended audience.
*   **Terminology Consistency:**  It's crucial to establish consistent terminology for technical terms like "NCDs" and "SIDS" across all translated documents.
*   **Review by Native Speakers:**  Ideally, a native Hausa speaker with expertise in health and international relations should review the translation for accuracy and naturalness.

translations.source: WHO’s Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting ends with stirring call for worldwide action Delegates and representatives left the Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting with a stirring call to action from its director Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall. As the multinational meeting, attended by some 300 key partners, came to an end in Geneva on Tuesday 13 June, Dr Fall urged participants to take on board the message that, “if we do not challenge ourselves, we cannot evolve and improve”. In a wide-ranging event held to review progress against the WHO road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030 and generate a better understanding of global NTD operations from both disease-specific and cross-cutting perspectives, partners, donors, technical partners, institutions and country representatives reinforced their commitment to achieving an ambitious target: a world free from neglected tropical diseases by 2030. The meeting’s primary theme centred around the vision of “One community delivering one Global NTD Programme”. Taking their cues from the three key strategic shifts advocated in the road map – greater country ownership, measuring impacts not process, and a commitment to cross-cutting activities and collaboration for greater efficiency – discussions were pragmatic, clear-sighted and suffused also with the need for health equity and justice to tackle NTDs. Only such an approach, the meeting heard, can bring about the change that is needed by some of the world’s poorest communities to achieve the United Nations 2030 Sustainable Development Goals. In the first such global meeting since the launch of the road map in January 2021, delegates heard also from WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. Welcoming participants at the beginning of the two-day symposium, Dr Tedros said, “This gathering illustrates what WHO is all about – partners coming together to leverage their collective strength to address global health challenges that none of us could address alone. “A multifaceted challenge like neglected tropical diseases takes a multifaceted approach, and that is what we bring, together”. Delegates and participants spent the subsequent two days identifying the major gaps and challenges to achieving high-level goals and local, on-the-ground challenges. Those on-the-ground challenges include ensuring that people directly affected by NTDs, and those directly responsible for administering interventions against NTDs, are solicited and acknowledged, particularly in terms of communication and community engagement. The meeting noted also the importance of developing communities of practice, in order to build the capacity and confidence of the global NTD workforce. In further discussions, delegates agreed on principles and practical steps to implement action towards the stated targets and planned together for a Global Action Plan for 2025–2030. Key actions and decisions arising from the meeting included recommendations for strengthening and operationalizing national coordination platforms to ensure coordination for joint planning and for monitoring and evaluation, and to ensure more efficient supply chain management within national systems. The Partners’ Meeting took place also at the end of a major functional review process within WHO’s Department of Control of Neglected Tropical Diseases. Over the course of the past four months, WHO has been hearing contributions and testimony from all its partners and stakeholders, in order to ensure that it itself embodies the values of accountability, transparency and equity that it espouses and demands. Those values also include ensuring that NTDs are repositioned in the global health architecture. Meeting participants welcomed the emphasis on the key roles that the NTD community can and must play in contributing to wider challenges such as Universal Health Coverage, One Health and the increasing threat of global climate change. Delegates attending in person were also able to enjoy an exhibition dedicated to neglected tropical diseases at WHO headquarters. A set of metal panels was commissioned to illustrate the 20 conditions that make up the NTD grouping, and these were accompanied by photographs and information illustrating the actions that have been put in place over the course of many decades to tackle them.
translations.reference: Tattaunawar ƙungiyar ma'aikatan lafiya na duniya, na ma'abota shirin nazarin cututtukan yankuna masu fama da zafin Rana wa'yanda a kayi sake; ya ƙare da kiran ɗaukar mataki a fadin duniya. Jama'a da wakilai sun bar wajen shirin da aka yi na abokan hulɗar nazarin cututtukan yankuna masu fama da zafin rana; tare da kiran mayar da hankali wajen ɗaukar mataki daga darektanta Dr Ibrahima SocÃ Fall. Yayin da taron kasa da kasa, wanda ya samu halartar wasu manyan abokan hulɗa 300, ya samu ƙarƙarewa a Geneva a ranar Talata 13 ga watan Yuni, Dr Fall ya bukaci mahalarta taron da su dauki sakon cewa, idan ba mu kalubalanci kanmu ba, ba za mu iya ingantawa ba ko Cigaba. A cikin wani babban taron da aka gudanar don nazarin ci gaban (ƙungiyar likitoci ta duniya) WHO, a tsare - tsarenta don kula da cututtukan yankuna masu fama da zafin rana a 2021- 2030, da samar da kyakkyawar fahimta game da ayyukan ( cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa )NTD na duniya daga ƙayyadaddun cututtuka da kuma hangen gaba, abokan tarayya, masu ba da gudummawa, abokan fasaha, cibiyoyi da wakilan ƙasa; sun ƙarfafa himmarsu don cimma manufa mai fa'ida: a hasashen duniya Na kubuta daga cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa a yankunan masu fama da zafin rana a shekara ta 2030. Jigon taron ya tattara ne akan Samar da al'umma guda, wajen gudanar da shirin NTD na duniya bai ɗaya. Ta hanyar daukar bayanai masu mahimmanci guda, Wanda ya assasa tsarin mallakar nagartacciyar kasa, wajen auna cigaba ba hanyoyinsa ba, da jajircewa na al'amura , da haɗin gwiwa domin tabbatar da nuna kwarewa, abubuwan da aka tattauna an gudanar dasu a aikace , a bayyane kuma an yaɗata tare da buƙatar daidaito da adalci a harkar kiwon lafiya, domin daƙile matsalar NTDs. Irin wannan tsarin ne kawai, in ji taron, zai iya samar da sauyin da wasu al'ummomin duniya mafi talauci ke buƙata domin cimma muradun ci gaba mai dorewa na Majalisar ɗinkin Duniya a shekarar 2030. A cikin irin wannan taron na farko na duniya tun bayan ƙaddamar da tsarin a watan Janairun 2021, wakilai kuma sun ji ta bakin daraktan WHO - Janar Dr tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. Da yake maraba da mahalarta farkon taron na kwanaki biyu, Dr tedros ya ce, wannan taron ya bayya komai game da WHO, abokan hadin gwiwa da ke haduwa don yin amfani da karfin hadin gwiwarsu don magance kalubalen kiwon lafiyar duniya wanda babu daya daga cikimmu da zai iya magancewa shi kadai. Kalubale iri-iri kamar cututtukan yankuna masu fama zafi rana, da aka yi sake da su, kuma shi ne abin da muka kawo tare. Wakilai da mahalarta taron sun shafe kwanaki biyu, suna bankaɗo manyan giɓina da ƙalubalen cimma manyan burika da kuma ƙalubale na gida. Waɗanda ke fuskantar ƙalubalen ƙasa da suka haɗa da mutanen da abin ya shafesu kaitsaye, dangane da NTDs da waɗanda ke da alhakin gudanar da ayyukan magance NTDa, an nemi su yarda, musamman ta fuskar sadarwa da haɗin gwiwar al'umma. Taron ya kuma lura da mahimmancin haɓaka al'ummomi masu kokari, don haɓaka iyawa da kwarin gwiwa da amincewar ma'aikatan NTD na duniya. A ci gaba da tattaunawa, wakilai sun amince da ka'idoji da matakai masu amfani don aiwatar da ayyuka zuwa ga maƙasudan da ake so a cimma, tare da tsarawa Bai ɗaya, domin ɗaukar mataki na duniya na 2025-2030. Muhimman matakai da yanke shawarwari a taron sun haɗa da shawarwari don ƙarfafawa da aiwatar da haɗin gwiwar amfani da kafafen sadarwa na zamani, don tabbatar da haɗin kai don cigaba da tsare - tsare, wajen lura da rairayewa, da kuma tabbatar da ingantaccen tsarin sarrafa kayan aiki a cikin tsarin kasa. Taron abokan hulɗar ya gudana kuma a ƙarshen babban aikin bita a cikin Sashen Kula da Cututtukan yankuna masu fama da zafin rana na WHO. A cikin watanni hudu da suka gabata, WHO tana jin gudumawa da shaida daga dukkan abokan hulɗarta da masu ruwa da tsaki, domin tabbatar da cewa ita kanta ta kunshi dabi’un rikon amana da gaskiya da daidaito,wanda ta yadda da kuma buƙata. Waɗannan abubuwa masu ƙima sun haɗa da tabbatar da cewa an bunƙasa NTDs a cikin tsare - tsaren cigaban kiwon lafiya na duniya. Mahalarta taron sun yi maraba da abin lura kan muhimman ayyukan da al'ummar NTD za su iya, kuma dole ne su bada gudummuwa ga manya - manyan kalubale, gamayyar harkar Lafiya ta Duniya, harkar lafiya ta Bai ɗaya, ƙaro ƙoƙarin magance barazanar sauyin yanayi a duniya. Wakilan da suka halarta kai tsaye sun sami damar jin daɗin wani baje kolin da aka keɓe don kula da cututtuka na yankuna masu fama da zafin rana a hedikwatar WHO. An dauki wasu Jerin shawarwari don killace yanayin 20 da ya haɗa da rukinin NTD, kuma waɗannan sun haɗa da hotuna da bayanai da ke nuna ayyukan da aka yi a cikin shekaru masu yawa don magance su.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided document into Hausa, aiming for clarity and accuracy. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices and potential areas for refinement.

**Translation (Hausa):**

Taron Abokan Kashin Shirin Tsare-Tsaren Cututtukan Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTD) na WHO ya ƙare da kiran da ya kunno kai don aiwatar da ayyukan a duniya. Wakilan taron da masanan sun bar taron Abokan Kashin Shirin Tsare-Tsaren Cututtukan Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTD) na WHO da kiran aiki mai karfi daga Babban Darakta, Dr. Ibrahima Socé Fall. Lokacin da taron, wanda aka yi a Geneva a ranar Laraba, 13 ga Yuni, ya ƙare, Dr. Fall ya bukaci mawaƙa su ɗauki sakon cewa, “Idan ba mu ƙara ƙarfafawa kanmu ba, ba za mu iya ci gaba da inganta al’amuranmu ba.”

A taron, wanda ya dauki tsawon lokaci kuma ya yi nazarin ci gaban da aka samu bisa ga Takardun Shiri na WHO don Cututtukan Neglected Tropical Diseases 2021–2030, da kuma ƙara fahimtar aiki na NTD a duniya daga dukkanin rawarin lafiya da kuma matakan da suka shafi dukkanin cututtuka, abokan tarin, masu ba da tallafi, masanan fasaha, jami’an hukumomi da wakilan kasashe sun ƙarfafa rawar da za su taka wajen cimma burin da ya kasance mai mahimmanci: duniya ba tare da Cututtukan Neglected Tropical Diseases ba har zuwa shekarar 2030.

Babban burin taron ya kasance kan gudanar da aiki ne ta hanyar “Wata al’umma ɗaya da ke aiwatar da Shirin NTD na Duniya ɗaya.”  Duk da cewa sun sami umurnin canja-canje guda uku da aka bayyana a Takardun Shiri – ƙarin hannun jarin kasashen, aiki kan sakamako mace, da kuma amincewa da aikin da ya shafi dukkanin cututtuka da kuma hadin gwiwa don ƙara yuwuwar aiki – taron ya kasance mai amfani, mai haske, kuma ya ƙara da buƙatar daidaito da adalci a lafiya don yaki da NTD.  Taron ya ce, irin wannan hanyar tilo za ta haɓaka canji da ake buƙata a cikin wasu al’ummomin duniya mafi talauci don cimma burin ci gaban Kudi na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya na 2030.

A karon farko a taron irin wannan a duniya tun bayan fitar da Takardun Shiri a watan Janairu na 2021, wakilan sun ji tsohon Babban Darakta na WHO, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus.  Yayinda yake maraba da mawaƙa a bikin da ya dauki tsawon kwanaki biyu, Dr. Tedros ya ce, “Taron kamar yayi bayanin abin da WHO ke nufi – abokan tarin da ke haɗuwa don amfani da ƙarfin da suka haɗa don warware matsalolin lafiya a duniya waɗanda babu wanda zai iya warwarewa kadai.”

“Matsalar da ta shafi lafiya ta buƙatar hanyar warwarewa da ta shafi lafiya, kuma hakan ne da muke kawo, tare.”  Wakilan da mawaƙa sun shaida wa kwanaki biyu na gaba suna ƙididdigar babban ɓangarɓan da matsalolin da suka hana cimma burin da ke sama da matakan da suka dace da matsalolin da ke faruwa a kasa.  Matsalolin da ke faruwa a kasa sun haɗa da tabbatar da cewa mutanen da ake shafar da su ta amsar NTD, da kuma waɗanda ke da alhakin gudanar da ayyukan yaki da NTD, suna da damar yin magani da kuma amincewa da su, musamman a kan hanyoyin sadarwa da kuma aminchen al’umma.

Taron ya lura da mahimmancin kafa al’ummomin aiki, don ƙirƙirar ƙarfi da amincin ma’aikatan NTD a duniya.  A taron, wakilan sun amince da ƙa’idoji da matakan aiki don aiwatar da aikin da ke kan gaba da burin da aka kafa da kuma shirye-shiryen aiki don Tsare-Tsaren Aiki na Duniya na 2025–2030.  Ƙa’idoji da yanke shawara da aka samu daga taron sun haɗa da shawarwari don ƙarfafa da aiwatar da wuraren da ke da alhakin shirya shirye-shirye a matakin kasa don tabbatar da shirya shirye-shirye da kuma saka ido da kuma aiki, da kuma tabbatar da sarrafa isar da abinci mai kyau a cikin tsarin kasa.

Taron Abokan Kashin ya yi kusa da karshen babban aikin bincike a cikin Sashe na WHO na Kariya da Cututtukan Neglected Tropical Diseases.  A tsawon watanni huɗu da suka gabata, WHO na ji daɗin rahotannin abokan tarin da masanan.  Don tabbatar da cewa WHO kanta ta jaddada daruruwan da ta riga ta yi, kamar aikin da ke da dangantaka da bayar da amsa, gaskiya da daidaito.  Waɗannan daruruwan sun haɗa da tabbatar da cewa NTD an sake shirya su a cikin ginin lafiya na duniya.  Mawaƙa sun maraba da fifikon rawar da al’ummar NTD za su iya taka da kuma buƙatar yin bayani kan matsalolin da suka shafi lafiya, irin su Yawanin Kare lafiya, Lafiya ɗaya da barazanar canjin yanayi a duniya.  Wakilan da suka halarci taron a wurin sun sami damar jin taron da aka shirya don NTD a hedkwarten WHO.  An shirya jefa allon ƙasa don nuna yanayi 20 da ke ƙunnuwa a NTD, kuma an haɗa su da hotunan da ke nuni da ayyukan da aka yi a tsawon shekaru da dama don yaki da su.

---

**Notes on the Translation & Potential Refinements:**

*   **Terminology:** I've used common Hausa terms for "Neglected Tropical Diseases" (Cututtukan Neglected Tropical Diseases - NTD).  It's possible there's a more established, localized term, which would be ideal.
*   **Clarity & Flow:** I've tried to maintain a natural flow in Hausa, but some sentences might need further adjustment for optimal readability.
*   **Cultural Nuances:**  Hausa has its own stylistic preferences. A native Hausa speaker with experience in medical/health translations would be able to refine the language further to ensure it resonates well with the target audience.
*   **Technical Terms:** Some technical terms related to health programs and international organizations might require further research to ensure the most accurate and appropriate Hausa equivalents.
*   **"One community delivering one Global NTD Programme"**: This phrase was tricky to translate directly while maintaining the intended meaning. I opted for a phrasing that conveys the idea of unified action.
*   **Proofreading:**  A thorough proofread by a native Hausa speaker is *essential* before using this translation for any official purpose.

*   Who is the intended audience for this translation? (e.g., healthcare workers, general public, policymakers)

translations.source: Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting, 12–13 June 2023 Representatives of the global NTD community will gather in Geneva next week to participate in the Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting, which takes place in headquarters’ auditorium on 12-13 June 2023. The objectives of the meeting are to review progress on the WHO road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030 and generate a better understanding of global NTD operations from both disease-specific and cross-cutting perspectives, as well as to engage in wide-ranging discussions around the vision for One WHO delivering on one Global NTD Programme. While previous global meetings of NTD partners were convened by WHO in 2007 and 2017, this third upcoming event is the first since the launch of the road map in January 2021. The WHO road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030 is a comprehensive framework that outlines targets, mechanisms for alignment with the Sustainable Development Goals and other global agendas and calls for strategic shifts in NTD approaches with the aim of ending the epidemic of neglected tropical diseases by 2030. The meeting will focus on: reviewing current progress to achieving the 2030 targets; identifying the major gaps and challenges to reaching those targets; agreeing principles and practical steps for how to implement action towards the targets; galvanizing support and commitment from NTD partners to operationalize the shifts advocated in the road map and to achieve its targets; and planning together for a Global Action Plan 2025–2030. Over 300 participants in the meeting are expected to attend, either in-person or online. They will include representatives of the global NTD community, from countries and donors to implementing and technical partners, from academia to patients’ associations and all other entities and institutions involved in the fight against NTDs. An exhibition on neglected tropical diseases will take place in the mezzanine of the B building, just above the cafeteria. A set of metal panels will illustrate the 20 conditions grouped under the NTD umbrella and provide information on actions implemented to tackle their burden.

Makasudin taron shi ne duba ci gaba akan tsare - tsaren WHO don kula da cututtuka na yankuna masu fama da zafin rana 2021- 2030 da samar da kyakkyawar fahimta game da ayyukan NTD na duniya daga dukkanin ainihin cututtuka, da ra'ayoyi daban-daban, da kuma tattaunawa mai yawa dangane da hangen nesa wajwn gudanarwar WHO Na bai ɗaya, na s hirin ( NTD). Yayin da taron duniya da ya gabata na abokan huldar NTD, an tattauna da WHO a 2007 da 2017, wannan taron na uku da ke tafe shi ne na farko tun bayan kaddamar da sababbin tsare - tsare a cikin Janairu na 2021. Tsare -tsaren WHO don kula da cututtukan yankuna masu fama zafin rana 2021 - 2030 wani tsari ne mai mahimmanci wanda ke bayyana manifofi, hanyoyin tsara dabaru tare da Manufofin Ci gaba mai dorewa, da sauran ajandoji na duniya, an kuma yin kira dangane da cigaban NTD Na nan gaba, tare da da nufin kawo karshen annobar cututtukan yankuna masu fama da zafin rana nan da shekarar 2030. Tattaunawar za ta ta'allaka ne a kan: duba irin ci gaban da aka samu a yanzu don cimma muradun 2030; gano manyan giɓina da ƙalubalen da ake fuskanta wajen cimma waɗancan maƙasudan; yarda da ka'idoji da matakai masu amfani don yadda za a aiwatar da ayyuka don cimma manufa; tallafawa goyon baya da sadaukarwa daga abokan hulɗar NTD don aiwatar da canjin shawarwarin da aka bayar a tsare - tsare, don cimma burika ; da kuma shiryawa a tare don gudanar da ayyukan Duniya na 2025 - 2030. **Taronar Kungiyoyin Bude Kasashen Duniya da Cututtukan Kwayoyin Halitta, 12–13 Yuni 2023**

Wakilan al'ummar duniya da ke da alaka da Cututtukan Kwayoyin Halitta (NTD) za su taro a Geneva a makon nan da ke zuwa don shiga taron Kungiyoyin Bude Kasashen Duniya da Cututtukan Kwayoyin Halitta. Taron zai gudana a fadar hedkwatar a ranakun 12-13 Yuni 2023.

Makamuwar taron ita ce a duba ci gaban da aka samu a bisa ga jagorar WHO kan cututtukan kwayoyin halitta 2021–2030, da kuma bunkasar fahimtar yadda ake aiwatar da ayyukan NTD a duniya daga dukkanin rawarin lafiya ta musamman da kuma ta dacewa da sauran ayyuka, da kuma shiga cikin tattaunawa mai karfi kan hangen nesa na WHO daya da ke aiwatar da Kungiyar NTD ta Duniya. A da, taron kungiyoyin NTD na duniya ya gudana a shekarun 2007 da iyin 2017, amma wannan taron na uku da zai zo, shi ne karo na farko tun bayan fitar da jagorar a watan Janairu na 2021.

Jagorar WHO kan cututtukan kwayoyin halitta 2021–2030, ita ce kyakkyawan tsari da ke bayanin abubuwan da za a cimma, hanyoyin da za a bi don daidaita da Malaman Ci Gaban Kula da Harkokin Duniya (SDGs) da sauran ayyukan duniya, da kuma kiran canje-canje na hanyoyin NTD da nufin kawar da cutar NTD zuwa shekarar 2030.

Taron zai mai da hankali ne akan: duba ci gaban da aka samu har yanzu don cimma burin shekarar 2030; gano babban rashi da kalubale a kan hanyar cimma wannan burin; amincewa da ka'idoji da matakan aiki don yadda za a aiwatar da ayyuka don cimma burin; bunkasar goyon baya da sadaukarwa daga kungiyoyin NTD don aiwatar da canje-canjen da ke cikin jagorar da kuma cimma burinta; da kuma shirye-shiryen hadin gwiwa don Tsarin Aiki na Duniya 2025–2030.

Kima huɗun sama da 300 da ke da alaka da taron za a sa ido a halin da ake ciki ko a layi don shiga taron. Za su haɗa da wakilan al'ummar NTD ta duniya, daga kasashe da abokan taimakon zuwa abokan aiwatar da ayyuka da masana, daga jami'ai zuwa kungiyoyin wadatar da ilimi da sauran hukumomi da ƙungiyoyin da ke da alaka da yaki da NTD.

Za a yi allon cututtukan kwayoyin halitta a wajan mezzanine na ginin B, kusa da kantin abinci. Wannan allon zai nuna yanayi 20 da ke ƙarƙashin umbranin NTD, da kuma bayanin ayyukan da aka aiwatar don kawar da cutar.

---

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **NTD:** I've kept "NTD" as is, as it's a commonly used abbreviation in the field, even in Hausa discussions.  I've also provided the full translation "Cututtukan Kwayoyin Halitta" for clarity on first mention.
*   **WHO:**  Similarly, I've kept "WHO" as it's widely recognized.
*   **"Road Map":** Translated as "jagora" which means "guide" or "roadmap" in this context.
*   **"Partners":** Translated as "kungiyoyin" which means "groups" or "partners."
*   **"Mezzanine":**  I used "wajan mezzanine" to indicate the location, as a direct Hausa equivalent isn't common.
*   **"Galvanizing Support":** Translated as "bunkasar goyon baya" which means "increasing support."
*   **Word Choice:** I tried to use relatively standard Hausa vocabulary that would be understood across different regions.
*   **Sentence Structure:** I adjusted sentence structure where necessary to ensure natural flow in Hausa.  Direct, word-for-word translations often sound awkward.

**Important Considerations:**

*   **Target Audience:** This translation assumes a reasonably educated audience familiar with global health terminology.
*   **Regional Variations:** Hausa has regional dialects. This translation aims for a generally understandable form.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:** Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Hausa speaker with expertise in health communication to ensure accuracy and cultural appropriateness.

I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other questions or would like me to refine any part of it.

translations.source: WHO announces winners of the 4th Health for All Film Festival 7 winning short films, 4 special mentions from the jury The World Health Organization has announced the official selection of this year’s winning films at its 4th Annual Health for All Film Festival, held at WHO Headquarters, Geneva. The event, attended in person and online by actors, producers and public figures, saw winning films announced for 7 different categories, while 4 films received special mentions from the jury. This is the fourth year of the film festival and competition was no less fierce, with some 93 shortlisted films covering issues ranging from anxiety and depression through to the effects of climate change on health, as well as the health challenges of people with disabilities. The films were judged by a panel of distinguished professionals, artists and activists, including the renown actors, Sharon Stone and Alfonso Herrera, dance choreographer, Sherrie Silver, climate activist, Sophia Kianni and media personality, Adelle Onyango. They were joined by senior United Nations officials and WHO staff. “The Health for All Film Festival brings a human face to WHO’s scientific work,” said WHO Director-General, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “Listening to the voices of people affected by health issues is a powerful way to raise awareness and improve our understanding of people’s experiences and this can help us advance towards health for all.” From the official selection one “GRAND PRIX” is attributed for each of the three main competition categories: Universal Health Coverage, Health emergencies, and Better health and well-being, which align with WHO’s Triple Billion Targets. Sharon Stone, Golden Globe and Emmy Award-winning actress from the United States of America, producer and activist for health and humanitarian causes said: "I am delighted to be part of the Health for All Film Festival. This is about creating better awareness on crucial actions needed for reaching healthier living conditions around the world. The stories selected talk to us about the intrinsic value of good health and its access, and they advocate for universal health coverage. Universal Health Coverage is a very important right, it’s a human right for everyone around the world. "Four special prizes were also given for a Student-produced film, a film on Climate Change and Health, a film on Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights and a Very Short Film. The theme of mental health featured heavily in this year’s winning entries, including a powerful and moving short film from Sweden about anxiety and depression. The film, set to a stirring soundtrack, featured a series of actors expressing different stages of anxiety and how it can manifest itself. The overall message was that you are not alone in experiencing negative thoughts and that it is okay to acknowledge such feelings.Another winning film, from Bangladesh, features a young boy who was exposed to lead from a local factory. The moving film details the effects this has had on his educational development and the work that a local non-governmental organization has done to both highlight the issue and take action to prevent lead poisoning in the affected community. List of films awarded: UHC "Grand Prix": “Jonathan’s Miracle Feet” – Sierra Leone / Disability, clubfoot Directed by Mamihasina Raminosoa and Nantenaina Rakotondranivo from Madagascar for the NGO Miracle Feet / Documentary – Duration 3’19’’ Health Emergencies "Grand Prix": “Nurses facing Covid / Na Lihna de Frente” – Brazil / COVID-19 and access to care
translations.reference: WHO ta sanar da waɗanda suka yi nasara a bikin Kiwon Lafiya na 4 na Dukan Fina-Finai 7 sun lashe na gajerun fina-finai, ambaton 4 na musamman daga rukuni Na musamman ƙungiyar likitoci ta Duniya ta sanar da zaɓin zaɓaɓɓu na wannan shekarar, na gasar fina - finai , a taronta na kiwon lafiya na 4 ga dukkanin tarukan ingancin fina - finai da yake gudana a hedikwatar WHO , Geneva. taron wanda ’yan wasa da furodusoshi da manyan jama’a suka halarta Ido da Ido da kuma ta kafar sadarwa, an bayyana fina-finan da suka samu nasara a sassa daban-daban guda 7, yayin da fina-finai 4 suka sami ambato na musamman daga alƙalan gasa. Wannan ita ce shekara ta hudu ta gudanar da bikin fim, kuma gasar ba ta yi kasa a gwiwa ba, inda wasu fina-finai 93 da aka tantance waɗanda suka kunshi batutuwan da suka hada da damuwa da ƙunci a sakamakon tasirin sauyin yanayi a harkar lafiya, da kuma kalubalen kiwon lafiyar nakasassu. Wasu gungun kwararrun masana, masu fasaha da masu fafutuka ne suka tantance fina-finan, ciki har da fitattun 'yan wasan kwaikwayo, Sharon Stone da kuma Alfonso herrera, mawaƙin rawa, sherri silver, mai fafutukar Lura da sauyin, sophia kianni da cikakken ɗan media, adelle onyango. Sun hade da manyan jami'o'i da ma'aikatan kungiyar likitoci ta duniya. Taron ingancin fina-finai ya yi Jan hankali ga WHO, cewar daraktan daraktoci na WHO, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. Sauraron muryoyin mutanen da al'amurran kiwon lafiya suka shafa dangane da matsalolin harkar lafiya, wata hanya ce mai karfi don wayar da kan jama'a da inganta fahimtarmu game da abubuwan da mutane ke fuskanta, kuma wannan zai iya taimaka mana mu bunƙasa kiwon lafiya zuwa gaba. Daga zaɓin alƙalan gasar , ɗayan 'yan gasar ana danganta shi ga ɗaya daga cikin ukun ɓangarorin gasar: Masu gamayyar kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya, Masu Lura da sashen tallafin gaggawa, masu Lura da Ingancin kiwon lafiya da walwala, waɗanda suka sanarwa da WHO biliyan uku wanda ake nema. Sharon Stone, Golden Globe da Emmy masu lashe lambar yabo ta 'yan gasa daga Amurka, furodusa kuma mai fafutukar kiwon lafiya da harkokin jin ƙai ya ce: "Na yi farin cikin kasancewa cikin bikin ingancin Fina-finai. Wannan ya kasance ne don samar da wayewa me kyau ,a Mahimman ƙoƙarin da ake buƙata don samun ingantaccen yanayi Na lafiya a duniya,. labarun da aka zaɓa suna magana da mu game da ainihin ƙimar lafiya da samun damarta, kuma suna ba da shawarar samar da gamayyar lafiya ta Duniya. Gamayyar kiwon lafiya ta duniya hakki ne mai matuƙar mahimmanci, haƙƙin ɗan adam ne ga kowa da kowa a duniya. An kuma bayar da kyautuka na musamman guda hudu na fim din da dalibai suka shirya a kan sauyin yanayi da lafiya, da fim kan lafiyar jima'i da haifuwa da hakkoki da kuma gajeren fim. Taken lafiyar kwakwalwa ya fito sosai a cikin shigarwar nasara ta wannan shekara, gami da gajerun fim mai ƙarfi da motsi daga mutanen ƙasar Sweden game da damuwa da baƙin ciki. Fim ɗin, wanda aka tsara da sauti mai ban sha'awa, ya ƙunshi jerin 'yan wasan kwaikwayo da ke bayyana matakan damuwa daban-daban da kuma yadda zai iya bayyana kansa. Gabaɗayan saƙon shi ne cewa ba kai kaɗai bane ke fuskantar munanan tunani kuma yana da kyau a gane irin wannan tunanin. Wani fim ɗin da aka yi nasara, daga ƙasar Bangladash, ya nuna wani yaro matashi da aka bayyana da jagoran wata masana'anta. Fim din mai ratsa jiki ya yi bayani kan illar da hakan ya haifar ga ci gabansa na ilimi da kuma ayyukan da wata kungiya mai zaman kanta ta ƙasa ta yi wajen bayyana lamarin tare da daukar matakin hana cutar a cikin al’ummar da abin ya shafa. Jerin fina-finan da aka karrama: Yan gasar gamayyar kiwon lafiya ta Duniya: Jonathan Miracle feet daga ƙasar Siraliyon/ Disability, clubfoot Umarni daga Mamihasina Raminosoa da Nantenaina Rakotondranivo daga madagaska, suka jagoranta daga ƙasar Madagascar don ƙungiyar Miracle Feet / Documentary , lokaci 3 - 19 Rukunin masu Lura da sashen agajin gaggawa suna fuskantar Covid / Na Lihna de Frente Brazil / COVID-19 da samun damar kulawa
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided document. I've aimed for clarity and accuracy, keeping in mind the target audience and the importance of conveying the message effectively.  I've also included some notes after the translation regarding potential nuances and choices made.

**WHO ta bayyana wadancan da suka yi nasara a Gasuwar Kallo ta 4 kan Lafiya ga Kowa**

**Wadandan fina-finai 7 suka yi nasara, 4 kuma an bayar da yabo na musamman daga kwamitin sherrar**

Kungiyar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) ta bayyana zaɓuɓɓukan fina-finai da suka yi nasara a wannan shekarar a Gasuwar Kallo ta Lafiya ga Kowa ta shekarar 4, wacce aka yi a hedkwatar WHO, Geneva. Taron, wanda aka halarta da mutane a wurin da kuma kan layi, ya hada da bayyana fina-finai da suka yi nasara a fannonin daban 7, yayin da fina-finai 4 suka samu yabo na musamman daga kwamitin sherrar.

Wannan shine shekarar ta hudu da aka yi gasar fina-finai, kuma gasar ta yi tsanaki sosai, da fina-finai kusan 93 suka shiga, suna magana akan batõ-batõ daban-daban, daga damuwa da cutar ta numfashi zuwa tasirin canjin yanayi akan lafiya, da kuma matsalolin lafiya da mutanen da ke da cikas suka fuskanta.

An daukaka fina-finai zuwa kwamitin sherrar da ke da masana, fannin adãbi da masu kasancewa da suka hada da fina-finan mashahuri, Sharon Stone da Alfonso Herrera, mai tsara rawa Sherrie Silver, mai kasancewa akan canjin yanayi Sophia Kianni da kuma jaridar Adelle Onyango. An haɗa su da jami'an kungiyar kasashen duniya da ma'aikatan WHO.

“Gasuwar Kallo ta Lafiya ga Kowa tana bawa aikin kimiyya na WHO fuska ta mutum,” in ji Babban Daraktan WHO, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “Sõyin muryõr mutanen da suka shafi matsalolin lafiya ya zama hanyar da ta fi kõma hankali da kuma inganta fahimtar mu akan abubuwan da mutane ke fuskanta, kuma hakan na iya taimaka mana a ci gaba zuwa lafiya ga kowa.”

Daga zaɓuɓɓukan da aka kãrar, an ba da “KYAUTA TA MANYAN DARASAR” (GRAND PRIX) a kowannen manyan fannõni uku: Kwarewar Lafiya ta Kowa, Matsalolin Lafiya, da kuma Lafiya mai kyau da kuma kõma hankali, waɗanda suka haɗu da burõin “Triple Billion” na WHO.

Sharon Stone, fina-fitar Golden Globe da Emmy Award daga Amurka, mai gabatar da fina-finai da kuma mai kasancewa akan lafiyar al'umma ta ce: "Ina farin ciki da kasancewa a Gasuwar Kallo ta Lafiya ga Kowa. Wannan yana da niyyar bunkasar hankali akan matakan da suka fi kõma buƙãta don samun yanayin rayuwar lafiya a duniya. Fina-finai da aka zaba suna magana mana akan darajar lafiya mai kyau da samun damar samun ta, kuma suna da'awar kwarewar lafiya ta kowa. Kwarewar Lafiya ta Kowa hakan na bukatar a yi amshi, hakan na haƙin mutum ga kowa a duniya."

An kuma bayar da kyautõ uku na musamman don fina-finai da suka haɗa: fina-finai da suka haɗa dalibai, fina-finai akan canjin yanayi da lafiya, fina-finai akan lafiya da jinsin maza da mata da kuma fina-finai mai sauri.

Batõn lafiya ta tunani ya bayyana sosai a fina-finai da suka yi nasara a wannan shekarar, gami da fina-finan matãimakin da kuma mai zafi daga Sweden game da damuwa da cutar ta numfashi. Fina-finan, wanda aka shirya tare da waƙa mai ban mamaki, ya nuna wasu fina-finai suna nuni da matakan damuwa da yadda hakan ke bayyana. Sarkin saƙon shine cewa ba ka da shi kadai a cikin fuskantar tunanin mara kyau kuma yana yiwuwa a amsa irin wannan ra'ayoyin.

Wani fina-finan da ya yi nasara daga Bangladesh, ya nuna yaro mai shekaru da aka sanya masa guba daga masana'antar gida. Fina-finan ya bayyana tasirin wannan abubuwan akan ci gaban iliminsa da aikin da kungiyar farar kai ta gida ta yi don nuna wannan matsala da kuma daukar matakan hana guba a al'ummar da ta shafi.

Jerin fina-finai da aka ba da kyautõ:

UHC “Kyautar Darasa Mai Girma”: “Jonathan’s Miracle Feet” – Sierra Leone / Cikas, ƙwallon ƙafã
An shirya ta Mamihasina Raminosoa da Nantenaina Rakotondranivo daga Madagascar don kungiyar NGO Miracle Feet / Takardun – Tsawaita 3’19’’

Matsalolin Lafiya “Kyautar Darasa Mai Girma”: “Mãlãmãi da ke fãcewa da Covid / Na Lihna de Frente” – Brazil / COVID-19 da samun damar kula da lafiya.

---

**Notes on Translation Choices & Potential Nuances:**

*   **Formal vs. Informal:** I've aimed for a relatively formal tone, appropriate for an official announcement from WHO.
*   **Terminology:** I've tried to use standard Hausa terms for medical and organizational concepts.  Some terms might have multiple translations, and I've chosen the ones that seem most appropriate in this context.
*   **"Health for All":**  I used "Lafiya ga Kowa" which is a common and well-understood translation.
*   **"Grand Prix":** I translated this as "KYAUTA TA MANYAN DARASAR" which is a direct translation and understandable.
*   **Cultural Sensitivity:** I've been mindful of cultural sensitivities when translating terms related to mental health and disability.
*   **Accuracy:** I've prioritized accuracy in conveying the meaning of the original text.
*   **Word Choice:** I have tried to use words that are commonly used and understood by a wide range of Hausa speakers.

**To help me improve the translation further, could you tell me:**

*   Who is the intended audience for this translation? (e.g., general public, healthcare professionals, etc.)
*   Are there any specific Hausa dialects that are preferred?
*   Is there any particular terminology that you would like me to use or avoid?

translations.source: Basic energy access lags amid renewable opportunities, new report shows Global energy access gap persists: 675 million people without electricity, 2.3 billion people reliant on harmful cooking fuels A new report by the International Energy Agency (IEA), the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), the United Nations Statistics Division (UNSD), the World Bank, and the World Health Organization (WHO), released today, finds that the world is not on track to achieve the Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 7 for energy by 2030. This year marks the halfway point for achieving SDGs by 2030. SDG 7 is to ensure access to affordable, reliable, sustainable and modern energy. The goal includes reaching universal access to electricity and clean cooking, doubling historic levels of efficiency improvements, and substantially increasing the share of renewables in the global energy mix. Attaining this goal will have a deep impact on people’s health and well-being, helping to protect them from environmental and social risks such as air pollution, and expanding access to primary health care and services. The 2023 edition of Tracking SDG 7: The Energy Progress Report warns that current efforts are not enough to achieve the SDG 7 on time. There has been some progress on specific elements of the SDG 7 agenda – for example, the increased rate of using renewables in the power sector – but progress is insufficient to reach the targets set forth in the SDGs. The global energy crisis is expected to stimulate the deployment of renewables and improve energy efficiency with several government policies pointing to increasing investment. However, IRENA estimates show that international public financial flows in support of clean energy in low- and middle-income countries have been decreasing since before the COVID-19 pandemic and funding is limited to a small number of countries. To meet SDG 7 targets and to ensure that people fully benefit from the socio-economic gains of the shift to sustainable energy, it is necessary to structurally reform international public finance and define new opportunities to unlock investments. The report also finds that mounting debt and rising energy prices are worsening the outlook for reaching universal access to clean cooking and electricity. Current projections estimate that 1.9 billion people will be without clean cooking and 660 million without electricity access in 2030 if we do not take further action and continue with current efforts. These gaps will negatively impact the health of our most vulnerable populations and accelerate climate change. According to WHO, 3.2 million people die each year from illness caused by the use of polluting fuels and technologies, which increase exposure to toxic levels of household air pollution. Key findings of the report In 2010, 84% of the world’s population had access to electricity. This increased to 91% in 2021, meaning more than a billion people gained access over that period. However, the growth pace of access slowed in 2019–2021 compared to previous years. Rural electrification efforts contributed to this progress, but a large gap within urban areas remains. In 2021, 567 million people in sub-Saharan Africa did not have access to electricity, accounting for more than 80% of the global population without access. The access deficit in the region stayed almost the same as in 2010. The world remains off track to achieve universal access to clean cooking by 2030. Up to 2.3 billion people still use polluting fuels and technologies for cooking, largely in sub-Saharan Africa and Asia.
translations.reference: Samun makamashi na asali yana da alaƙa a tsakanin damar sabuntawa, sabon rahoton ya nuna An ci gaba da samun gibin samun wutar lantarki a duniya: Mutane miliyan 675 ba su da wutar lantarki, mutane biliyan 2.3 sun dogara da abubuwan girki masu ɗumama. Wani sabon rahoton da hukumar kula da ikon ƙasa da ƙasa (IEA), da hukumar sabunta ikon ƙasa da ƙasa (IRENA), da hukumar kididdiga ta majalisar dinkin duniya (UNSD) da bankin duniya, da hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO), suka fitar a yau, ya nuna cewa duniya bata kan hanyar cimma burin ci gaba mai dorewa (SDG) 7 don samun iko nan da 2030. A wannan shekara ta nuna rabin lokaci don cimma SDGs nan da 2030. SDG 7 shi ne tabbatar da samun dama Kai tsaye,, abin dogaro, da dorewa da iko na zamani. Makasudin ya hada da isar da damar samun wutar lantarki da dafa abinci mai tsafta, ninka matakan inganta ingantaccen aiki na tarihi, da kara yawan kason abubuwan da za a iya sabuntawa a cikin hadakar samun iko na duniya. Cimma wannan buri zai yi tasiri mai zurfi ga lafiyar mutane da walwala, yana taimakawa wajen kare su daga hatsarori na muhalli da zamantakewa kamar gurɓataccen iska, da faɗaɗa hanyoyin samun kulawa da kiwon lafiya na farko da ayyuka. Bibiyar gyaran 2023 SDG 7: bunƙasar samun iko ya bayyana rahoton kashedin cewa ƙoƙarin da ake yi yanzu bai isa ba don cimma SDG 7 akan lokaci. An sami ɗan ci gaba kan taƙamaiman abubuwa na ajandar SDG 7 - alal misali, ƙaruwar yawan amfani da abubuwan sabuntawa a fannin wutar lantarki - amma ci gaban bai isa ba don cimma manufofin da aka tsara a cikin SDGs. Ana sa ran rikicin samun makamashi na duniya zai haifar da tura abubuwan sabuntawa da ingantaccen iko tare da manufofin gwamnati da yawa da ke nuna karuwar saka hannun jari. Ko yaya, ƙididdigar IRENA ta nuna cewa kwararar kudaden jama'a na ƙasa da ƙasa don tallafawa na samar da cikakken makamash, a cikin ƙasashe masu karamin karfi da matsakaita, suna na raguwa tun kafin barkewar cutar ta COVID-19 kuma an taƙaita kudade ga kananan ƙasashe. Don cimma burin SDG ta 7 da kuma tabbatar da cewa mutane sun ci gajiyar ci gaban zamantakewa da tattalin arziƙin na canjin makamashi mai dorewa, ya zama dole a sake fasalin tsarin kuɗin jama'a na duniya da ayyana sabbin damammaki don buɗe hannun jari. Rahoton ya kuma nuna cewa karuwar basussuka da hauhawar farashin na kara tabarbare hasashe na ganin an kai ga samun tsaftataccen makamashin dafa abinci da wutar lantarki. Hasashen da aka yi a halin yanzu ya nuna cewa mutane biliyan 1.9 ba za su rasa girki mai tsafta ba sannan miliyan 660 ba za su samu wutar lantarki ba a shekarar 2030 idan ba mu dauki wani mataki ba kuma a ci gaba da kokarin da ake yi a yanzu. wadannan giɓinan za su yi mummunan tasiri ga lafiyar al'ummar mu masu rauni da kuma hanzarta canjin yanayi. A cewar WHO, mutane miliyan 3.2 ne ke mutuwa a kowace shekara sakamakon rashin lafiya da amfani da gurbataccen mai da fasahohin kirki ke haifarwa, wanda ke kara yawan kamuwa da gurɓacewar iska na gida. Mahimmin sakamakon rahoton A cikin 2010, kashi 84% na al'ummar duniya sun sami wutar lantarki a wancan lokacin. wannan ya karu zuwa 91% a cikin 2021, ma'ana fiye da mutane biliyan sun sami damar shiga cikin wannan lokacin. Ko yaya, saurin haɓakar damar shiga ya ragu a cikin 2019 - 2021 idan aka kwatanta da shekarun da suka gabata. YunƘurin samar da wutar lantarki a yankunan karkara ya taimaka wajen wannan ci gaba, amma akwai babban giɓi a cikin birane. A cikin shekarar 2021, mutane miliyan 567 a yankin Sahara ba su da wutar lantarki, wanda ya kai sama da kashi 80% na al'ummar duniya ba tare da samun wutar lantarki ba. Rashin isa ga yankin ya ƙasance kusan daidai da na shekarar 2010. Duniya ta ƙasance a kan hanya don samun damar dafa abinci mai tsafta nan da 2030. Kimanin mutane biliyan 2.3 har yanzu suna amfani da gurbataccen mai da fasahohin dafa abinci, galibi a yankin Sahara da Asiya.

**Rashin Yaduwar Makamin Juya da Kwarewar Sabuntawa, Rahoton Sabo Ya Bayyana**

Kewayon rashin yaduwar makamin juya a duniya na ci gaba: miliyan 675 ba su da wutar lantarki, miliyan 2.3 suna dogon amsar makamin zafin jiki mai cutarwa.

Wani rahoton da aka fitar a yau ne ta Kungiyar Makamin Juya ta Kasa (IEA), Kungiyar Makamin Juya Mai Sabuntawa ta Kasa (IRENA), Rarrabuwar Bayanin Alkaloli ta Majalisar Dinkin Duniya (UNSD), Bankin Duniya, da Kungiyar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO), ya nuna cewa duniya ba ta kan hanyar cimma burin Ci gaban Kula da Dorewa (SDG) na 7 game da makamin juya zuwa shekarar 2030.  Kwanan wannan shekara na nuni da rabi na hanyar cimma burin SDG zuwa shekarar 2030. SDG 7 na tabbatar da samun isasshen, amintacce, mai dorewa, da zamani na makamin juya. Burin ya haɗa da samun isasshen wutar lantarki da zafin jiki mai tsafta a duk inda muke, sau biyu da ci gaban da aka samu a wajen ingancin aiki, da ƙara yawan makamin mai sabuntawa a cikin juyawar makamin duniya.  Cimma wannan burin zai yi tasiri mai zurfi akan lafiya da karkataccen rayuwar mutane, yana taimakon kare su daga haɗɗace-haɗɗace na muhittin da na zamantawa, irin su cutar da iskar abubuwa, da haɓaka samun damar kula da lafiya da ayyukan da suka shafi lafiya.

Edidiyon 2023 na Tracking SDG 7: The Energy Progress Report ya bayyana cewa ƙoƙarin da ake yi yanzu ba su isa don cimma SDG 7 a kan lokaci ba.  An samu wasu ci gaba a kan wasu bangare na burin SDG 7 – alalokacin, ƙarin yawan amsar makamin mai sabuntawa a fannin wutar lantarki – amma ci gaba bai isa don cimma burin da aka kafa a cikin SDGs ba.

Ƙungiyoyin makamin juya a duniya ana sa ran za su haɓaka amsar makamin mai sabuntawa da inganta ingancin aiki ta hanyar tsarin manufofin gwamnati da yawa, waɗanda ke nuni da ƙarin saka hannun jari.  Koyawa, kaddamar da ƙima ta IRENA ta nuna cewa hanyoyin kudi na jama'a na duniya don tallafawa makamin juya mai tsafta a cikin ƙasashe da ke da matsakaici da matalala sun ragu tun kafin jarrabawar COVID-19, kuma kudin ya iyakace ga ƙananan kasashe ne kawai.

Don cimma burin SDG 7 da tabbatar da cewa mutane suna amfanar da kwarin gwiwar ci gaban zamantakewa da tattalin arziki ta hanyar canjin zuwa makamin mai dorewa, ya zama dole a gyara tsarin kudin da ke kasawa a duniya da ƙirƙirar sabbin dama don bunkasar saka hannun jari.

Kwanan nan an kiyasta cewa miliyan 1.9 za su ba da wutar lantarki mai tsafta, kuma miliyan 660 za su ba da wutar lantarki a shekarar 2030 idan muka kasa ɗaukar mataki na gaba da ci gaba da ƙoƙarin da muke yi yanzu.  Waɗannan ƙididdiga za su yi mummunan tasiri akan lafiyar mutanenmu da ke cikin mawuyacin hali da saurin canjin yanayi.

A cewar WHO, mutane miliyan 3.2 suna mutuwa kowace shekara sakamakon cuta da aka samu ta amsar makamin zafin jiki mai cutarwa da fasahohin da ke haɓaka yawan gurbatar iskar abubuwa a gida.

**Babban abubuwan da aka gano a rahoton**

A shekarar 2010, kashi 84% na mutanen duniya sun sami damar amsar wutar lantarki.  Wannan ya ƙaru zuwa 91% a shekarar 2021, wanda ke nuni da cewa mutane sama da biliyan ɗaya sun sami damar amsar wutar lantarki a lokacin.  Koyawa, saurin samun damar amsar wutar lantarki ya ragu a shekarar 2019–2021 idan aka kwatanta da shekuwanni da suka gabata.  Ƙoƙarin samar da wutar lantarki a ƙauye ya taimaka wajen cimma wannan ci gaba, amma har yanzu akwai babban ɓangar ba a cikin biranen.

A shekarar 2021, mutane miliyan 567 a Afirka ta Kudu da Sahal sun ba da wutar lantarki, wanda ya ƙunshi sama da kashi 80% na mutanen duniya da ba su da wutar lantarki.  Ƙididdigar da ba da wutar lantarki a yanzu ta kasance kusan ta maida ma ta shekarar 2010.

Duniya har yanzu ba ta kan hanyar cimma isasshen wutar lantarki mai tsafta zuwa shekarar 2030.  Mutane har zuwa biliyan 2.3 suna amsar makamin zafin jiki mai cutarwa da fasahohin da ke haɓaka gurbatar iskar abubuwa don zafin jiki, galibi a Afirka ta Kudu da Sahal da Asiya.

translations.source: The use of traditional biomass also means households spend up to 40 hours a week gathering firewood and cooking, which prohibits women from pursuing employment or participating in local decision-making bodies and children from going to school. According to the 2019 WHO estimates, 3.2 million premature deaths each year were attributable to household air pollution created by using polluting fuels and technologies for cooking. Renewable electricity use in global consumption has grown from 26.3% in 2019 to 28.2% in 2020, the largest single-year increase since the start of tracking progress for the SDGs. Efforts to increase renewables’ share in heating and transport, which represent more than three quarters of global energy consumption, remain off target to achieve 1.5oC climate objectives. Energy intensity – the measure of how much energy the global economy uses per dollar of GDP – improved from 2010–2020 by 1.8% annually. This is higher than the 1.2% improvement from the previous decades. However, the rate of energy intensity improvement has slowed in recent years and dropped to 0.6% in 2020. This makes it the worst year for energy intensity improvement since the global financial crisis, albeit largely due to pandemic-related restrictions, which may indicate only a temporary setback. Annual improvements through 2030 must now average 3.4% to meet the SDG target 7.3. International public financial flows in support of clean energy in developing countries stand at US$ 10.8 billion in 2021, 35% less than the 2010–2019 average and only about 40% of the 2017 peak of US$ 26.4 billion. In 2021, 19 countries received 80% of the commitments.The report will be presented to top decision-makers at a special launch event on 11 July 2023 at the High-Level Political Forum (HLPF) on Sustainable Development, ahead of the second SDG Summit in September 2023 in New York. The authors urge the international community and policymakers to safeguard the gains made toward achieving SDG 7, to advance structural reforms, and to maintain a strategic focus on the vulnerable countries needing the most support. Quotes: “The energy crisis sparked by Russia’s invasion of Ukraine continues to have a profound impact on people all around the world. High energy prices have hit the most vulnerable hard, particularly those in developing economies. While the clean energy transition is moving faster than many think, there is still a great deal of work needed to deliver sustainable, secure and affordable access to modern energy services for the billions of people who live without it. Successful energy transitions rely on effective policies and technological innovation combined with large-scale mobilisation of investment capital. The international community must leverage all these tools to meet the Sustainable Development Goals by the end of this decade.” Fatih Birol, Executive Director, International Energy Agency “Cost-competitive renewable energy has yet again demonstrated remarkable resilience, but the poorest in the world are still largely unable to fully benefit from it. To realise SDG7 without compromising climate goals, we must bring about systemic change in the way international cooperation works. It is crucial that multilateral financial institutions direct financial flows more equitably around the world to support renewables deployment and related physical infrastructure development.” Francesco La Camera, Director-General, International Renewable Energy Agency “Despite advances towards sustainable energy targets at the mid-point of Agenda 2030, Goal 7 seems harder to reach than it was in 2015 and scaled-up action is necessary if we are to ensure access to affordable, reliable, sustainable and modern energy for all by 2030. Access to electricity and clean cooking still display great regional disparities and should be the focus of action to ensure that no one is left behind. Investment needs to reach the least-developed countries and sub-Saharan Africa to ensure more equitable progress toward Goal 7.” Stefan Schweinfest, United Nations Statistics Division “Despite a recent slowdown in the global pace of electrification, the number of people without electricity almost halved over the past decade, from 1.1 billion in 2010 down to 675 million in 2021. Nonetheless, additional efforts and measures must urgently be put in place to ensure that the poorest and hardest-to-reach people are not left behind. To reach universal access by 2030, the development community must scale up clean energy investments and policy support.” Guangzhe Chen, Vice President for Infrastructure, World Bank

Bisa kididdigar da WHO ta yi a shekarar 2019, mutane miliyan 3.2 ne ke mutuwa da wuri a kowace shekara sakamakon gurɓacewar iska da aka haifar ta hanyar amfani da gurbataccen mai da fasahohin dafa abinci. Wannan ya sanya ta zama mafi munin shekara don haɓaka ƙarfin makamashi tun bayan rikicin kuɗin duniya, kodayake galibi saboda ƙuntatawa masu alaƙa da cutar, wanda na iya nuna koma baya na ɗan lokaci kawai. Ci gaban shekara-shekara ta 2030 dole ne yanzu matsakaita 3.4% don cimma burin SDG ta 7.3. A cikin 2021, ƙasashe 19 sun sami kashi 80% na alkawuran. Za a gabatar da rahoton ga manyan masu yanke shawara a wani taron ƙaddamarwa na musamman a ranar 11 ga Yuli 2023 a Babban Taron Siyasa (HLPF) kan Ci Gaba mai ɗorewa, gabanin SDG na biyu Taron koli a watan Satumba na 2023 a New York. Marubutan sun tursasa al'ummomin ƙasa da ƙasa da masu tsara manufofi da su kiyaye nasarorin da aka samu wajen cimma burin SDG ta 7, don samar da sauye-sauyen tsarin, da kuma mai da hankali kan dabarun yaƙi da ƙasashe masu rauni da ke buƙatar tallafi mafi girma. Sharhi: Rikicin makamashi da mamayar Rasha ta yi wa Ukraine na ci gaba da yin tasiri sosai ga mutane a duk duniya. Haɓaka farashin makamashi ya yi fama da mafi rauni, musamman waɗanda ke cikin ƙasashe masu tasowar tattalin arziki. Yayin da tsaftataccen makamashi ke tafiya da sauri fiye da yadda mutane da yawa ke tunani, har yanzu akwai babban aiki da ake buƙata don isar da ɗorewa, mai aminci da kuma mai araha ga sabis na makamashi na zamani ga biliyoyin mutanen da ke rayuwa ba tare da shi ba. Nasarar canjin makamashi ta dogara ne akan ingantattun manufofi da sabbin fasahohi haɗe tare da tattara manyan jari. Dole ne ƙasashen duniya su yi amfani da dukkan wadannan kayayyakin aikin don cimma burin ci gaba mai dorewa nan da karshen wannan shekaru goma. Fatih Birol, Babban Daraktan Hukumar Makamashi ta Duniya. Sabuwar makamashi mai tsada ya sake nuna juriya na ban mamaki, amma mafi talauci a duniya har yanzu ba su iya amfana daga gare ta. Don gano SDG ta 7 ba tare da ɓata manufofin sauyin yanayi ba, dole ne mu kawo sauyi na tsarin yadda haɗin gwiwar duniya ke aiki. Yana da mahimmanci cewa cibiyoyin hada-hadar kuɗi da yawa suna jagorantar tafiyar da harkokin kuɗi cikin adalci a duk faɗin duniya don tallafawa tura abubuwan sabuntawa da haɓaka abubuwan more rayuwa na zahiri. Francesco La Kamara, Darakta-Janar, Hukumar Sabunta Makamashi ta Duniya. Duk da ci gaban da aka samu kan maƙasudan makamashi masu ɗorewa a tsakiyar ajandar 2030, Manufar ta 7 tana da wahala a cimma fiye da yadda yake a cikin 2015 kuma matakan haɓaka ya zama dole idan muna son tabbatar da samun damar samun araha, abin dogaro, ɗorewa da makamashi na zamani ga kowa da kowa. zuwa 2030. Samun wutar lantarki da dafa abinci mai tsafta har yanzu suna nuna babban bambance-bambancen yanki kuma yakamata a mai da hankali kan aikin don tabbatar da cewa ba a bar kowa a baya ba. Saka hannun jari na buƙatar ya isa ƙasashe masu ƙaramin ƙarfi da kuma hamadar sahara domin tabbatar da samun ci gaba cikin adalci wajen cimma burin na 7. Stefan Schweinfest, Sashen ƙididdiga na Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya. Duk da koma bayan da aka samu a harkar samar da wutar lantarki a duniya, adadin mutanen da ba su da wutar lantarki kusan ya ragu a cikin shekaru goma da suka gabata, daga biliyan 1.1 a shekarar 2010 zuwa miliyan 675 a shekarar 2021. Duk da haka, dole ne a ƙara yin ƙoƙari da matakai na gaggawa don tabbatar da cewa ba a bar masu fama da talauci da wahala a baya ba. Don samun isa ga duniya nan da shekarar 2030, dole ne al'ummar ci gaba su haɓaka hannun jarin makamashi mai tsafta da tallafin manufofi. Guangzhe Chen, mataimakin shugaban ƙasa kan samar da ababen more rayuwa, bankin duniya

translations.source: “We must protect the next generation by acting now. Investing in clean and renewable solutions to support universal energy access is how we can make real change. Clean cooking technologies in homes and reliable electricity in health-care facilities can play a crucial role in protecting the health of our most vulnerable populations.” Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General, World Health Organization About the report This report is published by the SDG 7 custodian agencies, the International Energy Agency (IEA), the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), the United Nations Statistics Division (UNSD), the World Bank, and the World Health Organization (WHO) and aims to provide the international community with a global dashboard to register progress on energy access, energy efficiency, renewable energy and international cooperation to advance SDG 7. This is the ninth edition of this report, formerly known as the Global Tracking Framework (GTF). This year’s edition was chaired by the World Health Organization (WHO). The report can be downloaded here Funding for the report was provided by the World Bank’s Energy Sector Management Assistance Program (ESMAP).
translations.reference: Dole ne mu kare al’umma masu ya sowa ta haryar yin aiki a yanzu. Zuba hannun jari a cikin mafita mai tsabta da sabuntawa don tallafawa samun damar makamashi ta duniya shi ne yadda za mu iya samar da canji na gaske. Tsaftace fasahohin dafa abinci a gidaje da ingantaccen wutar lantarki a wuraren kiwon lafiya na iya taka muhimmiyar rawa wajen kare lafiyar al’ummarmu masu rauni. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Darakta Janar na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya WHO. Ga me da rahoton Hukumomin kulawa na SDG 7 ne suka wallafa wannan rahoto, Hukumar Kula da Makamashi ta Duniya ta (IEA) da Hukumar Kula da Makamashi ta Duniya (IRENA), Sashen Kididdiga na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya (UNSD), Bankin Duniya, da Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) kuma yana da nufin samarwa al'ummomin kasa da kasa wata dama ta duniya don yin rijistar cigaba da samun makamashi da ingancin makamashi da sabunta makamashi da hadin gwiwar ƙasa da ƙasa don ciyar da SDG 7 gaba. Wannan shi ne bugu na tara na wannan rahoto, wanda a da ake kira Global Tracking Framework (GTF). Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) ce ta jagoranci bugun na wannan shekaran. Ana iya sauke rahoton a nan. Shirin Taimakawa Sashen Kula da Makamashi na Bankin Duniya (ESMAP) ne ya bayar da tallafin rahoton.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of Hausa. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices I made.

**Hausa Translation:**

"Muna bukatar kare zukakkun generasi mai zuwa ta hanyar aiwatar da ayyuka yanzu. Kwarewa a hanyoyin tsarkake makochi da na sake kunna su don tallafawa samun damar amsar makochi a duk inda aka samu, shi ne hanyar da za mu iya yi ta hanyar canza al'amarin gaske.  Tafiyar da kayan aikin da ke amsar abinci mai tsarki a gida da samar da wutar lantarki mai amshi da kuma mai tabbaci a asibitai na iya taka rawar da ke da mahimmanci wajen kare lafiyar mutanenmu mafi rauni. Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Babban Daraktan WHO, Kungiyar Lafiya ta Duniya.

**Game da Rahoton**

Wannan rahoton an buga shi ne ta hukumomin kula da SDG 7, Hukumar Kasa da Kasa kan Makochi (IEA), Hukumar Kasa da Kasa kan Makochi Mai Sabuntawa (IRENA), Sashe na Ilimin Kimiyya na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya (UNSD), Bankin Duniya, da Kungiyar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) kuma yana da nufin samar da al'ummar duniya da allon duniya don duba ci gaban samun damar amsar makochi, karfafa amsar makochi, makochi mai sabuntawa, da kuma hadin gwanon kasa da kasa don ci gaba da SDG 7.  Wannan shi ne editan na tara na wannan rahoton, a wancan lokacin an fi sani shi da Global Tracking Framework (GTF).  Editan wannan shekarar an jagorata ne ta Kungiyar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO).  Za a iya saukewa da rahoton nan a [link zuwa wurin saukewa - *Please provide the link here*].

Kudin da aka kashe wajen fitar da rahoton an samu ta hanyar Kungiyar Bankin Duniya ta hanyar Programman taimakon gudanar da makochi (ESMAP)."

---

**Notes on Translation Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Next Generation"**: I translated this as "zukakkun generasi mai zuwa" (the hearts/souls of the coming generation). This is a common and respectful way to refer to future generations in Hausa.
*   **"Clean and Renewable Solutions"**:  "Hanyoyin tsarkake makochi da na sake kunna su" - This aims to capture both the "clean" and "renewable" aspects.
*   **"Universal Energy Access"**: "Samun damar amsar makochi a duk inda aka samu" -  This emphasizes the idea of access being available everywhere.
*   **"Clean Cooking Technologies"**: "Kayan aikin da ke amsar abinci mai tsarki" - Straightforward translation.
*   **"Reliable Electricity"**: "Wutar lantarki mai amshi da kuma mai tabbaci" -  Combined "reliable" and "consistent" to convey the meaning effectively.
*   **"Most Vulnerable Populations"**: "Mutanenmu mafi rauni" - A direct and clear translation.
*   **SDG 7 & Acronyms**: I've kept the SDG 7 designation.  For acronyms like IEA, IRENA, UNSD, and WHO, I've provided the full Hausa translation the first time they appear, followed by the acronym in parentheses.
*   **"Global Dashboard"**: "Allon duniya" -  This conveys the idea of a visual overview or monitoring tool.
*   **"Formerly Known As"**: "A wancan lokacin an fi sani shi da" - This is a natural way to express "formerly known as" in Hausa.
*   **Link Placeholder:** I've included a placeholder "[link zuwa wurin saukewa]" for the download link.  **Please replace this with the actual URL.**
*   **ESMAP**: I translated this as "Programman taimakon gudanar da makochi" (Program for assisting energy management).

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  This translation is based on the provided text.  If the report has a specific target audience or context, further adjustments might be needed.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Hausa speaker, especially someone familiar with technical terminology related to energy and development.  They can ensure accuracy, naturalness, and cultural appropriateness.
*   **Terminology Consistency:**  For future translations related to this topic, it's important to establish a consistent glossary of terms.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other questions or need further refinements.

translations.source: The European Commission and WHO launch landmark digital health initiative to strengthen global health security The World Health Organization (WHO) and European Commission have announced today the launch of a landmark digital health partnership. In June 2023, WHO will take up the European Union (EU) system of digital COVID-19 certification to establish a global system that will help facilitate global mobility and protect citizens across the world from on-going and future health threats, including pandemics. This is the first building block of the WHO Global Digital Health Certification Network (GDHCN) that will develop a wide range of digital products to deliver better health for all. “Building on the EU’s highly successful digital certification network, WHO aims to offer all WHO Member States access to an open-source digital health tool, which is based on the principles of equity, innovation, transparency and data protection and privacy,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “New digital health products in development aim to help people everywhere receive quality health services quickly and more effectively”. Based on the EU Global Health Strategy and WHO Global strategy on digital health, the initiative follows the 30 November 2022 agreement between Commissioner Kyriakides and Dr Tedros to enhance strategic cooperation on global health issues. This further bolsters a robust multilateral system with WHO at its core, powered by a strong EU.“This partnership is an important step for the digital action plan of the EU Global Health Strategy. By using European best practices we contribute to digital health standards and interoperability globally—to the benefit of those most in need. It is also a powerful example of how alignment between the EU and the WHO can deliver better health for all, in the EU and across the world. As the directing and coordinating authority on international health work, there is no better partner than the WHO to advance the work we started at the EU and further develop global digital health solutions,” said Stella Kyriakides, Commissioner for Health and Food Safety. This partnership will include close collaboration in the development, management and implementation of the WHO GDHCN system, benefitting from the European Commission’s ample technical expertise in the field. A first step is to ensure that the current EU digital certificates continue to function effectively.“ With 80 countries and territories connected to the EU Digital COVID-19 Certificate, the EU has set a global standard. The EU certificate has not only been an important tool in our fight against the pandemic, but has also facilitated international travel and tourism. I am pleased that the WHO will build on the privacy-preserving principles and cutting-edge technology of the EU certificate to create a global tool against future pandemics,” added Thierry Breton, Commissioner for Internal Market. A global WHO system building on EU legacy One of the key elements in the European Union’s work against the COVID-19 pandemic has been digital COVID-19 certificates. To facilitate free movement within its borders, the EU swiftly established interoperable COVID-19 certificates (entitled ‘EU Digital COVID-19 Certificate’ or ‘EU DCC’). Based on open-source technologies and standards it allowed also for the connection of non-EU countries that issue certificates according to EU DCC specifications, becoming the most widely used solution around the world. From the onset of the pandemic, WHO engaged with all WHO Regions to define overall guidelines for such certificates. To help strengthen global health preparedness in the face of growing health threats, WHO is establishing a global digital health certification network which builds upon the solid foundations of the EU DCC framework, principles and open technologies. With this collaboration, WHO will facilitate this process globally under its own structure with the aim to allow the world to benefit from convergence of digital certificates. This includes standard-setting and validation of digital signatures to prevent fraud. In doing so, WHO will not have access to any underlying personal data, which would continue to be the exclusive domain of governments.
translations.reference: Hukumar Tarayyar Turai da WHO sun kaddamar da wani muhimmin shiri na kiwon lafiya na dijital don karfafa tsaron lafiyar duniya. Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) da Hukumar Tarayyar Turai sun ba da sanarwar ƙaddamar da wani muhimmin haɗin gwiwa na kiwon lafiya na dijital. A watan Yuni 2023, WHO za ta ɗauki tsarin Tarayyar Turai (EU) na takardun shaida na dijital na COVID-19 don kafa tsarin duniya wanda zai taimaka wa sauƙaƙe zirga-zirgar duniya tare da kare 'yan ƙasa a duk faɗin duniya daga ci gaba da barazanar lafiya na gaba, game da annoba. Wannan shi ne tubalin farko na cibiyar sadarwa ta WHO Global Digital Health Certification Network (GDHCN) wacce za ta samar da nau'uka samfuran dijital da yawa don isar da ingantacciyar lafiya ga kowa. "Gina kan hanyar sadarwar satifiket ɗin dijital ta EU mai nasara sosai, WHO na da niyyar bai wa duk ƙasashe, membobin WHO damar samun buɗaɗɗen kayan aikin kiwon lafiya na dijital, wanda ya dogara da ƙa'idojin daidaito, ƙirƙira, bayyana gaskiya da kariyar bayanai da kuma keɓancewa. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Darakta Janar na WHO. Dangane da dabarun kiwon lafiya na duniya na EU da dabarun WHO na duniya kan lafiyar dijital, yunƙurin ya biyo bayan yarjejeniyar 30 ga Nuwamba 2022 tsakanin Kwamishinan Kyriakides da Dr Tedros don haɓaka dabarun haɗin Guiwa kan lamuran kiwon lafiya na duniya. Dangane da dabarun kiwon lafiya na duniya na EU da dabarun WHO na duniya kan lafiyar dijital, yunƙurin ya biyo bayan yarjejeniyar 30 ga Nuwamba 2022 tsakanin Kwamishinan Kyriakides da Dr Tedros don haɓaka dabarun haɗin gwiwa kan lamuran kiwon lafiya na duniya. Wannan yana ƙara ƙarfafa tsare-tsaren ƙungiyoyi da yawa tare da WHO a ainihinsa, wanda ƙaƙƙarfan EU ke ƙarfafa shi. Wannan haɗin gwiwar wani muhimmin mataki ne ga shirin aiwatar da dijital na dabarun lafiya na duniya na EU. Ta amfani da mafi kyawun ayyuka na Turai muna ba da gudummawa ga ƙa'idodin kiwon lafiya na dijital da haɗin kai a duniya ''don fa'idar waɗanda suka fi buƙata. Hakanan misali ne mai ƙarfi na yadda daidaitawa tsakanin EU da WHO na iya isar da ingantacciyar lafiya ga kowa, a cikin EU da duk duniya. A matsayin hukumar gudanarwa da daidaitawa kan ayyukan kiwon lafiya na kasa da kasa, babu wani abokin tarayya mafi kyau fiye da WHO don ci gaba da aikin da muka fara a EU da kuma ci gaba da samar da hanyoyin magance lafiyar dijital ta duniya, "in ji Stella Kyriakides, Kwamishinan Lafiya da Tsaron Abinci. Wannan haɗin gwiwar zai haɗa da haɗin gwiwa ta kut-da-kut a cikin haɓakawa, gudanarwa da aiwatar da tsarin GDHCN na WHO, tare da cin gajiyar ƙwararrun Hukumar Turai a fagen. Mataki na farko shi ne tabbatar da cewa takaddun shaida na dijital na EU na yanzu sun ci gaba da aiki yadda ya kamata." Tare da ƙasashe da yankuna 80 da ke da alaƙa da EU Digital CVID-19 Certificate, EU ta saita daidaitattun duniya. Takardun shaida na EU ba kawai ya zama muhimmin kayan aiki a yaƙinmu da cutar ba, har ma ya sauƙaƙe balaguron balaguron ƙasa da yawon buɗe ido. Na yi farin ciki da cewa WHO za ta gina ƙa'idodin kiyaye sirri da fasaha mai mahimmanci na takardar shaidar EU don ƙirƙirar kayan aiki na duniya game da annoba ta gaba, in ji Thierry Breton, Kwamishinan Kasuwar Cikin Gida. Tsarin WHO na duniya yana ginuwa a kan gadon tsarin na EU Ɗaya daga cikin mahimman abubuwa a cikin aikin Tarayyar Turai game da cutar ta COVID-19 shi ne takardun shaida na COVID-19 na zamani. Don sauƙaƙe motsi kyauta a cikin iyakokinta, EU ta hanzarta kafa takardun shaida na COVID-19 masu aiki (mai suna 'EU Digital COVID-19 Certificate' ko 'EU DCC'). Dangane da fasahohin bude-bude da ka'idoji ya ba da izinin haɗin kai na ƙasashen da ba na EU ba waɗanda ke ba da takaddun shaida daidai da ƙayyadaddun EU DCC, zama mafita mafi amfani da ita a duniya. Tun daga farkon barkewar cutar, WHO ta yi hulɗa tare da duk yankuna na WHO don ayyana gama - garin ƙa'idodin a irin waɗannan takardun. Don taimakawa ƙarfafa shirye-shiryen kiwon lafiya na duniya ta fuskar ci gaban barazanar kiwon lafiya, WHO tana kafa cibiyar sadarwar ba da takardar shaida ta dijital ta duniya wacce ke gina tushen tushen tsarin EU DCC, ka'idoji da fasahohi masu buɗewa. Tare da wannan haɗin gwiwar, WHO za ta sauƙaƙe wannan tsari a duniya a ƙarƙashin tsarinta tare da manufar ba da damar duniya ta ci gajiyar haɗuwa da takaddun shaida na dijital. Wannan ya haɗa da daidaitaccen saiti da tabbatar da sa hannun na zamani don hana zamba. Ta yin hakan, WHO ba za ta sami damar yin amfani da kowane bayanan sirri ba, wanda zai ci gaba da zama keɓaɓɓen yanki na gwamnatoci.

Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided document. I've aimed for accuracy and clarity, keeping in mind the target audience and the importance of conveying the technical details correctly.  I've also included some notes after the translation regarding potential nuances and areas where further refinement might be needed depending on the specific context.

**Translation (Hausa):**

Kungiyar Kwalliyar Turai da WHO sun ƙaddamar da wani sabuwar dabarar dijital don samar da tsaro na lafiya a duniya.

Kungiyar Kwalliyar Turai (European Commission) da Kungiyar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) sun bayyana a yau, ƙaddamar da sabuwar alƙawarin dijital na lafiya. A watan Yuni na shekarar 2023, WHO za ta karɓi tsarin Dijital na COVID-19 na Tarayyar Turai (EU) don kafa wata tsarin duniya wacce za ta taimaka wajan samar da hanyoyin tafiya a duniya da kuma kare mazauna duniya daga barazban lafiya da ke ci gaba da kuma barazanun gaba, gami da cutar lafiya ta duniya (pandemics). Wannan shi ne shida na farko a gaba ɗaya na Ganganarwar Dijital ta Lafiya ta Duniya ta WHO (GDHCN) wacce za ta haɓaka samfuran dijital da yawa don samar da lafiya mafi kyau ga kowa.

“Bisa ga hanyoyin da kungiyar Tarayyar Turai ta yi amfani da su a samar da takardun dijital, WHO tana da nufin ba da dama ga duk kasashen mambobin WHO don samun damar amfani da kayan aikin dijital na buɗewa, wanda ke daidaita da ka'idoji na daidaito, ƙwarai, bayyanaci da kuma kiyaye bayanai da sirri,” in ji Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Babban Daraktan WHO. “Sabbin kayan aikin dijital da ke aiki a halin yanzu suna da nufin taimaka wa mutane a ko'ina cikin duniya don samun ayyukan lafiya na ingila cikin sauri da kuma ƙara tasiri.”

Bisa ga Tsarin Lafiya ta Duniya na Tarayyar Turai da Tsarin WHO kan lafiyar dijital, wannan dabarar ta bi bayan yarjejeniyar da aka kulla a ranar 30 ga Nuwamba na shekarar 2022 a tsakanin Minista Kyriakides da Dr Tedros don ƙara haɓaka hadin gwiwa kan batun lafiya a duniya. Wannan ya ƙara ƙarfawa tsarin kasa da kasa mai ƙarfi da WHO a gaba, ta hanyar hadin gwiwar kungiyar Tarayyar Turai. “Wannan alƙawarin yana da mahimmanci a matsayin mataki na gaba a cikin shirin aiki na dijital na Tsarin Lafiya ta Duniya na Tarayyar Turai. Ta amfani da ayyukan da suka fi kima ce a Turai, muna ba da gudummawa ga ƙa'idoji da kuma hanyoyin haɗin gwiya na dijital a duniya – don amfanin wadanda suka fi bukata. Hakanan, wannan misali ne na ƙarfi na yadda haɗin gwiya tsakanin Tarayyar Turai da WHO na iya samar da lafiya mafi kyau ga kowa, a Turai da kuma a duniya. A matsayin mai jagorancin da kuma daidaita aikin lafiya a duniya, babu abokin tarayya mafi kyau fiye da WHO don ci gaba da aikin da muka fara a Tarayyar Turai da kuma haɓaka sakamakanwar dijital a duniya,” in ji Stella Kyriakides, Ministan Lafiya da Tsaro na Abinci.

Wannan alƙawarin zai haɗa da hadin gwiwar kusan dukkanin aikin da za a yi, gudanar da da kuma aiwatar da tsarin GDHCN na WHO, ta amfani da ilimin fasaha mai yawa da ke a kungiyar Tarayyar Turai a wannan fanni. Mataki na farko shi ne tabbatar da cewa takardun dijital na EU na ci gaba da aiki yadda ya kamata.

“Tare da kasashe da yankuna 80 da ke da alƙawari da Takardun Dijital na COVID-19 na EU, Tarayyar Turai ta kafa alkawari a duniya. Takardun EU sun kasance kayan aiki na muhimmanci a cikin gwagwarmayarmu da cutar lafiya ta duniya, kuma sun ba da izinin tafiya da yawon bude kasashen duniya. Ina farin ciki da WHO za ta dogon zama a kan ka'idoji na kiyaye sirri da fasahar gaba da lokaci na takardun EU don ƙirƙirar kayan aiki na duniya don fafatawa da cutar lafiya ta duniya a gaba,” in ji Thierry Breton, Ministan Kasuwar Ciki.

**Tsarin WHO na duniya da ke da alƙawari da EU**

Ɗaya daga cikin abubuwan da suka fi kima a aikin Tarayyar Turai da ke adawa da cutar COVID-19 shi ne Takardun Dijital na COVID-19. Don samar da hanyoyin tafiya cikin iyaka, EU ta kafa takardun COVID-19 na daidaito (an yi wa suna ‘Takardun Dijital na COVID-19 na EU’ ko ‘EU DCC’). Ta amfani da fasahar buɗewa da ƙa'idoji, ta kuma ba da izinin haɗin kasashen da ba su jima cikin EU ba waɗanda ke fitar da takardun bisa ga ƙa'idodi na EU DCC, wacce ta zama mafi yawan amfani a duniya.

Duk da farkon cutar lafiya ta duniya, WHO ta tuntuɓi duk yankunan WHO don ƙirƙirar umurnin gabaɗaya don irin waɗannan takardun. Don taimaka wajan ƙarfawa da shirye-shiryen lafiya a duniya a fafatawa da barazanun lafiya da ke ƙara yawan gaske, WHO tana kafa ganganarwar dijital ta lafiya ta duniya wacce ta dogon zama a kan ƙa'idoji na ƙa'idoji na EU DCC, ka'idoji da fasahar buɗewa. Ta amfani da wannan hadin gwiwa, WHO za ta ba da dama ga wannan aikin a duniya a ƙarƙashin ƙungiyarta, da nufin ba duniya damar amfani da haɗin gwiya na takardun dijital. Wannan ya haɗa da ƙirƙirar ƙa'idoji da kuma tabbatar da sa hannu na dijital don hana cutar da cuta. A wannan fannoni, WHO ba za ta sami damar shiga kowane bayanai na mutane, waɗanda za su ci gaba da zama a cikin ikon gwamnati.
